Description: If a model contains a variant subsystem that is outputting a virtual bus and is configured to generate preprocessor conditionals, MATLAB may crash.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account. Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.  Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:  Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.    Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.  Windows Systems:  Open the .zip file. Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.     Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  *Change log:    May 03, 2016: attachment_1338316_10bsp2_win32_2016-01-04.zip: added.  May 03, 2016: attachment_1338316_10bsp2_win64_2016-01-04.zip: added.

Summary: Sometimes the Model Reference Signal Logging dialog does not display all logged signals in referenced models
Description: If a model hierarchy contains more than three levels of models, it is possible for a signal logged in one of the lowest level models to not show up on the Model Reference Signal Logging dialog.

Summary: Code generation for a referenced model results in empty terminate function
Description: Code generation for a referenced model with a Stateflow chart might result in a terminate function containing only comments. This terminate function is not optimized out even if the Code Generation > Interface > Terminate function required  check box is cleared and the function does not include executable code.

Summary: Running Simulink.findVars in cached search mode causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Simulink.findVars  provides an argument called SearchMethod , which accepts cached  as one of two possible values. The value cached  avoids unnecessary model compilation, thereby optimizing the search. However, if you use the value cached  while writing custom Model Advisor checks that take a model in and out of compiled mode, MATLAB can crash.
Workaround: If you see this bug while calling Simulink.findVars in code for custom Model Advisor checks, remove the name-value pair of SearchMethod and cached from the list of arguments for Simulink.findVars.Cached search method is a performance optimization, and therefore removing it will not impact the output of Simulink.findVars.

Summary: Model build using Microsoft Visual Studio 2010 to generate Visual C/C++ Solution File fails with compiler not found (Windows only)
Description: On a Windows system, building a model for a Visual C/C++ Solution File target might fail if Microsoft Visual Studio 2010 is installed on the system. The build process does not recognize Visual Studio 2010 as supported for MSVC project creation and incorrectly displays the following error message: No supported Visual C/C++ compiler found. Check your installation of Visual C/C++ Note that this bug affects only Visual C/C++ Solution File builds and not template makefile (TMF) based builds.

Summary: Models containing ForEach subsystems which, in turn, contain conditionally executed logic, can cause MATLAB to produce incorrect results or crash during linearization.
Description: For models containing ForEach subsystems, performing linearization can cause MATLAB to produce incorrect results or crash if these models contain certain modeling features. The following list describes these features: 1.A ForEach subsystem containing any of the following blocks: Switch Multiport switch An S-function with at least one input marked as conditionally executed A conditionally executed subsystem where the 'Propagate execution context across subsystem boundary' setting is set to ON  2. Any one of the above blocks are traceable (via a connection) to an input port of the ForEach subsystem to which the block belongs. 3. A ForEach subsystem, which meets conditions 1 & 2 above, is on the linearization path. This bug does not affect simulation results.
Workaround: To work around this issue, change the following settings whenever you perform linearization on such models:1. In the Optimizations pane of the model's configuration parameters dialog box, set the 'Conditional Input Branch Execution' setting to OFF. To do this programmatically,set_param(model,'ConditionallyExecuteInputs','off');2. Check to see if the models contain conditionally executed subsystems (such as Enabled subsystems or Triggered Subsystems). In thise case,i)Right click on the subsystem block. Select the 'Subsystem Parameters' option.ii)Set the 'Propagate execution context across subsystem boundary' setting to OFF.

Summary: Original model file incorrectly renamed with ".bak" when you try to cancel the "Saving Referenced Models" dialog
Description: When you save a model containing model references, you might see a dialog prompting you to also save the referenced models. If you click the "Cancel" button in the "Saving Referenced Models" dialog, Simulink incorrectly renames the file containing the model to end in ".bak".For example, if you save the model "mymodel", and then click "Cancel" in the "Saving Referenced Models" dialog, Simulink renames the file "mymodel.mdl" to "mymodel.bak".
Workaround: Rename the file back to its original name.  For example, rename "mymodel.bak" back to "mymodel.mdl".

Summary: Error detecting a common compiler for parallel model reference builds if only MSVC Express is installed
Description: The build process might fail to identify Microsoft Visual C++ (MSVC) Express as a suitable common compiler for parallel model reference builds if MSVC Express is the only supported compiler installed on a Windows host system. If this occurs, the build process displays the following message: A common compiler is needed in order to link the final executable together. However, a common compiler does not exist across all workers in the MATLAB Distributed Compute Server pool.

Summary: wavefun errors when loading user-defined type III, IV, or V wavelets
Description: Prior to R2011a, attempting to use a type III, IV, or V wavelet added to the toolbox as a MAT file with wavemngr  results in the error ??? Error using ==> fevalUndefined function or method 'FSN.mat' for input arguments of type 'double'.  where FSN  is the wavelet family short name.  Because wavefun  is called by a number of other functions, including cwt , you can experience this error anytime you attempt to use a type III, IV, or V wavelet you have added to the toolbox.

Summary: Menus added to the Mac screen menu bar with uimenu cannot be disabled
Description: If you set the uimenu  'Enable'  property to 'off' , all of the menu items become disabled (gray) but the menu itself remains selectable. This behavior is a side effect of moving MATLAB menus to the top of the screen in R2011a (see the Update on MATLAB for the Mac  and the uimenu  documentation ). Menus now appear in the screen menu bar and Apple's JDK does not allow menus in the screen menu bar to appear disabled.
Workaround: Consider reworking your menu hierarchy to avoid the need to disable a top-level menu.If this is not feasible, one alternative to disabling a uimenu is to hide it by setting its 'Visible' property to 'off'.A more drastic workaround is to disable the Mac screen menu bar in MATLAB. For more information, see the workaround in Bug Report 718285.

Summary: cfamounts produces incorrect cash flows for cases where Period is 1, Basis is a non-ISMA day count, and AdjustCashFlowsBasis is set to true
Description: For example, cfamounts  produces incorrect cash flows for the following: cfamounts(0.1, '28-Feb-2009', '28-Feb-2014', 'Period', 1,'Basis',0, 'AdjustCashFlowsBasis', true)   ans = 0 4.9589 4.9589 4.9863 4.9589 104.9589   These cash flows should be:  cfamounts(0.1, '28-Feb-2009', '28-Feb-2014', 'Period', 1,'Basis',0, 'AdjustCashFlowsBasis', true)   ans = 0 10.0000 10.0000 10.0274 9.9727 110.0000
Workaround: Attached is the fix for the problem.To workaround this issue, replace the version of cfamounts.m function in your current installation with the updated version attached.1. Exit MATLAB?.Make a backup copy of the following files.matlabroot\toolbox\finance\cfamounts.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing files by adding "old" to the file extension, cfamounts.m.OLD.2. Download the attached file and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.3. Restart MATLAB.4. Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: When submodel output is returned by value, the behavior of the code and simulation may not match
Description: The results of a model's simulation and generated code might be inconsistent, if the model contains a submodel under the following conditions: You use Function Prototype Control to configure the signature of the submodel step function On the Diagnostics > Data Validity  pane, the diagnostic Underspecified initialization detection  is set to Classic  You specify that one of the submodel outputs is returned by value The submodel output is conditionally computed.  Modeling patterns that indicate conditional computations include: A conditionally executed subsystem (enabled subsystem) connected to the root model Outport block A Stateflow chart, Model block, or S-function block that conditionally computes its output and is connected to the root model Outport block
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block at the input to the root model Outport block, which is both conditionally computed and configured as a return argument of the model step function. Select the Signal Conversion block parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When model output is returned by value, the value might be undefined
Description: If you use Function Prototype Control to configure the signature of the model step function, and you specify that one of the model outputs is returned by value, then the return value might be undefined if it is conditionally computed. Modeling patterns that indicate conditional computations include: A conditionally executed subsystem (enabled subsystem) connected to the root model Outport block A Stateflow chart, Model block, or S-Function block that conditionally computes its output and is connected to the root model Outport block
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block at the input to the root model Outport block, which is both conditionally computed and configured as a return argument of the model step function. Select the Signal Conversion block parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Possible incorrect unit conversion applied to variables in through equations
Description: If unit of variable passed to through  statement differs from the unit of the Balancing  variable passed to the same through  statement, then an incorrect unit conversion is applied.
Workaround: Change unit of variables passed to through statement to match those of the Balancing variable passed to the same through statement.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect reusable code generated for For Each subsystem whose input is driven by either a non-inlined S-function block or a block that inlines code using a TLC file
Description: The number of iterations of a For Each subsystem in the generated code is incorrectly initialized if the For Each subsystem block generates a reusable function and meets both  of the following conditions: The subsystem has an input port for which the For Each block dialog box has the Partition  parameter checked. This input port is driven by a non-inlined S-function block or a block that inlines code using a TLC file. Common examples of blocks with a TLC file for code generation are Model and S-function (C-Mex S-function, or a Level-1 or Level-2 MATLAB S-function) blocks.  Additionally, similar incorrect reusable code is generated for any For Each Subsystems that are nested within a For Each subsystem that satisfies the conditions above. To check whether this issue affects your model in the R2010a and R2010b releases, run the DetectForBugReport708786.p  script. See the Workaround  section below for details. ?
Workaround: Download the attached DetectForBugReport708786.p script to a folder and add the folder to the MATLAB path using the command addpath. Run the script on your model using the following syntax:ret = DetectForBugReport708786('modelname')For a model that is unaffected by this issue, the script returns an empty array. For an affected model, the script returns an array of structures with the following three fields:TopForEachSS: The For Each subsystem that meets the two conditions described in the Bug Description section above.TopForEachSSInput: The index of the input mentioned in the condition above.AffectedForEachSS: The full list of For Each subsystems including nested subsystems that are affected by the bug identified for the subsystem listed in TopForEachSSInput.Use one of the following two workarounds for the For Each subsystems in your affected model:For the For Each subsystems identified in TopForEachSS, place a Signal Conversion block in front of the input port marked in TopForEachSSInput. In the Block Parameters dialog box for the Signal Conversion block, set the Output parameter to Contiguous copy and check the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.For all For Each subsystems identified in AffectedForEachSS, set the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to Inline or Function.

Summary: Segmentation fault when generating code for a model using variant subsystems
Description: You might see a segmentation fault when you generate code for a model containing variant subsystems, if You are generating preprocessor conditionals for those variant subsystems Any of the variants contain a subsystem that generates a reusable function, and that subsystem is reused elsewhere in the model, outside of any variant subsystem.  ?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, for all subsystems inside variant subsystems, on the Code Generation tab of the Block Parameter dialog, set the value of Function packaging to Function or Inline, so that the code generator does not attempt to generate reusable functions for these subsystems.

Summary: In the presence of Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks, parallel model reference build fails in some cases
Description: In the presence of Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks, parallel model reference build might fail with this error: Stateflow Internal Error: Error calling rtw_optimization info. Please regenerate code to fix this problem.  This error happens when the model reference hierarchy is deeper than one level.

Summary: On target PC running multicore kernel, the target PC might stop responding when a model is driven by an external interrupt
Description: On a target PC, the xPC Target kernel might stop responding when both are true: You configure the target PC to use multicore kernel You trigger the model with an external interrupt
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following:When available, upgrade to xPC Target Version 5.0 (R2011a)Contact MathWorks Technical Support for an xPC Target Version 4.4 (R2010b) patch

Summary: Simulink might crash when triggered or function-call subsystems have Inport blocks that are not sequentially numbered
Description: Inport blocks inside a subsystem or model should be numbered sequentially starting at 1. However, when constructing the model, you might have Inport blocks that are not sequentially numbered. For example, this situation might occur if you have only two Inport blocks, but their Port Number  values are 1 and 4. This non-sequential numbering results in the Subsystem block having 4 external ports instead of 2. When you perform update diagram on this model, you will see an error about the missing Inport blocks for ports 2 and 3. In R2010bSP1, R2010b, or R2010a, if a triggered or function-call subsystem is created with non-sequential Inport blocks as described above, an update diagram action causes a crash instead of an error. This behavior is illustrated in the attached model mTrigLatch_BadInportNum.mdl . You can check a model to see whether it is affected by this bug using the detectInportNumSkip_707514.p  function included in the attachment.
Workaround: As a workaround, to prevent this issue, ensure that Inport blocks in a triggered or function-call subsystem are sequentially numbered starting from 1 before you perform update diagram.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Behavior of code generated for merged outputs of exported AUTOSAR runnables may not match behavior of Normal mode simulation
Description: If you apply a Merge  block to the outputs of function-call subsystems being exported as AUTOSAR runnables, then the behavior of generated code may not match the behavior of the Normal mode simulation. This mismatch in behavior: Applies to both the SIL simulation and real-time application Can occur if the nonvirtual source of at least one signal entering the Merge  block is inside a conditionally executed subsystem (for example, an enabled subsystem) that in turn lies inside one of the function-call subsystems being exported
Workaround: Identify which signals entering the Merge block have nonvirtual sources located inside conditionally executed subsystems. For each of these signals:Place a Signal Conversion block on the signal as it enters the outport of the function-call subsystem from which an AUTOSAR runnable is exported.Double-click the block. The Block Parameters dialog box opens.Select the check box Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.Click OK..  Note that this workaround may change the model's semantics.

Summary: hsv2rgb can return the incorrect answer if the input color has a hue value equal to 1.
Description: hsv2rgb  can return the incorrect answer if the input color has a hue value equal to 1. For example, the following code: hsv2rgb([1 1 1]) returns: [1 0 1] This is magenta, rather than the expected red: [1 0 0]

Summary: "Microsoft Word is exporting ..." message appears after synchronizing a Simulink model with DOORS.
Description: When trying to synchronize a Simulink model with a new surrogate module in IBM Retional DOORS, DOORS application refuses to close the newly created module and informs you that "Microsoft Word is exporting". This is unrelated to Microsoft Word running or not running at the time of synchronization. The problem was first noticed in DOORS 9.3 version. The problem does not occur in DOORS 9.2 and earlier versions. The problem starts to occur after a DOORS upgrade, not after a MATLAB upgrade. The "Version Originated In" field is therefore not applicable for this problem. Any MATLAB version before the shown "Version Fixed In" is affected. ?
Workaround: A patched file for R2009b is attached to this bug report. Because each MATLAB version expects a slightly different version of the DOORS communication add-on script, the following manual patching procedure is recommended:1) Locate the dmi.inc file in the <DOORS_INST_DIR>/lib/dxl/addins/dmi/ folder. Back up the file.2) Open the file in the editor and locate the following line of code:interfaceMakeMod_(path, moduleDescription, "", 1)3) Replace the above line with the following new code:localMakeMod_(path, moduleDescription, "", 1)4) Extract the attached toolbox.zip file and find dmi.inc.modified file under reqmgt subdirectory5) Copy the entire localMakeModel_() function from the dmi.inc.modified file and paste it into your dmi.inc file immediately ABOVE the following line:void dmiNewSurrogate_(string path, string modelName) {6) Restart DOORS. Synchronization should now work.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation for initialization of states that have fixed-point or enumerated data types.
Description: The generated code for initializing a block state might be incorrect if any of the following are true: The state data type is a fixed-point data type with a nonzero bias, and the initial value of the state is specified as the value of the bias.  The state data type is an enumerated data type whose default enumeration does not map to the integer value zero, and the initial value of the state is specified as the enumeration that maps to zero.  The state data type is a structure that has an element that falls into one of the previous two cases, or the structure has a substructure with such an element.  In the case of the fixed-point data type, the state might be initialized to the real world value zero. In the case of the enumerated data type, the state might be initialized to the default enumeration.

Summary: Simulink Verification and Validation tool may throw errors or warnings while generating reports
Description: When generating reports through Simulink Verification and Validation tool, errors or warnings similar to the following may appear and report creation may fail: In component rptgen.cml_eval : No appropriate method, property, or field isInsertString for class rptgen.cml_eval.
Workaround: ?Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The obsolete Lookup Table (2-D) block generates incorrect code for integer or fixed-point inputs when row and column index inputs have different kinds of evenly spaced data
Description: The obsolete Lookup Table (2-D) block generates incorrect code when the following conditions are met: (1) The inputs to the block are either integers or fixed-point data. (2) The parameters for Row and Column index input values are not tunable. (3) The parameter 'Row index input values' has power-of-two even spacing. (4) The parameter 'Column index input values' has even spacing, but not power-of-two even spacing.
Workaround: Because the Lookup Table (2-D) block is obsolete as of R2011a, you are recommended to use slupdate to replace it with the n-D Lookup Table block in the Simulink/Lookup Tables library.If you are using R2010a or later releases, you can use slupdate to upgrade the Lookup Table (2-D) block by following these steps:(1) Open your model that has the obsolete Lookup Table (2-D) blocks.(2) At the MATLAB command line, open slupdate by typing:  slupdate('modelname')(3) Slupdate prompts you to replace each obsolete Lookup Table (2-D) block.You can also fix the issue by manually modifying one of Simulink's source files. This fix works in any release. Please follow the steps below:(1) Enter the MATLAB root folder.(2) Then, go to the rtw/c/tlc/fixpt folder.(3) Open the file fixptlook2D.tlc using the MATLAB Editor.(4) In the file, search for the string:u0SearchMethod != searchEven(5) Go to the second occurrence of the string, and change 'u0SearchMethod' to 'u1SearchMethod'.(6) To verify this modification, download the attached Simulink model mLookupTable2D.mdl. The instruction for the verification is in the model.

Summary: Verification fails during the P_EVNT phase with an "exception match" error.
Description: The verification fails durng the P_EVNT phase with an exception match: **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 50 (P_DAL) **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 50 (P_DAL) took 5.1real, 4.8u + 0.2s (0.2gc) **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 51 (P_ENVT) *** c: src =&T7 missing={W3155v, W3203v} exception Match(Match) . Note that identifiers of the error message (W3155v, W3203v) can be different from verification to verification.

Summary: Single instance model reference code might not compile when containing a Stateflow exported graphical function
Description: Simulink Coder might generate uncompilable code for a Model Reference block if all of the following conditions are true: On the Configuration Parameters > Model Reference  pane, the drop-down list box Total number of instances  allowed per top model  is set to once . The referenced model contains a Stateflow chart with graphical functions or non-inlined states. In the Stateflow chart properties dialog box, the Export Chart Level Graphical Functions (Make Global)  check box is selected.
Workaround: In the Stateflow chart properties dialog box, clear the Export Chart Level Graphical Functions (Make Global) checkbox. Exporting chart level functions might not be intended within a model reference build.

Summary: Round off in function roundn
Description: In the expression roundn(x,n) , when the power of 10 specified by the second argument, n , is negative, the actual result may differ from the expected result by the amount eps(x) .
Workaround: If you are passing the output of roundn to num2str, then no workaround is needed. Otherwise, for n?<?0, you can replace an expression like y?=?roundn(x,n) with y?=?round(x?*?10^(-n))?/?(10^(-n)).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignment to contiguous subrange of matrix is optimized incorrectly
Description: This bug in Embedded MATLAB is triggered by the following code pattern:   x(:,2) = myfcn(a,b,c); Subsequent uses of the variable x might be incorrectly transformed. For example:   x = zeros(3,2);  x(:,2) = myfcn(a,b,c);  y = x(2,2); The third line of this example is incorrectly transformed to:   y = 0; This bug can only occur when: The variable on the left-hand side of the assignment (x in the example above) is a matrix with at most 10 elements. The assignment must assign a contiguous subrange of the matrix. For example, x(:,2) is contiguous because it corresponds to subscripts 4, 5, and 6 of x. However, x(2,:) is not contiguous because it corresponds to subscripts 2 and 5 of x. The right-hand side of the assignment must be a function call. This bug can also affect some built-in operators because they are implemented internally as function calls. For example: x(:,2) = b/c  is equivalent to x(:,2) = mrdivide(b,c)
Workaround: Overwrite the same subrange immediately before the function call as shown below. Assigning two different values to the subrange ensures that the incorrect transformation does not occur.  x(:,2) = 0;  x(:,2) = 1;  x(:,2) = myfcn(a,b,c);

Summary: Models with Stateflow or MATLAB Function blocks fail to simulate when mex compiler is not set up
Description: When "mex -setup" is not run at the command line, simulating a model with Stateflow or MATLAB Function blocks produces the following Simulink error: Unable to locate 'mexopts.bat', and therefore cannot determine which compiler to use for simulation builds.Use 'mex -setup' to select a supported compiler.
Workaround: Run "mex -setup" at the MATLAB command line to select a supported compiler.

Summary: Error when saving models with library links to Model Info blocks
Description: Errors can occur when you save a model that contains a library link to a Model Info block, if that library is not open or loaded.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, perform an "update diagram" operation on the model before saving. Update diagram opens the linked library. Alternatively, manually open the linked library before saving the model.

Summary: For HTML reports, the Simulink Snapshot component does not generate links for subsystems whose names contain new line characters
Description: For HTML reports, the Simulink Snapshot component does not generate links for subsystems whose names contain new line characters.
Workaround: Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached gr_getFileName.p file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of gr_getFileName.p.Delete the gr_getFileName.p file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to custom-code functions from Stateflow transitions are sometimes incorrectly optimized away
Description: One of the code generation optimizations tries to identify identical code in both branches of an if-else construct and replace them with a single, unconditional copy of the branch code. For example, the optimization converts the pattern labeled "BEFORE" to the pattern labeled "AFTER": BEFORE: if(c==1) {   x = x+1;   y = sin(1);}else{   x = x+1;   y = sin(1);} AFTER:    x = x+1;   y = sin(1); This optimization sometimes identifies calls to different custom-code functions as being the same and wrongly optimizes them. Specifically, under the following conditions, calls to custom-code functions from Stateflow transitions get incorrectly optimized away: 1. The diagram has a flow-graph construct that results in the following pattern: if(condition) {   foo();}else{   bar();} where foo() and bar() are user functions defined in custom C-code. Note that the calling signatures for foo() and bar() must be identical if they have any input arguments. For example, if foo() and bar() have an input argument, then the calls should be passing in identical inputs as in {foo(2), bar(2)} or {foo(a+b), bar(a+b)}. 2. The option for Simulink block / Stateflow object comments  is turned off in the Configuration Parameters dialog. Existence of traceability comments or non-identical user-comments causes the pattern match to fail and the bug does not occur. Under these two conditions, the optimization incorrectly infers that foo() and bar() are identical and replaces the above construct with just: foo();
Workaround: In the Comments pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog, select Simulink block / Stateflow object comments. This option creates distinct comments in the if-else branches that stop this optimization from doing this incorrect pattern match.

Summary: Incorrect output signal type when upgrading a 'Sqrt' block from an earlier release
Description: When you use slupdate to upgrade a 'Sqrt' block created in R2009b or earlier, the replacement 'Signed Sqrt' block does not have the correct output signal type. slupdate incorrectly changes the output signal type to 'auto'.
Workaround: To correct the problem, manually set the output signal type in the 'Signed Sqrt' blocks .Open the model.Select View > Model Explorer.Use the Search bar to search by block type for blocks of type 'Sqrt'.For each 'Signed Sqrt' block found, set the appropriate output signal type.

Summary: Building model that contains PIL block for TI DSP/BIOS generates error
Description: When the following conditions are true, building a model generates a MATLAB command line error: In the Configuration Parameters, under Real-Time Workshop > SIL and PIL Verification, Create block  is set to PIL  In the Target Preferences block for your model, on the Board Pane, Operating System  is set to DSP/BIOS   The MATLAB command line error states: "Error: Errors occurred - aborting" ### Writing source file MW_c6xxx_csl.c ### TLC code generation complete. ??? Subsystem build failed
Workaround: To avoid generating the error during a PIL simulation:Locate the Target Preferences block in the PIL subsystem.Open the Target Preferences block by double-clicking the block.On the Board pane, set the Operating System parameter to None.Start a normal PIL simulation.To generate production code when you are done using PIL:Complete the PIL simulation.Delete the PIL blocks from the system.From the menu bar in the model, select Simulation > Configuration Parameters.In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select the Real-Time Workshop > Embedded IDE Link pane.Set Build action to something other than Create_Processor_In_the_Loop_project.Open the Target Preferences block and change the Operating System parameter to DSP/BIOS.Generate code by entering CTRL+B.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB crashes when two variable-sized horizontal vectors are multiplied.
Description: If X and Y are both variable-sized horizontal vectors then the following MATLAB code causes Embedded MATLAB to crash:    X*Y For example, the following code triggers the bug:    zeros(1,n)*zeros(1,n);
Workaround: Replace X*Y with either X.*Y or X*Y', depending on whether the desired answer is an array multiply or a dot product.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier sporadically throws an exception during the compatibility check.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier sporadically throws an exception during compatibility checking of a model that contains state variables that are read multiple times, for example, Data Store Memory blocks.

Summary: Code generated for reusable subsystems may fail to compile
Description: Embedded Coder may generate code that fails to compile due to a type mismatch if all of the following are true: A block (say, A) lies inside a parent subsystem (say, B) that generates a reusable function, and block A is connected to an inport within block B Block B also lies within another parent subsystem (say, C) that generates a reusable function, and block B is connected to an inport within block C Block C is connected to a root model inport  The signal leaving the root inport is non-scalar  ?
Workaround: To workaround the issue:Place a Signal Conversion block at the root inport that passes the nonscalar signal to the nested reusable functions.From the block parameter dialog box, select the check box Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Model containing global data store memory and Embedded MATLAB Function blocks may crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB may crash if you update a model that meets all the following conditions: The model contains at least two Embedded MATLAB Function blocks Two of the Embedded MATLAB Function blocks access the same global data store memory You do not explicitly specify the attributes of the signal object used to define this global data store; in other words, the signal object inherits attributes such as Data type or Dimensions.
Workaround: Before compiling a model, ensure that you have specified all attributes of the Simulink.Signal object that represents the global data store memory. No attribute should have values such as Inherit or -1.In addition, consider building a simple model first, such as one in which a Data Store Read block accesses the global data store memory. Then verify the validity of the Simulink.Signal object associated with the global data store memory before using the signal object in the model that causes the crash.

Summary: Applying eml.cstructname to a structure that is both input and output of the same function causes compilation error
Description: To ensure call by reference, you can use the same variable as both an input and an output. However, when you apply eml.cstructname  to such a variable, you get a compilation error when the call site references the variable only as an output. For example, consider the following MATLAB functions: function s = foo %#emls = init(); s = init(s);  function s = init(s) s.x = 0; s.y = 0; eml.cstructname(s, 'mystruct');  At the first call site, s = init() , the variable s  is referenced as an output only. At the second call site, s = init(s) , s  is already named. However, if you compile the functions with emlmex  or emlc , you get the error message "The C struct name cannot be set on function inputs..." Even though the structure s  is not named as a function input, eml.cstructname  fails to recognize that the variable s  can sometimes be an output only.
Workaround: Rename the input variable and copy the renamed variable to the original in a guarded if statement. For example, rename function init as follows:function s = init(sin) if nargin > 0 s = sin;ends.x = 0; s.y = 0; eml.cstructname(s, 'mystruct');Despite assigning different variable names for input and output, the compiler ensures call by reference because it detects that sin and s can reuse the same memory.

Summary: Explore modes inoperative in version 7.11 (R2010b) on rotated figures saved by an earlier release
Description: If you saved a figure with an axes in version 7.10 (R2010a) or earlier and open it in version 7.11 (R2010b), you might not be able to pan, zoom, or rotate the axes. This issue arises only if, prior to saving the figure, you had manipulated the axes in rotate3d mode. MATLAB version 7.11 (R2010b) incorrectly interprets the state that using rotate3d mode induced in the figure, preventing any explore mode (zoom, pan, and rotate3d) from functioning on the restored figure.
Workaround: After loading the saved FIG-file, delete a hidden axes from the figure. That axes is not needed and prevents the rotate, zoom, and pan modes from responding to events. Use this code: delete(findall(gcf,'Tag','MATLAB_Rotate3D_Axes'))?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For charts with local events, exit actions sometimes get removed from generated code
Description: Suppose that a Stateflow chart satisfies all of the following conditions: It has local events, either explicit or implicit. It has a parent state with two or more substates. Some (but not all) child substates have exit actions.  Under certain circumstances, some of the exit actions can disappear from the generated code. The attached model mg702315.mdl demonstrates the problem. In this model, when the outer state "A" is exited, the exit action of "A2" needs to execute. However, when debugging is turned off, or when code is generated for the model, the exit action is not executed. The attached function findProblemStates  finds any states that are affected by this issue. Please see its help for how to use it. Running this function as: >> findProblemStates('mg702315'); after opening the attached model will print out the following message at the MATLAB prompt: Warning: State A has some child states whose exit actions might disappear! with the state name (in this case "A") hyperlinked to open the state.
Workaround: This bug can be avoided by executing the following command once per sesson before generating code:sf('feature','Optimized Exit Fcns',0);You can add this command to your startup.m file so that it executes in every session. You can also add this command to the file toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/private/sfinit.m so that it executes every time you load a chart. Doing so helps you avoid this bug in the generated code.The only other effect of this command is to turn off a minor readability feature that minimizes switch statements in state exit functions. No observable effect on the code efficiency exists when you turn off this optimization.

Summary: Code generation might fail when source multiplies variable-size matrices
Description: MATLAB Coder might produce an error during code generation when the source performs an inner product operation with two variable-size matrices.

Summary: SegV occurs when a model is opened, closed without compiling, and then reopened.
Description: A SegV may occur when a model containing Simulink.Signal  objects with InitialValue  set is opened, then closed without compilation, and then reopened.

Summary: Model Explorer search can cause error if you search again or open the Contents pane
Description: After you perform a search using the Model Explorer and then you either select the Contents  tab or you perform another search, you may see an error that starts with text similar to the following: ??? No appropriate method, property, or field Name for class DAStudio.MEViewProperty. Error in ==> DAStudio.MEViewManager.enableLiveliness>clearTransientProperties at 259                 if strcmp(view.Properties(index).Name, view.GroupName) Error in ==> DAStudio.MEViewManager.enableLiveliness>doSearchPropertiesAdded at 215         clearTransientProperties(actView); Error in ==> DAStudio.MEViewManager.enableLiveliness>syncMEViewManagerFromME at 89     doSearchPropertiesAdded(eventData, manager); Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.
Workaround: To recover from this error:Near the top of the Model Explorer Contents pane, select Show Details (if the column view details section does not already appear).In the column view details section, click the Options button.Select Reset This View to Factory Settings.

Summary: The Modulo Integrator and Discrete Modulo Integrator blocks are missing from the product.
Description: The Modulo Integrator and Discrete Modulo Integrator blocks were made obsolete in release 3.0 (R14, Jun2 2004) of the Communications Blockset product. These blocks have been issuing obsolescence warnings since release 2006A (March 2006). Customers are expected to create alternate implementations, or copies of the blocks, for their existing models to continue working as before.
Workaround: Download the included files in the attached .zip file into a writable folder on path.

Summary: Hermitian operations with complex vector inputs not replaced with target function library (TFL) implementations during emlc code generation
Description: During emlc  code generation for an Embedded MATLAB function, TFL replacements do not occur as expected for a Hermitian transposition or Multiplication with Hermitian transposition operation if both of the following conditions are true: The Hermitian transposition or Multiplication with Hermitian transposition operation has complex vector inputs. You are using a custom TFL that registers replacements for the Hermitian transposition (RTW_OP_HERMITIAN) or Multiplication with Hermitian transposition (RTW_OP_HMMUL) operation.  Under these conditions, code is generated without the expected replacements. Executing the generated code produces correct numerical results, but without the potential benefit of the replacement implementations.

Summary: Incorrect explicit type cast to custom data type in generated code when Prelookup block uses Simulink.NumericType
Description: A custom data type is used in lookup table utility functions. Currently, Simulink.NumericType  or Simulink.AliasType  type names cannot be used in the shared utility code that is generated from lookup table blocks.

Summary: Reduced accuracy of Spherical Harmonic Gravity Model block
Description: The International Centre for Global Earth Model's (ICGEM) new Calculation Service sites enable the generation of additional test case reference data for the validation of the spherical harmonic gravity model block. This new capability has revealed an inaccuracy from the conversion of zero-based indexing implementation to one-based indexing implementation of the cos(m¦Ë)  and the sin(m¦Ë)  terms. The resulting accuracy is reduced to the second decimal place, or 1e-02 relative tolerance.
Workaround: As a workaround, to regain the accuracy of the ICGEM Calculation Service reference in the R2010a and the R2010b releases, perform the following:Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/aerolibgravity2.mdlTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: The isKey method of containers.Map errors if the Map is a public property of an object and isKey is called using dot syntax.
Description: If a containers.Map is contained in an object, like this: classdef HasMap    properties       aMap;    end   methods      function obj = HasMap          obj.aMap = containers.Map({'aaa'}, {1});       end   endend Then calling the isKey  method on the map using dot syntax and a key value of the wrong type causes MATLAB to crash. obj = HasMap;obj.aMap.isKey(0);
Workaround: Use functional syntax for this type of call to isKey: obj = HasMap; isKey(obj.aMap, 0);

Summary: Examples show a flawed usage of the demcmap function
Description: Over a half dozen Mapping Toolbox examples, and two demos, include a line like this: colormap(demcmap(Z)) , where Z  contains gridded terrain or bathymetry. Without close inspection, this usage may produce a reasonable looking map, but it will typically cause the cut-over between the colors for ocean bathymetry and the colors for land elevations to occur at a non-zero elevation. As a result, either some land areas will be colored in a shade of blue or some ocean areas will be colored in a shade of green.
Workaround: If you adapt any of these examples for your own use, simply remove the call to the colormap function. Replace instances of colormap(demcmap(Z)) with demcmap(Z) instead. The result will be better, because in addition to resetting the figure's Colormap property, demcmap will also update the CLim property of the current axes.

Summary: Model block SIL/PIL error when using Function Prototype Control and tunable parameters
Description: The following Model block SIL/PIL error occurs when the component under test uses Function Prototype Control to specify a step function return value (for an outport) and  has tunable parameters: Error using ==> CodeInfoUtils.CodeInfoUtils>CodeInfoUtils.getDataInterfacesForFunction at 525 CAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.

Summary: Sample time propagation issue in Simulink can adversely impact some Simscape models
Description: Sample time propagation issue in Simulink can adversely impact some Simscape models. This Simulink issue causes a port-based sample time block to have a continuous, [0,0], sample time on an input port. Instead, this block should have a fixed in minor step, [0,1], sample time. If the input signal to a Simscape S-PS Converter block has a fixed in minor step sample time (for example, Rate Transition block output), Simscape might fail to react to discontinuities in the input signal. For Simulink, a port-based sample time block (for example, a Simscape block) must have the following conditions for the issue to occur: Inherits a fixed in minor step sample time on an input port Inherits a continuous sample time on all output ports Has a subsystem boundary between the input port with the inherited fixed in minor step sample time and the block that propagates [0,1] to it Exists in a model for which inline parameters are disabled.  This issue originated in Simulink in R12.1, but did not impact Simscape until R2010b.

Summary: For the Rockwell Automation RSLogix target IDE, the Generate, Import and Verify Code for Subsystem operation fails
Description: For the Rockwell Automation RSLogix target IDE, the Generate, Import and Verify Code for Subsystem  operation fails. The MATLAB Command Window displays errors and the project cannot be imported or verified.

Summary: linprog reports a matrix dimension mismatch error on some infeasible problems.
Description: linprog  reports a matrix dimension mismatch error when:* The problem is infeasible.* There are variables with infinite lower bounds.* There are empty constraint columns and zero objective coefficients corresponding to these variables.* You ask linprog  for a lambda  (Lagrange multiplier) structure, or for an output  structure. linprog  correctly reports that the problem is infeasible, but then returns a matrix dimension mismatch error.
Workaround: Do not request output arguments output and lambda when calling linprog.

Summary: On Linux systems, fscanf ignores specified widths for some values
Description: When you specify an integer conversion format, fscanf  looks at the first few characters of the source text to determine how to convert the data. For example, %i  converts text to a hexadecimal value when the text has a leading 0x  sentinel.On Linux systems, if the source text contains characters that could be leading sentinels (such as zeros or signs), and the specified width is less than or equal to the number of leading characters, fscanf  ignores the specified width.

Summary: Documentation example files for Simulink LMS tutorial are not found in R2010b?
Description: The following Embedded MATLAB .m and .mdl files are referenced from the documentation, but they are not installed with R2010b: Tutorial: Integrating MATLAB Code with a Simulink Model for Filtering an Audio Signal (web([docroot '/toolbox/simulink/ug/bsoywg7.html'])) acoustic_environment.mdldesign_templates.mdlnoise_cancel_00.mdlnoise_cancel_01.mdlnoise_cancel_02.mdlnoise_cancel_03.mdlnoise_cancel_04.mdlnoise_cancel_05.mdlnoise_cancel_06.mdllms_01.mlms_02.mlms_03.mlms_04.mlms_05.mlms_06.m
Workaround: Download the attached zip archives and extract the .m, .mdl, and .mat files to a local writable folder.

Summary: IMAQHWINFO may cause a crash of MATLAB for GigE Vision cameras that store their GenICam XML without ZIP compression
Description: Many cameras store their GenICam XML file with ZIP compression. However, for those that do not store the XML file with ZIP compression, IMAQHWINFO will cause MATLAB to crash, for example: imaqhwinfo('gige')  would cause MATLAB to crash. ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: ERT S-function block generated from a virtual subsystem with automatic rate transitions might produce delayed output
Description: An ERT S-function block might produce delayed output in a model simulation if all of the following conditions exist: You generated the ERT S-function from a virtual subsystem using a right-click build. An output port of the virtual subsystem specifies a Sample time  value, and the specified value differs from one or more of the source block sample times. Automatic rate transition is selected for the model (that is, the option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is selected on the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box). In the model, you replace the virtual subsystem with the generated ERT S-function block.  Under these conditions, when you simulate the model, the output of the ERT S-function block might be delayed, compared to the output of the virtual subsystem it replaced.
Workaround: To work around this bug, avoid specifying a sample time at the output ports of the virtual subsystem, especially if the specified sample time differs from the source block sample times and might require automatic rate transition handling.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Incorrect simulation behavior for enabled and triggered subsystem driven by signals with different sample times
Description: A model containing an Enabled and Triggered Subsystem block might produce incorrect simulation results if both of the following conditions are true: The enable signal and the trigger signal driving the block have different sample times. The Solver option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is selected for the model. This option enables automatic insertion of hidden Rate Transition blocks to handle rate transition at the enable port.  Under these conditions, the results of executing the generated code are correct, but the results of simulation are not. The issue occurs because an incorrect SampleHit check is performed by enable signal checking code.
Workaround: To work around this bug, take one of the following actions:Avoid using different sample times for enable and trigger signals driving an enabled and triggered subsystem.If the first action is not possible, manually insert a Rate Transition block at the enable port of the Enabled and Triggered Subsystem block.

Summary: Improper handling of coordinate limits and tilename in function gtopo30
Description: Several problems occur with the function gtopo30  in regard to its handling of coordinate limits and tilename  inputs: In rare cases in which you specify a folder or CD-ROM (dirname ) containing multiple GTOPO30 tiles, and the latitude or longitude limits (latlim  or lonlim ) that you provide cover only a very small part of a given tile, then the gtopo30  function ends with an error issued by the readmtx  function. If you specify longitude limits (via the lonlim  input) that span the 180-degree meridian, then the number of columns in the resultant data grid, Z , may exceed the number of columns in the full GTOPO30 data set. Also, the longitude extent of the grid, as calculated from size(Z )  and the referencing vector output, refvec , may exceed 360 degrees. Unless they already coincide exactly with the edges of GTOPO30 cells, each user-supplied latitude and longitude limit (in inputs latlim  and lonlim ) are snapped inward to the nearest edge. This behavior is incorrect. The limits should snap outward as described in the gtopo30  function documentation: "The latitude and longitude limits are snapped outward to define the smallest possible rectangular grid of GTOPO30 cells that fully encloses the area defined by the input limits." When user-supplied latitude or longitude limits need to be snapped to grid cell edges, the referencing vector output, refvec , may suffer from a small but avoidable loss of precision. If as the first input, tilename , you mistakenly specify a file that exists and has a suffix .DEM  but is not an actual, valid GTOPO30 tile, then the function quietly returns a scalar NaN for the output grid Z,  rather than issuing an error.
Workaround: To work around this problem:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Download the attached archive file, workaround_697497-9a-through-10b_2010-12-23.zipExtract the full contents of the archive to a folder in a convenient location. For example, on Windows, double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux, use the unzip command. Another option is to use the unzip command in MATLAB itself.Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txtThe updates provided in this workaround are only for releases R2009a and later.

Summary: Segmentation violation when generating code for all variant subsystems if Data Store Memory blocks are missing
Description: A segmentation violation occurs when generating code for all variants if Data Store Read blocks in variant subsystems do not have corresponding Data Store Memory blocks. You should see an error instead.
Workaround: Ensure that a corresponding Data Store Memory block exists for every Data Store Read blocks used in variant subsystems.

Summary: Computer may reboot unexpectedly while processing a model that uses the Sensoray 626 driver
Description: The computer may unexpectedly reboot if, while processing a model, it calls a certain real-time kernel function from one of several board drivers, among them the Sensoray 626.
Workaround: As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files, named according to the release they support.Select and download the workaround file for your release.Locate the directory of the downloaded file. This directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Type matlabroot to locate your MATLAB installation. This directory is referred to as <MATLABROOT> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Navigate to <DOWNLOAD> and open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to <MATLABROOT>.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type rtwintgt -setup to reinstall the Real-Time Windows Target kernel.December 20, 2010: attachment_679135_10b_win32_2010-12-20.zip: initial version; works on R2010b only

Summary: imdilate crashes on Mac when given logical datatype image
Description: When given images of type logical, the imdilate  function may cause a segmentation violation.

Summary: Junctions disappear inside subcharts that are grouped
Description: A data-structure corruption occurs when a subchart with junctions is grouped (for example, by double-clicking it) and then a hierarchy rebuild occurs (for example, when you navigate up out of the subchart and then back into it). After the hierarchy rebuild, the junctions in the subchart might still be visible, but the firstJunction  property is lost.  As a result, the junctions eventually become invisible (for example, if you save/load the model or navigate up and down the hierarchy twice).
Workaround: Do not group a subchart. If your model is already corrupted or you suspect it is corrupted, extract attached zip and run the corruptionDetector.m script.  This script detects corruptions in your model and explains how to fix it manually. Only one edit to the model file is required per corrupted subchart.

Summary: Segmentation violation when displaying variant subsystem if the mask drawing code contains set_params
Description: If the mask icon drawing code of blocks inside inactive variants contains a set_param call, then you may see a segmentation violation when Simulink tries to render the block on the screen.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, do not change a value on the block during drawing. Do not use set_param in the mask icon display section.

Summary: Variant subsystem with preprocessor conditionals doesn't work in simplified initialization mode.
Description: Enabling 'Generate preprocessor conditionals' on a Variant subsystem will not work with 'Underspecified initialization detection' is set to Simplified.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: SIL behavior for exported functions/runnables with feedback loops may be incorrect
Description: The behavior a set of exported AUTOSAR runnables may differ between Normal mode and SIL simulations if the model serving as a test harness feeds the output of any runnable back to an input of the runnable.
Workaround: ?For both the original model from which runnables are exported and the test-harness model, set the value of the field Configuration Parameters > Connectivity > Context-dependent inputs to Enable all. Any subsequent diagnostic indicates a situation that may lead to inconsistencies between Normal mode and SIL simulations. Examine the documentation suggested by the diagnostic.

Summary: Index out of bounds error when fitting a power model with a single positive y-value
Description: If you try to fit either 'power1' or 'power2' power models, and you have only one positive y-value, then you see an "index out of bounds error" like the following example.>> fit( [1; 2; 3], [1; -2; -3], 'power2' ) ??? Attempted to access x(2); index out of bounds because numel(x)=1.Error in ==> fit at 329                errstr = handleerr(es.identifier, es.message, suppresserr);
Workaround: To work around this problem, repeat the data so that there is enough positive y-data, for example:>> x = [1; 2; 3];>> y = [-1; -2; 3];>> f = fit( [x; x], [y; y], 'power2' )The problem occurs during computation of start points for the fit. Repeating the data avoids the problem in the start point computation. It has no effect on the fit because it is equivalent to giving each data point the same weight of 2.

Summary: Assertion during code generation for Switch block
Description: Code generation might cause an assertion under all of the following conditions: A [m,n]  matrix signal is the input to a Selector block, which produces a [1,n]  output signal. The Selector block output signal connects to a Switch block, which has at least one other [n]  input signal.
Workaround: Reshape the [n] input signal(s) connected to the Switch block to [1,n].

Summary: Using matrices of structures in Embedded MATLAB code might produce incorrect results
Description: You might get incorrect simulation results or generated code if you use matrices in Embedded MATLAB code where each element is a structure that contains at least one matrix field. This bug can occur in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink models, Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts, and code compiled with emlc . For an example, see the attached MATLAB file CompositeMatrices.m . To reproduce the problem, follow these steps: Produce baseline results by running CompositeMatrices  in MATLAB Build a MEX file using this command: emlc CompositeMatrices  Run the MEX file CompositeMatrices . The MEX results differ from the MATLAB results.

Summary: Incorrect block initialization and code generation when a subsystem is inside another subsystem that contains a For Each block
Description: Generated code does not correctly initialize the block output, dwork or zero-crossing for a block inside a function-call subsystem under the following conditions: The block is not inside a subsystem in that function-call subsystem The function-call subsystem is inside another subsystem which contains a For Each block
Workaround: As a workaround, add an atomic subsystem in such function-call subsystem and move the block into this atomic subsystem.

Summary: Model Explorer hangs when a Stateflow chart containing a Simulink function and a linked atomic subchart with a broken library link is opened
Description: The Model Explorer becomes unresponsive when you try to navigate to a chart in the Model Hierarchy pane, and the following conditions are met: A Stateflow chart contains a Simulink function. The chart also contains a linked atomic subchart. The linked atomic subchart refers to a missing library model.
Workaround: Please fix each broken linked atomic subchart by double-clicking on it and linking to the correct library chart.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Behavior of generated code and Normal mode simulation may not match for exported AUTOSAR runnable
Description: If you export a runnable from a single function-call subsystem, the behavior of the generated code may not match the Normal mode simulation if you select the parameter Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs  for an inport to the subsystem.
Workaround: For each Inport block inside function-call subsystems from which AUTOSAR runnables are exported:Double-click the Inport block to open the block parameter dialog box.Clear the check box Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs.Click OK.

Summary: Misplaced checks when using the option -unit-by-unit-common-source
Description: When using the option -unit-by-unit-common-source  for a unit-by-unit verification, the file specified as common source is not displayed correctly, but is replaced by the preprocessed version of this file. Hence, the checks will be misplaced.

Summary: Error during validation or code generation with multiple runnable AUTOSAR model
Description: Consider an AUTOSAR model with multiple runnables inside a wrapper subsystem. The software may produce an error when you try to validate the AUTOSAR configuration or generate code for the runnables if  the wrapper subsystem contains a From  block with no corresponding Goto  block. The error message is similar to the following: Error:Undefined function or method 'eq' for input arguments of type 'cell'.
Workaround: To work around the problem:Place the corresponding Goto block inside the wrapper subsystem.Connect the input of the Goto block to a Ground block or a Constant block with Constant value 0.

Summary: MATLAB ceases execution without any stack trace or error while simulating with implicit Simulink solver
Description: An uncaught memmory allocation error causes MATLAB to stop running without any stack trace or error. This memory allocation exception occours when the model has large dimension signals and uses:  An implicit solver  A solver Jacobian method other than full perturbation  This result is due to the need for more memory and time to get the sparse pattern information.
Workaround: On the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select 'Full perturbation' for the solver Jaobian method parameter.

Summary: When "Port and parameter mismatch" is set to error, and all input or output ports in a referenced model have their names hidden, the parent model always warns on loading
Description: When Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Model Referencing > Port and parameter mismatch  is set to error  and all input or output ports in a referenced model have their names hidden, the parent model always warns on loading.
Workaround: Do one of the following: Set Port and parameter mismatch to none or warning in the parent model.Show the block name on one or more of the input ports and one or more of the output ports in the referenced model.

Summary: Software failure during export of multiple AUTOSAR runnables
Description: The software may fail during the export of multiple AUTOSAR runnables if there is a Goto  block inside the wrapper subsystem and the corresponding From  block is outside the wrapper subsystem.

Summary: Generated code may fail to compile for variant subsystems that are controlled by C preprocessor conditionals
Description: The code generated for the C API may fail to compile when the model contains variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals. The problem is caused by the code referencing the address of parameter data that is excluded from compilation due to the preprocessor conditionals.

Summary: Compiler error or warning when code generated for variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals includes tunable parameters
Description: The code generated for the C API may contain an empty data structure for the model's tunable parameters when the model contains variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals.  This empty structure may be treated as an error by the compiler and can result in a warning or error.

Summary: Parameter mismatch error during code generation when mixing R2010b models with models created in earlier releases
Description: If you generate code for a model in which models created in R2010b are mixed with models from earlier releases, an error similar to the following might occur: The following Configuration parameter option(s) do not match between the models 'x' and 'y':Hardware Implementation- ProdLargestAtomicInteger (Long != Char)ProdLargestAtomicInteger is mapped to 'integer:'- ProdLargestAtomicFloat (Double != None)ProdLargestAtomicFloat is mapped to 'floating-point:' This error is likely to occur if both of the following conditions exist: At least one R1010b model and at least one model from an earlier release coexist in a model reference hierarchy or share a utilities directory.  The models have their hardware configured to a device that does not match at least one of the following settings: Number of bits: int  set to 32 Largest atomic size: integer  set to Char Largest atomic size: floating point  set to None   (For example, this condition could apply to Freescale 32-bit PowerPC, which does not match the second and third settings.) Example devices include: Most Freescale devices Analog Devices: Blackfin, SHARC, or TigerSHARC ARM Compatible: ARM 7, ARM 8, ARM 9, ARM 10, ARM 11, or ARM Cortex Texas Instruments: C6000 or C2000 All 16-bit integer devices
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2010b, for each model from an earlier release, go to the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box and toggle the hardware device selection. This reapplies the hardware device characteristics to the model, making the values consistent with the R2010b model.

Summary: Requesting the edit-time signal hierarchy crashes MATLAB if a block with a broken link exists upstream
Description: When you request the edit-time signal hierarchy for a signal, if a broken link block exists upstream on the path of a signal, then MATLAB crashes instead of producing an error message. You can request edit-time signal hierarchy either by opening the Bus Creator, Bus Selector, or Bus Assignment block dialog, or by calling the get_param  method on a port handle, using the SignalHierarchy  property.
Workaround: Update your model. During the update process, Simulink reports broken links as an error.Resolve the broken link.Request the edit-time signal hierarchy.
