Summary: Incorrect code generation: Indexing into scalar with variable-size indices causes wrong answers
Description: The problem occurs when one of the following code patterns is used: x(I) = s;  x(I,J) = s;  x(I,J,K) = s;  ...   under the following conditions: x  and s  must be scalars (size 1x1). At least one of the indices (I , J , ...) must be variable sized.  In R2010b the bug causes an assertion failure during code generation, rather than generating incorrect code.
Workaround: Rewrite the assignment as a loop:for i = 1:numel(I)  for j = 1:numel(J)    x(I(i),J(j)) = s;  endend

Summary: Error reported when structure field, used by nontunable block parameter, shares name with tunable variable
Description: If the following conditions exist, Simulink incorrectly reports a warning or error: You specify an expression for a block parameter that cannot be tuned, such as Sample time  The expression refers to a structure field, object property, or object method that has the same name as a tunable variable in the base workspace Diagnostics > Data Validity > Detect loss of tunability  in the Configuration Parameters dialog box is set to warning  or error
Workaround: Suppress the warning or error by setting Diagnostics > Data Validity > Detect loss of tunability to none.

Summary: Error message or crash when block uses elapsed time with negative start time
Description: An error message or crash might occur when a block uses elapsed time with a negative start time under the following conditions: On the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Start time  parameter is set to a negative value. On the Optimization  pane, the Application life span  parameter is set to in  or a larger value such that Simulink must use two unsigned int32  to store the absolute time counter. This avoids overflow. For example, a value greater than MAX_UINT32/(3600*24)/ts , where ts  is the base rate step size of the model. The block is in a triggered or function-call subsystem and must use elapsed time, for example, a Discrete Integrator block.  Simulation does not finish successfully. You might see the following behavior: In R2006b-R2007a, simulating the model causes the following error message: Unexpected internal error.  MATLAB crashes.  In R2007b-8b, the model continuously runs. If you try to close the model, Simulink displays the message: 'ModelName' is running. Do you want to stop it and close the model?  The model does not close successfully.  In R2009b-R2010b, you might see the following error message: The time between the last two triggers was so long that the Elapsed Time Counter used more than 32 bits. The block 'BlockName' can not carry out accurate calculations when this happens.   ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can do one of the following:Avoid a negative start time. Code generation does not support a negative start time.On the Optimization pane, reduce the value of the Application life span parameter.Change the Sample time type parameter of the function call port from triggered to periodic.?

Summary: histfit produces a histogram and a density plot with different areas.
Description: The histfit  command produces a graph containing a histogram and a fitted density plot. However, the area under the density plot is smaller than the area under the histogram. This discrepency can make it hard to compare the two plots.
Workaround: To decide whether the distribution fits the data, it is safe to compare just the shapes of the histogram and fitted density plot. For example, the fit can be good if both are similar bell-shaped curves, even if the density plot is lower than the histogram bin heights.

Summary: Model with array of buses signal may not generate accurate code
Description: Code generation for may be inaccurate when all  of the following conditions are true: The model contains an array of buses signal. The array of buses signal has the Struct  custom storage class specified (via Signal Properties dialog box or a Simulink.Signal  object). The Configuration Parameters -> Code Generation -> SIL and PIL Verification -> Create Block  option is set to SIL .  When code is generated for this kind of model, the compilation process may error. The output of the compilation process looks similar to the following: modelname_sf.c(344) : error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct modelname_sf.c(345) :error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct modelname_sf.c(352) :error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct modelname_sf.c(353) : error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct

Summary: Generated code may fail to compile for reused variant subsystems that are controlled by C preprocessor conditionals
Description: The generated code may fail to compile when the model contains variant subsystems that are reused as well as controlled by C preprocessor conditionals. The problem is caused by the code referencing the address of non-constant parameter data that is excluded from compilation due to the preprocessor conditionals.

Summary: Code generation errors with arrays of buses and custom storage classes
Description: Indexing code for arrays of buses signal lines in a Simulink model may be incorrect when generating code from the model. The incorrect code will appear in the output and/or the update methods if both  of the following conditions are true: The model has at least one array of buses signal. The custom GetSet  storage class is specified for the array of buses signal (via the Signal Properties dialog box or a Simulink.Signal  object).  Under these conditions, if TLC assertions have been turned on (using the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Debugging  option), then a TLC assertion similar to the following may be thrown: Error: File: (matlabroot)/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/tlc/GetSet.tlc Line: 56 Column: 9 Assertion failed
Workaround: See EBR 923193.

Summary: Incorrect value returned for negative log likelihood of inverse Gaussian distribution
Description: The reported negative log likelihood value for the inverse Gaussian distribution is incorrect. It is missing a constant additive term. This constant is not a function of the parameters. The erroneous log likelihood value appears in the fit summary of the Distribution Fitting Tool, and also as the NLogL  property of a fitted probability distribution object.
Workaround: For many purposes you can safely ignore the missing term. For example, some hypothesis tests involve the difference between two log likelihood values, and the constant term does not affect the difference.There are cases where the constant is important, such as using the value to compute an AIC statistic for comparing an inverse Gaussian fit with the fit of a different distribution. In that case, the workaround is to compute the negative log likelihood directly. For example, to compute the negative log likelihood for a data vector x at parameter values mu and lambda, enter:nlogl = -sum(log(pdf('inversegaussian',x,mu,lambda)))

Summary: Callback function error dialog box may appear when you click items in Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box rapidly
Description: In the Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box, if you click certain items in rapid succession, for example, items on the Runnables  tab, you may see the Callback function error dialog box with the following message: Error: Callback function Detected 1 error(s) in RTW.FcnCtlUI.getDialogSchema: Subscript indices must either be real positive or logicals.
Workaround: If you see the Callback function error dialog box, click OK, which closes the dialog box. In the Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box, when you select an item, wait until the display is updated before you select another item.

Summary: Parameters UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection and MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec are not saved in MATLAB file when using the saveAs method of the Simulink.ConfigSet object
Description: When using the saveAs method of the Simulink.ConfigSet object, the parameters UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection  and MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec  are not saved in the resulting MATLAB file. Consequently, the Simulink.ConfigSet object loaded from the MATLAB file will always have the default values for these parameters. The default values UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection  and MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec  are 'Simplified' and 'none', respectively. As an example, the problem is illustrated for the parameter UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection  by the following MATLAB command sequence: >> currConfigset = Simulink.ConfigSetcurrConfigset =Simulink.ConfigSet (handle)  Description: ''  Components: [8x1 Simulink.ConfigComponent]  Name: 'Configuration'  >> get_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection')  ans =  Classic  >> set_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection', 'Simplified')>> get_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection')  ans =  Simplified  >> currConfigset.saveAs('modifiedConfigset.m')>> clear currConfigset>> currConfigset = modifiedConfigsetcurrConfigset =Simulink.ConfigSet (handle)  Description: ''  Components: [8x1 Simulink.ConfigComponent]  Name: 'Configuration'  >> get_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection')  ans =  Classic  >>

Summary: Code for variant subsystems may define unnecessary data
Description: The code generated for a model containing variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals may fail to conditionally compile those subsystems' constant and unstructured parameter data. As a consequence, the compiled executable is not optimally efficient because it defines data that is never used.

Summary: Selecting Find Referenced Variables context menu item in Model Explorer for libraries and blocks in libraries renders Model Explorer unresponsive or crashes MATLAB
Description: The Model Explorer context menu item Find Referenced Variables  is not disabled for libraries and blocks in libraries, even though you cannot use this feature with libraries or blocks within libraries. Selecting that menu item causes Model Explorer to become unresponsive. On some platforms (for example, Linux x86-64), it may cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: int can return wrong results if boundaries are given in descending order.
Description: Definite integration with the larger boundary given first can return answers with the wrong sign. For example, int  returns 3 instead of -3 for the following definite integral: syms xint(x^2,2,-1)ans =3
Workaround: When computing definite integrals, specify boundaries in ascending order.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model reference with variable-size signal with non-Auto storage class
Description: Simulink might give an incorrect answer and Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for a model, which contains a model block and its referenced model has either of the following: A variable-size root inport and the output signal of the root Inport block has an ExportedGlobal , ImportedExtern,  or ImportedExternalPointer  storage class. A variable-size root outport and the input signal of the root Outport block has an ExportedGlobal , ImportedExtern,  or ImportedExternalPointer  storage class.

Summary: In Japanese Windows 7 environments, MATLAB Report Generator generates unreadable text
Description: In a Japanese Windows 7 environment, for PDF documents, MATLAB Report Generator and Simulink Report Generator may generate garbled or incorrect text.
Workaround: ?Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file for your release (for example, attachment_706307_9b_2011-02-07.zip for R2009b) and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Data Type Conversion block might not report overflows for certain built-in type conversions
Description: For the Data Type Conversion block, the "Detect overflow" diagnostics might not report overflows for floating point to built-in integer conversions. The software always performs the wrapping operation correctly, but the diagnostics might not report that wrapping occurred. This bug occurs under the following circumstances: When converting from double or single data types to 8, 16, and 32-bit signed and unsigned integers With all rounding modes except Zero and Simplest Only for values in the vicinity of the limits of the integer range. For a specific integer type, MIN_INT > x > MIN_INT-1 and MAX_INT < x < MAX_INT+1.  ?
Workaround: Use rounding to zero or simplest rounding for floating point to integer conversions.If you use rounding modes other than Zero and Simplest, do not rely on the diagnostics for values around the limit of the integer range (around MIN_INT and around MAX_INT for the specified word length and signedness).

Summary: Incorrect algebraic loop simulation
Description: Simulink returns an incorrect algebraic loop result when the following is true: The model contains an algebraic loop. In the Data Import/Export  pane of the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Save to workspace > States  check box is cleared. In the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, ert.tlc  is selected as the current system target file. In the Real-Time Workshop > Interface  pane of the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, Code interface > Single output/update function  check box is selected.
Workaround: As a workaround, before starting a simulation of a model that contains algebraic loops, in the Data Import/Export pane of the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, select the Save to workspace > States check box.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when acquiring from a JAI GigE Vision camera in a BayerGB format
Description: MATLAB may crash when acquiring video data from a JAI GigE Vision camera using a BayerGB video format. This is caused by improper handling of a change to the minimum y-offset and maximum height allowed.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Comments on OVFL checks are not imported when you import comments from verification results generated with older versions of the software?
Description: When importing comments from verification results generated with older versions of the software, justifications and comments on scalar OVFL and float OVFL checks are not imported into the new results.

Summary: Subsystem to referenced model conversion may cause a segmentation violation
Description: If a subsystem has one or more Subsystem Variants blocks as children, then Simulink may crash with a segmentation violation if you convert that subsystem to a referenced model.

Summary: When a model that contains Simscape blocks is simulated in Accelerator mode, a segmentation violation might occur
Description: When a model that contains Simscape blocks is simulated in Accelerator mode, a segmentation violation might occur. You might see this behavior if you rerun the model after you edit the Simscape blocks.
Workaround: As a workaround, in the file matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/accelreg.tlc replace line%<FcnGenInitMassMatrixPrInRoot()>withif (ssGetSizeofDWork(S) == sizeof(%<::tDWorkType>)) {%<FcnGenInitMassMatrixPrInRoot()>}

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Software-In-the-Loop (SIL) and Simulink simulation mismatch for multi-rate subsystem
Description: A SIL block created for a subsystem might not reflect the same behavior under the following conditions The input signals to the selected Subsystem block are updated at multiple rates. The Inport blocks of the Subsystem block have the block parameter, Sample time  (for -1 inherited) , set to -1 .
Workaround: Avoid setting the block parameter, Sample time (-1 for inherited), to -1 for Inport blocks of subsystems intended for code generation.

Note that this fix does not allow you to launch ModelSim from a directory with white-space in the path. To change the ModelSim launching directory, change the current MATLAB directory or specify a valid path with the 'rundir' property of the vsim command.Alternatively, you can use a currently supported version of ModelSim to avoid this error. Summary: A warning for a minimum step size violation causes Simulink to become unresponsive
Description: In very rare situations, the warning for a minimum step size violation is thrown consecutively and Simulink becomes unresponsive. You cannot stop the simulation without terminating MATLAB. This bug is fixed and you can use CTRL-C in the MATLAB command window to stop the simulation.
Workaround: By adjusting solver parameters, such as Relative tolerance, Absolute tolerance, or Initial step size, you can avoid the warning for a minimum step size violation.

Summary: Underspecified initialization detection parameter value incorrectly displayed on Japanese machine
Description: On a Japanese machine, on the Diagnostics > Data Validity  pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box, the Underspecified initialization detection  parameter always displays Classic , even when the parameter is set to Simplified .

Summary: Non-Compilable code for terminate function of ForEach Subsystem
Description: The code generator might generate a non-compilable terminate function for a ForEach subsystem. This might occur when you right-click a virtual subsystem, select C/C++ Code , and then Export Functions .
Workaround: Do not generate a terminate function. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Interface pane, clear the Terminate function required parameter.

Summary: Acquiring from two instances of a single GigE Vision camera may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Attempting to simultaneously acquire video data from two instances of VIDEOINPUT objects that both refer to the same device may cause MATLAB to crash. This same problem can also occur if you have two From Video Device blocks pointing to the same device.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: If a referenced model's Model arguments have changed, opening a parent model with Port and parameter mismatch set to error or warning may crash Simulink
Description: Simulink crashes when all  of the following are true: You are opening a model where Diagnostics > Model Referencing > Port and parameter mismatch  is set to error  or warning . A mismatch is reported and is caused by a change to a referenced model's Model arguments. One of the mismatched Model arguments is the empty string.
Workaround: Change the referenced model so that the Model arguments mismatch error does not involve the empty string. If the crash is occurring because the referenced model was changed:From not having Model arguments to having Model arguments, then open the referenced model, remove the Model arguments, and save the referenced model.From having Model arguments to not having Model arguments, then open the referenced model, add one or more Model arguments, and save the referenced model.On the parent model, set Diagnostics > Model Referencing > Port and parameter mismatch to none.In the referenced model, set the Model arguments to the desired value.In the parent model, refresh the Model blocks by selecting Edit > Links and Model Blocks > Refresh.On the parent model, set Diagnostics > Model Referencing > Port and parameter mismatch to its previous value.The parent model should no longer crash when you open it.

Summary: MATLAB hangs while simulating a model using an implicit solver
Description: For a model with large dimension signals, using an implicit solver in combination with any solver Jacobian method other than full perturbation may cause MATLAB to hang. This result is due to the large memory and time required to get the sparse pattern information. ? ?
Workaround: On the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select 'Full perturbation' for the solver Jaobian method parameter.

Summary: When downloading the results of a remote verification, the download fails if the archive is larger than 2Gb.
Description: When downloading the results of a remote verification, the software creates a file named analysis-downloaded.zip  (n being the verification ID). If this file is bigger than 2Gb, the download will fail. In the file polyspaced.log , you will see a message like the following: 11-Nov-2010, 14:35:09: Initializing connections... 11-Nov-2010, 14:35:10: Listening to port 12427... Call from C to SML raised exception. unhandled exception: Subscript
Workaround: Even for huge applications, the results folder is not bigger than 2Gb unless you use the option -keep-all-files.Therefore, if you experience this problem, do not use the option -keep-all-files.

Summary: When trying to zoom into multiple overlaid axes simultaneously, only the first axes gets the call to zoom.
Description: If there are several axes on top of each other and zoom is called interactively on the axes, only the top axes zooms. If zooming has been disabled for the top axes with setallowaxeszoom , then no zoom is performed.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by downloading the attached file, zoom.m: 1. Rename your zoom.m file to zoom.m_old by navigating to matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\graph2d, where matlabroot is the result of typing matlabroot at the command prompt. 2. Download the attachment and save the file into the same folder. 3. Restart MATLAB. You will now be able to zoom into multiple axes simultaneously.

Summary: When Production Hardware is ASIC/FPGA, Simulink Sum block configured to saturate might incorrectly allow overflows to wrap
Description: A Simulink Sum block configured to provide saturation might incorrectly allow overflows to wrap around instead of saturate. For a Sum block to be exposed to this bug, the following necessary conditions must be met: Production Hardware is set to ASIC/FPGA. For example, Device vendor  is set to ASIC/FPGA on the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Sum block parameters are set as follows: Output data type  is set to Inherit: Same as accumulator. Saturate on integer overflow  is turned ON. Accumulator data type  is NOT set to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule. None of the data types are floating point. The accumulator used by the block must be less than full precision.
Workaround: For a Sum block that is potentially exposed to the bug:Compile the model to determine the output data type.Change the block's output data type setting. Do not use Inherit: Same as accumulator. Instead, explicitly set the output data type determined by compiling the model.The attached MATLAB function detectSumLostSatForAsicFpgaSameAsAccum automatically checks a model for Sum blocks potentially exposed to the bug and suggests an automated workaround.Usage   res = detectSumLostSatForAsicFpgaSameAsAccum(mdl,verbose)Input Arguments   mdl            = model name, defaults to bdroot   verbose        = display analysis information, defaults to trueOutput Argument   res            = structure with three fields   res.comments   = string describing finding   res.blks       = cell array of paths to blocks that may have bug   res.workaround = string of commands that can workaround the bugIf the proposed workaround is appropriate for your model, it can be applied automatically by executing the following command:eval(res.workaround)

Summary: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation may fail when code coverage is enabled
Description: If you run a SIL simulation and use the BullseyeCoverage tool to collect coverage metrics, you may get an error that includes the following message: An error occurred while calling into the SIL or PIL target connectivity implementation. There must be exactly one probe with empty column number This error occurs when the generated code uses a ternary operator and  the code is formatted such that BullseyeCoverage inserts a probe to measure both a condition and a decision at the first non-whitespace character on a line. For example, the following code will trigger this error:       14 xcv3 = =>F  15 (xcv1==0) && =>t       ^ =>t  16 (xcv2==1)  =>f  17 && (xcv2==0) ?      18 1 : 0;

Summary: When running a target application that contains raw Ethernet blocks, you might observe a one sample time delay on packet reception
Description: If the Ethernet Rx block executes before the Ethernet Init block, your model might experience a one sample time delay when receiving packets. To verify the model block execution order, select Format > Block Displays > Sorted Order  in the model window. With the exception of this potential delay, a raw Ethernet model continues to send and receive packets as expected. The raw Ethernet blocks have the following execution priority, from first to last: Buffer Mngmt Ethernet Init All other raw Ethernet and network buffer blocks
Workaround: As a workaround:In release R2010a and R2010b, manually change the Ethernet Init Priority property to -1.In R2011a and beyond, do one of the following Manually change the Ethernet Init Priority property to -1.Replace the Ethernet Init block with a copy from the R2011a (or beyond) library?

Summary: Unhelpful error message generated when SIL simulation with code coverage fails because of very long path names
Description: If you run a simulation using the Software-in-the-Loop (SIL)  mode and the combination of file name and file path for any source file used in the build exceeds 255 characters, the software produces the following assertion: ??? Error due to multiple causes. Caused by: An error occurred while calling into the SIL or PIL targetconnectivity implementation. Assertion failed. Error while bringing model references of model model_name upto date: An error occurred while calling into the SIL or PIL target connectivity implementation.  This message does not contain useful information, and does not indicate how you can resolve the problem.
Workaround: You can resolve this issue by shortening the path and file name combinations that are generated during the SIL simulation. Change the current working folder so that there is no source file with a full path that is longer than 255 characters. This limit to the length of the path and file name combination applies to automatically generated code as well as legacy code that is integrated into the build.

Summary: Incorrect "class mismatch" errors for local scope data of Truth Table block
Description: If local scope data is defined and used in a Truth Table block, a model might not simulate due to incorrect "class mismatch" errors. For example, you might see an error that looks something like this: Class mismatch (double ~= single).The class to the left is the class of the left-hand side of the assignment.

Summary: With JobManager Security Level 3 on Windows, job submissions from a user name that contains non-alphanumeric characters always fail
Description: When running the JobManager at Security Level 3 on Windows, submitting a job as a user with a user name that contains non-alphanumeric character always fails. Even though the user name and password are specified correctly, the submission returns something like this error message: Failed to evaluate task as XXXX.YYYY because:  While attempting to execute"C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\bin\win64\matlab.exe -dmlworker -noFigureWindows -r initworker": Logon failure: unknown user name or bad passwordThe user name or password you entered does not match the system credentialson the cluster machines. To correct this, you can update the Username propertyof the job manager, or use changePassword to change the password to the correctsystem password. For information on this method, type help distcomp.jobmanager/changePassword

Summary: HPC Server Integration: Validation fails the Find Resource stage if the cluster has no WCF broker nodes
Description: If the cluster has been configured so that there are no WCF broker nodes, and an hpcserver configuration specifies ClusterVersion = 'HPCServer2008' , then findResource  errors. This causes the configuration validation to fail at the Find Resource stage. This applies only for ClusterVersion = 'HPCServer2008' . Configurations with ClusterVersion = 'CCS'  work correctly.
Workaround: There are three workarounds to this problem:Use ClusterVersion = 'CCS' instead of 'HPCServer2008'.Configure the cluster to have 1 or more WCF broker nodes. This enables you to submit SOA jobs to the cluster.If you want to use ClusterVersion = 'HPCServer2008' without SOA jobs, then download the attached file (HPCServerSchedulerConnection.m) and use it to replace the existing file of the same name in the folder matlabaroot\toolbox\distcomp\distcomp\+distcomp.

Summary: The generic scheduler support for nonshared file systems, and the sftp protocol of the remote command line, are unable to transmit more than 100 files to sftp servers with OpenSsh versions earlier than 4.9
Description: The sftp server of OpenSsh had a bug that prevented more than 100 files from being transferred at one time, as described in https://bugzilla.mindrot.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1397 . This bug was fixed in OpenSsh 4.9. Red Hat 5.5 comes with OpenSsh 4.3p2 and exhibits this bug. Symptoms of the bug can be somewhat obscure. When the sftp client transmits the 101st file from the client to the server, error messages like these can result: >> j.submit??? Error using ==> distcomp.genericscheduler.pSubmitJobCommon at 64 Job submission did not occur because the user supplied SubmitFcn (distributedSubmitFcn) errored.Error using ==> RemoteClusterAccess>RemoteClusterAccess.startMirrorForJob at 264 Failed to start mirror for job with ID 9.Error in ==> distributedSubmitFcn at 122 remoteConnection.startMirrorForJob(job);Error in ==> distcomp.genericscheduler.pSubmitJobCommon at 48    feval(submitFcn, scheduler, job, setprop, args{:});Error in ==> distcomp.genericscheduler.pSubmitJob at 16 scheduler.pSubmitJobCommon( job, scheduler.SubmitFcn );Error in ==> distcomp.abstractjob.submit at 48    scheduler.pSubmitJob(job);Caused by:    Error using ==> RemoteClusterAccess>RemoteClusterAccess.waitForChoreToFinishOrError at 814    The following errors occurred in the com.mathworks.toolbox.distcomp.clusteraccess.UploadFilesChore:    Could not send Job9 for job 9    com.mathworks.toolbox.distcomp.remote.ProtocolFulfillmentException:    com.mathworks.toolbox.distcomp.remote.ProtocolFulfillmentException: Copy \\drive\username\usertest\local\jobs\Job9 to    0.0.1.1:/export/home/matlab/userid/remote/jobs/Job9 : while copying    \\drive\username\usertest\local\jobs\Job9\Task20.state.mat to    /export/home/matlab/userid/remote/jobs/Job9/Task20.state.mat: FailureError in ==> distcomp.genericscheduler.pSubmitJob at 16 scheduler.pSubmitJobCommon( job, scheduler.SubmitFcn );Error in ==> distcomp.abstractjob.submit at 48    scheduler.pSubmitJob(job); Failure  indicates that one of the sftp servers hit an unanticipated problem: in this case, running out of file handles. Because the MDCS generic scheduler uses at least (5 x tasks + 1) files, a simple 19-task job will work, but a 20-task job will exhibit the problem.
Workaround: Limiting jobs to no more than 19 tasks avoids this problem, but is generally not a practical solution. For parallel jobs, this limit means 19 or fewer workers.If possible, upgrade to OpenSsh 4.9 or later, although this might require upgrading the operating system. However, to use the generic scheduler nonshared filesystem code, you need to upgrade only the submission host in the cluster. That option may give some leeway for a quick fix if upgrading the whole cluster is not practical.For the remote command line tool, use the scp protocol to copy files.

Summary: Polyspace results displays a false green IDP after a NULL assignment to a reference-to-pointer
Description: Polyspace verification reports a false green IDP after a NULL assignment to a reference-to-pointer. The following example illustrates this behavior: void foo(int **dptr) {  *dptr=(int *)0;}int main (void){  int x = 1;  int *ptr=&x;  foo(&ptr); /* ptr is set to NULL in foo() */  x = *ptr + 1; /* green IDP that should be red */  return x;}

Summary: The Block Processing block does not process all blocks of image
Description: The Block Processing  block produces wrong results by only processing the first block of the image and ignoring the other blocks. This error occurs when the sample time of the input block is set to a constant, such as "inf ".
Workaround: Set the sample time of the input block to the Block Processing block to a finite value. For example, set sample time to "1".

Summary: As of R2011a, blockproc more restrictive for paths and file names of JPEG2000 files
Description: In releases prior to R2010b, blockproc  was more permissive with regard to the formatting of paths and file names of JPEG2000 input images. For example, files could be specified like this:  '~/file.jp2' './file.jp2'  In R2010b, these "relative path" file names began to error erroneously. Instead files must be specified as either a file name only ('file.jp2'   ) or else as a fully qualified absolute path and file name.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can simply specify your input JPEG2000 file to blockproc as a simple file name string, such as 'file.jp2', or else as a fully qualified absolute path and file name string.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier ignores specified input minimum and maximum values when Test Condition or Proof Assumption block is also specified on the same signal.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier ignores specified input minimum and maximum values in certain cases. This bug surfaces in some cases when a Test Condition or Proof Assumption block is also specified on the connecting signal and when the model contains certain math operation blocks, such as sqrt, etc.

Summary: Digital Output blocks for C28X3X, C2833x, and C2834x display incorrect checkbox labels for GPIO pins 35 through 38
Description: In the Digital Output  block, when you set GPIO Group  to GPIO32~GPIO39 , the block displays the checkbox labels for GPIO pins 35, 36, 37, and 38 incorrectly.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Acquiring from more than one GigE Vision camera at a time is not possible
Description: Due to a bug, it is not possible to acquire image data from more than one GigE Vision camera at a time and you will instead see the error message: Could not connect to the image acquisition device. Device may be in use.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Compiling models that use Simulink.Parameter objects with struct data type can sometimes crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB may crash when you compile a model that uses Simulink.Parameter  objects for which DataType  is explicitly set to struct  while Value  is not a MATLAB structure.
Workaround: To prevent a MATLAB session crash, do not explicity set the DataType of a Simulink.Parameter object to struct.The DataType of a Simulink.Parameter object is automatically set to struct when its Value is set to that of a MATLAB structure. The automatic setting to struct does not occur when the DataType has already been set to struct or a bus data type.

Summary: Using the option '-machine-architecture 64' can stop a verification with the error message "Out of memory. Insufficient space in nursery." during a 'Software Safety Analysis Level' stage
Description: When launching PolySpace verification, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: Out of memory. Insufficient space in nursery.  This happens mostly when using the option '-machine-architecture 64 ' on a large verification (more than 100 kloc lines), and with a powerful machine (at least 8 cores and 8GB RAM) including a 64-Bits OS.
Workaround: Relaunching your verification using the option '-machine-architecture 32' and scaling options may solve the issue.

Summary: Fourier block and Total Harmonic Distortion block of powerlib_extras cannot handle nonscalar inputs
Description: When the Fourier block is used to analyse a vectorized signal (a three-phase voltage, for example), only the magnitude (Mag  output of the block) of the first element of the input signal is calculated. The calculated phase (the Phase  output of the block) is then giving wrong results. Similarly, when the Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) block is used to analyze a vectorized signal, only the first element of the input singal is analyzed. Note that both blocks work correctly when they analyze a scalar signal.
Workaround: Use a Demux block to demultiplex the input signal, then perform the Fourier analysis or THD analysis using the blocks on every scalar signal. Depending on your application, the calculated signals can be multiplexed (using a Mux block) at the output to return a vectorized signal.

Summary: Neural network training fails when running on a parallel cluster
Description: Neural network training fails in a MATLAB worker, because these workers run without Java.

Summary: In Bus Assignment and Bus Selector blocks, unexpected selection after repeatedly using the Remove button
Description: When you use the Remove  button to remove a signal from the selected list for a Bus Assignment or Bus Selector block, Simulink removes the selected signal and then automatically selects the next signal and  the first signal in the selection list. If you press the Remove  button again, Simulink removes the two signals that it automatically selected.
Workaround: Do not press the Remove button twice in a row. Instead:Select the first signal to remove.Press the Remove button.Manually select the next signal that you want to remove.Press the Remove button.

Summary: Adding an include path containing ampersand character (&) causes problem in the Polyspace Graphical Interface
Description: If you add a path to the list of includes that contains an ampersand character (&), the 'Run' and 'Stop' button in the Polyspace Graphical Interface become greyed out.For example, inside the configuration file, an ampersand character can be found: <include>...  <file path="file:/C:/MyApplication/MainSources/Mysub& folder/" order="N"/>...</include>  Note : This could also occur with '>' or '<' characters.
Workaround: If the characters '&', '<' and '>' are found in your XML configuration file, and if these characters are not inside an XML tag, replacing them with '&amp', '&lt' and '&gt' respectively works around the issue.

Summary: Using the Value property of the xPCParameter class from the xPC Target .NET API framework to set a 0 (zero) value is not reflected on the target PC
Description: Using the Value  property of the xPCParameter class from the xPC Target .NET API framework to set a 0 (zero) value is not reflected on the target PC. A C# code syntax like the following might cause the problem: ... tgpc.Connect(); paramObj = tgpc.Application.Parameters["BlockPath","ParameterName"]; double[] ptArray = new ptArray[1]; ptArray[0] = 0;paramObj.Value = ptArray; ...
Workaround: As a workaround, use the SetParam method to tune the parameter of the xPCParameter type.... tgpc.Connect(); paramObj = tgpc.Application.Parameters["BlockPath","ParameterName"]; double[] ptArray = new ptArray[1]; ptArray[0] = 0;paramObj.SetParam(ptArray); ...

Summary: Polyspace Graphical Interface (Polyspace Verification Environment) does not delete all temporary files
Description: When you use the Polyspace interface to review results containing many MACROS, some temporary files required may not be deleted. This occurs with files that have a '.ci ' extension which are put in a temporary sub-folder pointed to by %TEMP%  environement variable. These temporary files are put in %TEMP%\PolySpace  sub-folder. This temporary sub-folder, over the time, can grow and its size can become very big.
Workaround: Periodically delete the folder %TEMP%\PolySpace, when the Polyspace graphical interface is closed.

Summary: Simulink HDL Coder can generate incorrect results for a divide operation in a MATLAB Function block
Description: HDL code generated for a Simulink model can produce division results that do not match the original model, under the following conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block contains a divide operator with  rounding mode set to  'nearest' . The denominator of the divide operator is a constant declared inside the MATLAB function.
Workaround: To work around this problem, edit the global fimath specification for the MATLAB Function block in Model Explorer. Change the rounding mode specification to 'RoundMode', 'Fix'.Alternatively, you can set the rounding mode explicitly to 'Fix' on either the numerator or denominator of the division, while using any supported rounding mode in the global fimath specification. For example, to set the rounding mode on the numerator:fm = fimath('RoundMode', 'Fix');x = fi(a, fm) / b;

Summary: Code generator might produce incorrect code for referenced model containing reusable system?
Description: Incorrect code might occur under all of the following conditions: Referenced model has a vector or matrix input. The referenced model immediately selects the first element of this input signal, by using a Selector or Demux block. The selected signal is routed to an inner subsystem with no intervening operations.  The generated code might be missing a subscript for the selected model reference argument. To see the error, build the attached model and open childModel.c . You can see the error in the first call to childModel_pickA : Incorrect: childModel_pickA(&rtu_dataArray, &(*rty_out)); Correct: childModel_pickA(&rtu_dataArray[1], &(*rty_out)); When the target language is C++, some compilers might error during compilation with an error message, which explains the incompatibility between the actual and formal arguments. For example, the Microsoft C++ compiler might produce the following error message: cannot convert parameter 2 from 'const real32_T *[]' to 'const real32_T *'Types pointed to are unrelated; conversion requires reinterpret_cast, C-style cast or function-style cast
Workaround: As a workaround, you can do one of the following:Specify the inner reusable subsystem as Inlined.Add a non-virtual block before or after the Selector block.

Summary: Navigating certain XML files in the Current Folder browser is extremely slow
Description: If XML files that reference external entities are in your current folder, then navigating between files in the Current Folder browser is extremely slow. The Current Folder browser appears to be unresponsive for several seconds while it is parsing the XML files. This problem exists in MATLAB? R2010b (7.11) only.
Workaround: Installation instructions for R2010b only       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux? systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store it in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems?: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                     Linux/Macintosh? Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: eig may return nonorthogonal eigenvectors for certain real symmetric matrices.
Description: If you call eig  with two outputs: [V,D] = eig(A)  for certain real symmetric matrices A, the eigenvectors V are not orthogonal. In those cases, A*V is not equal to V*D.

Summary: MATLAB stops responding to user input after you copy text, and then open a Simulink UI such as Library Browser, Model Explorer, or some block dialogs
Description: After copying text from a Simulink element (such as a block, signal label, or annotation), opening some UI windows or dialogs will cause MATLAB to stop responding. This occurs on all Windows platforms.

Summary: Using slbuild to build a model reference SIM or RTW target may error when 'Model block version mismatch' or 'Port and parameter mismatch' is set to warning
Description: The slbuild  function may fail when building a model reference SIM or RTW target for a model that has the Model block version mismatch  or the Port and parameter mismatch  diagnostic set to warning . If the build fails, Simulink displays an error that says that the model is unsaved.
Workaround: If you have you are getting a Model block version mismatch or Port and parameter mismatch warning because of changes made to a model changedModel that is referenced, either directly or via one or more library links, by a Model block in another model referencingModel that has Model block version mismatch or Port and parameter mismatch set to warning, then you can use either of these workarounds:In the Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Model Referencing pane of referencingModel, set Model block version mismatch and Port and parameter mismatch to none.Before you call slbuild on referencingModel, you will need to refresh the Model blocks in referencingModel and in the libraries used by referencingModel that have Model blocks that reference changedModel and then save referencingModel and the libraries. You can refresh the Model blocks with the following command sequence:model_obj = get_param(modelOrLibraryName, 'Object');model_obj.refreshModelBlocks;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bitwise OR and bitwise XOR return wrong values for Fixed Point inputs with non-zero bias
Description: This bug might occur when a bitwise OR or bitwise XOR is applied to a fixed point value with a non-zero bias. A code generation optimization which simplifies 0 | X  to X  is incorrectly applied when the real-world value of the constant is zero, instead of when the stored integer value is zero. For example, if the type of the constant is sfix19_Sp3_Bp3  (scaling is 0.3 and bias is 0.3) and the stored integer is -1, then the real-world value is 0, which causes the code generator to incorrectly apply the optimization.
Workaround: Use a Data Type Conversion block to convert the operands of the bitwise operator to simple integer types.

Summary: sldvmakeharness and slvnvmakeharness do not update correctly for models that are configured to load input data from a workspace before the simulation begins.
Description: If a model is configured to load input data from a workspace before the simulation begins, and you run the sldvmakeharness  or slvnvmakeharness  function to generate a harness model for that model, the generated harness model doesn't update successfully.
Workaround: Before you run sldvmakeharness or slvnvmakeharness to generate a harness model, make sure to disable loading input data from a workspace before the simulation begins.

Summary: Bus-capable block that uses a MATLAB structure for Initial Value or Initial Condition parameters and that is in a referenced model in Accelerator mode can cause a build error
Description: Updating a top-level model can result in a build error when all  of these conditions apply: The top-level model references a model that is in Accelerator mode. The referenced model contains a bus-capable block (such as a Memory or Merge block) that uses a MATLAB structure for its Initial Value  or Initial Condition  parameter.  ?
Workaround: If the specified MATLAB structure does not have the exact same structure and attributes as that of the incoming bus (that is, the structure is a partial structure), consider using the Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct command to create a matching full structure. Then use that generated full structure for the bus-capable block parameter.

Summary: Incorrect ASAP2 description generated for a lookup table block in a masked reusable subsystem
Description: The ASAP2 file generation process might generate an incorrect description of a lookup table block (Lookup n-D, Interpolation Using Prelookup, or Prelookup) if both of the following conditions are true: The lookup table block is located within a reusable subsystem. The table or breakpoints data for the lookup table block is a masked parameter of the reusable subsystem.  For example, if a reusable subsystem contains a 2D lookup table, and the table or breakpoints data for the lookup table is a masked parameter of the reusable subsystem, the generated ASAP2 file might incorrectly describe the lookup table as 1D CHARACTERISTIC (CURVE) .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generation of literal constant as unsigned
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might generate an unsigned constant in a expression, that is expected to be evaluated using a signed type. The unsigned constant forces the expression to evaluate using an unsigned data type, which causes a simulation and code generation behavior mismatch. You can detect this failure condition using Polyspace or any MISRA compliance checker.
Workaround: You can use the following command to disable unsigned constants emission for constants of type unsigned char and unsigned short:>> slfeature('CGForceUnsignedConsts', 0)

Summary: Edit-Time requests for signal hierarchy information followed by a model update may lead to a segmentation violation.
Description: At edit-time, users can request signal hierarchy information by executing any one of the following tasks: Open dialog for Bus Creator/Selector/Assignment blocks Executing get_param(<portHandle>, 'SignalHierarchy')   If any one of these actions precedes a model update, then Simulink might trigger a segmentation violation.
Workaround: There are two potential work arounds:Update the model before performing any of the edit-time tasks.Copy the attached file, attachment_684927_20110412.zip, in a temp dir and unzip it.Execute attachment_684927_10b.p before opening your model.Note that you can place a call to this P-file in your startup.m file, however; this may lead to longer MATLAB initialization times since this call will load Simulink as well.

Summary: Using H5D.write to write HDF5 compound dataset with incorrect MATLAB datatype can cause segfault
Description: Supplying an incorrectly composed structure to H5D.write  for an HDF5 dataset with a compound datatype could cause MATLAB to segfault.
