Summary: MissingResourceException regarding "accessibleRole.titlebar" on the Macintosh
Description: Depending on your Macintosh's accessibility configuration (for example, if you change your "Speakable Items" setting under Speech Recognition), you may experience the following Java exception in the Command Window:java.util.MissingResourceException: Can't find resource for bundle java.util.PropertyResourceBundle, key accessibleRole.titlebar at java.util.ResourceBundle.getObject(ResourceBundle.java:374) at java.util.ResourceBundle.getString(ResourceBundle.java:334)...

Summary: Using the 'gige' adaptor of the Image Acquisition Toolbox, Dalsa cameras could cause MATLAB to crash if set to an invalid PacketSize of 16128.
Description: Some Dalsa cameras do not support a PacketSize of 16128 bytes. However, they do not report an error and due to a bug in the 'gige' adaptor, MATLAB may crash.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: In Target Preferences Block, setting Board to BeagleBoard DSP or OMAPL138/C6748 EVM can generate error message
Description: In the Target Preferences block, setting Board  to BeagleBoard DSP  or OMAPL138/C6748 EVM  can generate the following error message: Error due to multiple causes.--> Error evaluating 'OpenFcn' callback of Target Preferences block (mask) 'untitled/Target Preferences;.Index exceeds matrix dimensions.--> Index exceeds matrix dimensions. The Target Preferences block imports the processor definition files from the Texas Instruments DSP/BIOS installation folder. During the import, it locates the DSP/BIOS installation directory through the environment variable 'BIOS_INSTALL_DIR'. If this environment variable is not set or points to a DSP/BIOS installation where, for example OMAPL138 processor information is not available, the error message shown above is thrown.
Workaround: The patch file, patch_684389.zip, applies to release R2010b.1. To install the patch, download the attached files, attachment_684389_20110316.zip and patch_684389.zip.2. Extract patch_installer.m from attachment_684389_20110316.zip.3. In MATLAB, set Current Folder to the location of patch_installer.m and patch_684389.zip. (e.g., "C:\Users\username\Downloads")4. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt:  patch_installer('patch_684389.zip', 'install')5. Complete the installation by following the instructions on the MATLAB command line.6. Set the DSP/BIOS installation folder:For CCS v3.3, enter the following command:  setDspbiosFolder('(path to DSP/BIOS)\bios_5_33_06'); % If using CCS 3.3For CCS v4.1, also specify the XDC installation folder:  setDspbiosFolder('(path to TI tools)\bios_5_41_02_14', '(path to TI tools)\xdctools_3_10_05_61')7. Import all processor / board information from DSP/BIOS:  importDspbiosConfig('board', 'all')  importDspbiosConfig('proc', 'all')

Summary: USRP2 Receiver and USRP2 Transmitter Simulink blocks do not support variables in mask parameters.
Description: The USRP2 Receiver and USRP2 Transmitter blocks in the commsdrhw Simulink library do not support user variables as mask parameters.
Workaround: Populate the mask parameters of these blocks with specific numeric values.

Summary: Compiling a Standalone application using the -R -nodisplay option on Linux produces unexpected NO DISPLAY messages.
Description: Compiling a Standalone application using the -R -nodisplay  option on Linux produces unexpected NO DISPLAY  messages. These messages are printed to stdout  for console applications.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: imshow produces misleading error message when no display available
Description: When you start MATLAB using -nodisplay (Unix) or -noFigureWindows (Windows) startup options, calling imshow  results in the following misleading error message: ??? Error using ==> imshow at 191. IMSHOW requires Java to run.  This is a misleading error message because it implies that the MATLAB session does not have Java available. The following error message should be produced in this case: ??? Error using ==> imshow at 191. IMSHOW unable to display image.

Summary: When following a remote verification from the Polyspace Verification Environment, the software displays the message 'Remote verification monitoring is stopped due to psqueue-progress command failure'
Description: When launching a verification to the server and following the remote verification from the Polyspace Verification Environment, the software displays the message 'Remote verification monitoring is stopped due to psqueue-progress command failure with the following error: Error: An I/O error occured (Function ReceiveResults: BinIo.getc). ' This message happens in a sporadic way and stops the monitoring of the verification on the client side. The same message can also occur when there is a communication loss between the Polyspace client and Polyspace server machine. Note that on the server side, the verification continues to run.
Workaround: Installation instructions (release R2010b only)Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\PolySpace or in /usr/local/PolySpace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. PolySpace_Common and PolySpaceFor<lang>_R20xx are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Exit any Polyspace Graphical Intferface and stop Polyspace queue manager service.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Restart Polyspace queue manager service.This worakround has to be installed on each CPU including Queue Manager. It can also be applied to client side (optional).Attached zip files include correction of bug report https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/664760

Summary: Saturation block does not saturate remaining fixed-point matrix elements when one input is within threshold
Description: When the following conditions are all true, a Saturation block does not saturate some input elements during simulation: The data type of the input or output is of fixed-point type. The input is a matrix. One element of the matrix input is within the lower and upper limit of the Saturation block.  The input elements that follow the in-threshold element are not saturated.

Summary: Indexing into scalar with variable-size indices causes assertion failure during code generation.
Description: The problem occurs when this code pattern is used: x(I,J) = s; under the following conditions: x  and s  must be scalars (size 1x1). One or both of I  and J  must be variable sized. There must be at least two subscripts. The problem does not occur for assignments of the form x(I) = s .  The following internal error message is generated during code generation: Assertion failed numIndices == aMatrixType->numDimensions() at line 782 of file "dom_core/utility/cg_sanity_checker.cpp".
Workaround: Rewrite the assignment as a loop:for i = 1:numel(I)  for j = 1:numel(J)    x(I(i),J(j)) = s;  endend

Summary: Model with machine-parented data and only linked Stateflow charts produces TLC error during code generation
Description: Suppose that a model has machine-parented data and contains only linked Stateflow charts that belong to other library models (that is, the model does not contain any non-linked Stateflow charts). Generating code for the main model results in a TLC error with the following symptom: Error: File: ...\matlab\stateflow\c\tlc\sflib.tlc Line: 403 Column: 29Unable to find StateflowMachineVarGroupVarIdx within the VarGroups scope
Workaround: Delete the machine-parented data in the main model. Because the main model does not have any non-linked Stateflow charts, this data object is redundant. Note that machine-parented data from a main model cannot be accessed by linked charts.

Summary: Code generation results differ from simulation results when Probe block input is a variable-size signal with size or dimensions greater than range of selected output data type
Description: When a variable-size signal is input to a Probe block, there can be a difference between simulation and code-generation results for the "Probe width" and "Probe signal dimensions" outputs. Such a difference is due to an input width or input signal dimension being large enough to cause an overflow. In particular, this bug occurs when both the following conditions are true:  The input to a Probe block is a variable-size signal. The input signal width cannot be represented within the range of the data type selected for the width output, or the input signal dimension cannot be represented within the range of the data type selected for the signal dimension output.
Workaround: This bug occurs due to a difference in overflow and rounding modes between simulation and generated code for the Probe block. It manifests itself  for variable-size signal inputs. To detect whether this bug occurs in your model, extract and run the attached detection script as follows:detectProbeOverflowRoundingBug('modelname') The script returns 'true' if the bug manifests itself in your model. To work around this issue, change the selected data type for width and signal dimensions to 'double' and insert 'Data Type Conversion' blocks at the output with the following parameters (use default values for unspecified parameters): Output data type: [Your desired type]Integer rounding mode: NearestSaturate on integer overflow: on

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for complex conjugate operations with fixed-point data types and saturation may be incorrect
Description: Saturation on a conjugate operation may be applied incorrectly in the generated code. This bug occurs if all the following conditions are met: The code contains certain conjugate operations. For example, code that results from using a Math Function block in Hermetian mode. The conjugate uses fixed-point data types and saturation. The block input signal is reused as an input to additional blocks.   ?
Workaround: You might be able to prevent this issue by either disabling saturation or by not splitting the signal, that is, not reusing the block output.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulating an enable subsystem that contains Variable Transport Delay block(s) and has StatesWhenEnabling set to reset, can generate incorrect code.
Description: If an enable subsystem contains Variable Transport Delay block(s) and also has StatesWhenEnabling set to reset, simulating the subsystem can generate incorrect code. This is because the TLC file of the Variable Transoort Delay block may not have the initialization method required for proper code generation.
Workaround: ?For Simulink releases from R2006a to R2010b, insert this into the following code in the TLC file:  matlab\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\vtdelay.tlc, after the end of FcnVTDelayStart function. %% Function: FcnVTInitializeCondition =======================================%% Abstract:%% For the variable transport delay block, initialize the states%function InitializeConditions(block, system) Output %if (ParamSettings.Transportdelay == "yes" )  %assign ncStates = ContStates[0] %assign rollRegions = [0:%<ncStates-1>] %assign rollVars = ["Xc"] %roll sigIdx = rollRegions, lcv = RollThreshold, block, "Roller", rollVars %<LibBlockContinuousState("", lcv, sigIdx)> = 0.0; %endroll %else %% For variable time delay, no state, do nothing  %endif%endfunction %% InitializeConditions

Summary: Simulink crashes when a model containing Simscape block in a subsystem is opened
Description: When a block in a custom Simulink library has a subsystem containing Simscape blocks, or blocks from add-on products, and the number of ports in this subsystem changes, opening a model containing that Simulink library block can cause Simulink to crash.

Summary: n-D Lookup Table block with evenly spaced search might return different results in code generation and simulation
Description: Suppose that Inline parameters  is selected on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. When breakpoints are power-of-2, evenly spaced data, the n-D Lookup Table block might give different results in code generation and simulation if the following are true: Index search method  is set to Evenly spaced points . Fraction data type  is set to Inherit via internal rule .
Workaround: One workaround is to set the fraction data type to something other than Inherit via internal rule.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while creating code generation report
Description: MATLAB might crash while creating a code generation report under all of the following conditions: Code is generated from an empty subsystem In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation (formerly Real-Time Workshop ) > Report  pane, either Model-to-code  or Code-to-model  is selected.

Summary: esig.history requires end date to be current date
Description: When specifying a date range with esig.history , the end date must be the current date. If the end date is not the current date, data for an incorrect date range will be returned.
Workaround: To get expected data results, specify the end date as the current date and filter out the desired data after the data is returned.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Merge block with constant input might generate incorrect code
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a Merge block if all of the following conditions exist: Merge block has one or more constant inputs and at least one non-constant input One of the constant inputs is inside an atomic subsystem Atomic subsystem has a constant sample time  To identify signals and subsystems that have a constant sample time, in the model window, select Format > Sample Time Display  > Constant . This setting causes constant signals to appear magenta.
Workaround: Change the sample time of the atomic subsystem to match the output sample time of the Merge block.

Summary: PolySpace verification fails during compilation phase with the error message: "Error: unable to fork/spawn"
Description: This behavior occurs most frequently at the end of compilation phase on verifications that include thousands of files. Message is the following: System error Invalid argument (inval: Invalid argument)Error: unable to fork/spawn  \\...\verifier\bin\x86-mingw32\mergesuif.x86-mingw32.exe (errno=22).  Please check your memory and swap amount.

Summary: SEGV when displaying .NET generic object while Profiler is on
Description: Displaying an instance of a .NET generic while the MATLAB Profiler is running might cause a SEGV. The following example demonstrates the steps which can cause MATLAB to crash:      % Start profiling  profile on  % define generic key-value pair type, key - Int32, value - String  kvpType = NET.GenericClass('System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair',...                              'System.Int32', 'System.String');  % Create an instastance of a generic List of key-value pairs    kvpList = NET.createGeneric('System.Collections.Generic.List',...                              { kvpType }, 10);  % Display the object  kvpList   % This might crash

Summary: R2010b changes to function geoshow cause unintended incompatibilities
Description: Two unintended incompatibilities are caused by changes in function geoshow  in R2010b: If you create a surface by calling function geoshow  with the 'DisplayType'  parameter set to 'surface ' and you do not set the 'CData ' property in the function call but instead set the 'CData ' property by using the handle to the surface, then in some cases, warnings are issued and the plot displays a blank surface. If you have a regular data grid that wraps around the world and you create a surface with that data grid by calling function geoshow  with the following parameters set, then a warning is issued and there may be a small strip on the surface in which the alpha data has not been applied:'DisplayType'  set to 'texturemap ', 'AlphaData ' set to an M-by-N array, and 'FaceAlpha ' set to 'texturemap '
Workaround: To work around this problem:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Download the attached file, workaround_683496-10b_2010-11-09.zipOn Windows double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux use the unzip command.Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txtThe updates provided in this workaround are only for release R2010b.

Summary: Code generation failure when Utility Code Generation is set to Shared Location
Description: If both the following conditions are met, code generation may fail when you use the ert.tlc  target. You are generating code for a referenced model or a model with the Utility Code Generation  field in the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Code Placement  pane set to Shared Location . The Source file (*.c) template  field  in the Configuration Parameters > Templates Real-Time Workshop  pane is set to code_c_template.cgt .

Summary: Error when connecting to eSignal datafeed interface
Description: Connecting to the eSignal datafeed interface through the Datafeed Toolbox produces the following error: E = esig('username) ??? Undefined variable "IESignal" or class "IESignal.HooksClass".   Error in ==> esig.esig>esig.esig at 33 e.eSignal = IESignal.HooksClass;
Workaround: To work around the issue:1. Visit the eSignal Desktop API and download the .NET example DesktopAPI_TimeAndSales.zip2. Extract the zip file to a convenient location and add the assembly Interop.IESignal.dll to MATLAB by invoking the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:NET.addAssembly('Interop.IESignal.dll');Note that the absolute path to the DLL must be specified.

Summary: Code generation can fail when Table Lookup block refers to tunable structure parameter
Description: When a Table Lookup block refers to an element of a tunable parameter structure, code generation can fail due to an assertion similar to the following: Code generation assertion 'var->type()->scope() == fScope || var->type()->equals(fVar2Type[var])' failed in 'rtwcg_utils/RTWCGMoveGlobals.cpp:306'[  0] 0x00007ff789dd0ad9 /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwfl.so+00539353 fl::sysdep::linux::unwind_stack(void const**, unsigned long, unsigned long)+000009[  1] 0x00007ff789d7961b /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwfl.so+00181787 fl::diag::stacktrace_base::capture(unsigned long)+000027[  2] 0x00007ff74b184fe8 /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwrtwcg.so+00786408 rtwcg_assertion_failed(char const*, int, char const*)+000120[  3] 0x00007ff74b151923 /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwrtwcg.so+00575779[  4] 0x00007ff74b23d76a /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwrtwcg.so+01541994 RTWCG::RTWFcnConst::CommonTransformsToOptimizeAndEmitCode(RTWCG::TransformDispatcher&, PtrSet*, char const*)+008698 Alternatively, a Target Language Compiler (TLC) error, similar to the following, might result: Error: File: /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/sysarglib.tlc Line: 16 Column: 45Unable to find CanonicalPrmArgDef within the Interface scopeMain program:==> [00] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/sysarglib.tlc:LibAccessCanPrmArg(16)    [01] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:FcnGetCanParmArg(127)    [02] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:FcnBlockParameterAddr(1097)    [03] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:FcnBlockParameterBaseAddr(1231)    [04] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:LibBlockParameterBaseAddr(1255)

Summary: Simulating a model in simplified initialization mode may lead to a SEGV if the model contains a function-call subsystem driven by a conditionally executed subsystem
Description: Simulating a model in simplified initialization mode causes a SEGV if all  of the following conditions apply: The Configuration Parameters  -> Diagnostics -> Data Validity -> Underspecified initialization detection  parameter is set to Simplified . The model contains a function-call subsystem whose function-call trigger port is driven by an output of another conditionally executed subsystem. The Outport block corresponding to the driving output port of the above conditionally executed subsystem is unconnected or is connected to a Ground block.

Summary: Point-Curve block in SimMechanics models causing incorrect simulation results
Description: The Point-Curve block has a bug that causes models using the block to simulate incorrectly.
Workaround: The following workaround is valid for R2010b (SimMechanics 3.2.1). For other releases, contact Technical Support.Close all MATLAB sessions.Rename the file $matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/mech_engine.MEXEXT to mech_engine.MEXEXT.old. ($matlabroot is the directory returned by the matlabroot command.)Depending on your platform, substitute:MEXEXT = mexw32 for 32-bit WindowsMEXEXT = mexw64 for 64-bit WindowsMEXEXT = mexa64 for 64-bit LinuxDownload the attached mech_engine.MEXEXT file and place it in the $matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Memory reduction fails with TRAINBR
Description: The training function trainbr  generates an error when used with memory reduction. For example, the following code generates an error: [x,t] = house_dataset;net = feedforwardnet;net.trainFcn = 'trainbr';net.efficiency.memoryReduction = 10;net = train(net,x,t); ? ?
Workaround: To work around this problem, place the files from the zip attachment into the current working directory.

Summary: A SegV occurs when you load a model containing data store memory blocks that was created using save-as
Description: A SegV may oocur after you use the Save As  command to save a model containing data store memory blocks in an earlier version of Simulink, specifically versions R13 through R2007b, while you attempt to load the model in that earlier version.

Summary: HDL Cosimulation block generated by EDA Cosimulation Assistant crashes MATLAB sporadically at start of cosimulation.
Description: In the last step of the EDA Cosimulation Assistant workflow for Simulink, the option ?Automatically determine timescale at start of simulation? is enabled by default. If you do not change this option, a "Timescale Details" dialog appears when you perform simulation with the Simulink model that contains the automatically generated HDL Cosimulation block. For some models, MATLAB crashes before the dialog appears and generates a segmentation violation report.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, un-check the option "Automatically determine timescale at start of simulation" in the last step of the EDA Cosimulation Assistant workflow for Simulink. To use the automatic timescale calculation feature, configure the automatically generated HDL Cosimulation block in your model according to these instructions:1. Make sure that the HDL simulator is up and ready for simulation.2. Open the mask of the generated HDL cosimulation block, click the "Timescales" tab, and then click "Determine Timescale Now". This action launches the "Timescale Details" dialog. Make any changes, if necessary, and click OK.3. Continue cosimulation.If you would like to disable the ?Automatically determine timescale at start of simulation? option in the EDA Cosimulation Assistant, use the attachment.Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                     Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: n-D Lookup Table block might return different answers in simulation and code generation when input data type and corresponding breakpoint data type have different scaling
Description: When the input data type has different scaling from the corresponding breakpoint data type for an n-D Lookup table block, code generation results might differ from simulation results.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, set the breakpoint data type to Inherit: Same as corresponding input on the Data Types tab for the n-D Lookup Table block.

Description: H5D.read will segfault when given an "empty" memory type identifier for the purposes of reading a compound or enumerated dataset.
