Summary: Verification stops in intermediate phase, with the message: "@FFT: Misaligned offset (second operand) in pointer arithmetics"
Description: Verification stops in in intermediate phase, with the following error message: Myfoo is dead code**** C to intermediate language translation - 3 (P_SIA)----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: Misaligned offset (second operand) in pointer arithmetics in foo P._init_globals_0_17instr : 56809: e1: add t:g46 (pointer.32) e3, e4| 56810: e3: cvt t:g46 (pointer.32) e5| 56811: e5: add t:g2955 (pointer.32) e6, e7| ["fields" | "myfied"]|...An internal consistency problem has been detected.|...  The reason for this error is specific to the code and could be due to option '-no-fold'
Workaround: If you used the option '-no-fold', relaunch your verification without it. If you did not use the option, please contact technical support, and send your code if possible, as we may provide a workaround.

Summary: Slow performance when using main generator
Description: When you use the main generator for code that contains many packages with numerous constants and variables, the verification may take a long time to complete.
Workaround: Installation instructions (for R2010a)Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. For Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default, the location is c:\PolySpace or /usr/local/PolySpace on Linux systems. This parent folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. PolySpace_Common and PolySpaceFor<lang>_R20xx are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Exit any Polyspace Graphical Interface and stop running Polyspace Queue Manager service.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report, and store the file in <POLYSPACE>.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: On Windows Systems, open the .zip file and look at the contents.On Linux Systems, type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. On Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.On Linux Systems, type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Restart Polyspace Queue Manager service.

Summary: Bus Selector block does not find element of bus that is in an array of buses if signal name mismatch occurs
Description: At compile time, if a Bus Selector block attempts to select an element of a nonvirtual bus that originates from a Selector block as part of an array of buses, the Bus Selector block cannot find bus elements that do not match the signal names of the bus object used with the nonvirtual bus.
Workaround: 1. Set the following diagnostic to error: Configuration Parameters > Diagnostic > Connectivity > Element name mismatch 2. Update the model and resolve the reported error. 3. You may revert the setting in step 1 to the original value if you wish

Summary: Some FLIR cameras may not be usable with the GigE Vision adaptor.
Description: During the creation of a VIDEOINPUT object, some FLIR cameras will not be found. Alternatively, the VIDEOINPUT object may fail to acquire properly.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: On Windows the Simulink Editor may become unresponsive when trying to drag a signal line in a locked library
Description: If you attempt to drag the signal arrow head in a locked library, a dialog pops up asking to "unlock" or "cancel." The dialog and the Simulink Editor may then become unresponsive.
Workaround: A binary fix is required. Here are the installation instructions:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file.Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Corrected text for Configuring GigE Vision Devices documentation
Description: The following documentation topic had an incorrect text: Image Acquisition Toolbox Users Guide > Configuring GigE Vision Devices > Software Configuration > Linux Examples Correct text for the entire section: GENICAM_ROOT_V2_0=/opt/GenICam_2_0_1 GENICAM_CACHE_V2_0=~/.GenICam/xml/cache GENICAM_LOG_CONFIG_V2_0=/opt/GenICam_2_0_1/log/config-unix/DebugLogging.properties Note that you may need to create the .GenICam/xml/cache directory in your home directory. Also, this example assumes that you have un-tarred the files in the /opt/GenICam_2_0_1 directory. LD_LIBRARY_PATH must contain: /opt/GenICam_2_0_1/bin/Linux32_i86 or /opt/GenICam_2_0_1/bin/Linux64_x64 (depending on your architecture) Ensure that the cache directory in your GENICAM_CACHE_V2_0 environment variable exists for GenICam to be able to use it. You can test the installation by using the following command: imaqhwinfo('gige') and by looking at the relevant sections when you run the imaqsupport function. For example, you should see something similar to the following: --------------------GENICAM-------------------- GENICAM_CACHE_V2_0 = /home/jsmith/.GenICam/xml/cache /home/jsmith/.GenICam/xml/cache present. GENICAM_LOG_CONFIG_V2_0 = /opt/GenICam-2.0.1/log/config-unix/DebugLogging.properties /opt/GenICam-2.0.1/log/config-unix/DebugLogging.properties present. GENICAM_ROOT_V2_0 = /opt/GenICam-2.0.1 /opt/GenICam-2.0.1 present. MWIMAQ_GENICAM_LOCAL_XML = ?

Summary: Subscripted assignment from extrinsic function might cause build error in Embedded MATLAB
Description: You might get a build error when generating C code from a MATLAB function that contains an assignment with all of the following elements: Left-hand side is a subscript expression Size of the subscript index can vary at runtime Right-hand side is the return value from an extrinsic function  This bug can occur during code generation using the emlc command in Real-Time Workshop and the Embedded MATLAB Function Block in Simulink. For example, the last line in the following code might cause a build error: ... eml.extrinsic('myfcn'); buffer = zeros(1,100); % Next line might cause build error buffer(1:n) = myfcn();...
Workaround: Save the return value of the extrinsic function to a pre-initialized temporary variable before storing it to the final destination. For example:...eml.extrinsic('myfcn');buffer = zeros(1,100);% Pre-initialize temporary variable with desired class and sizetemp = zeros(1, n);% Next line converts return value of extrinsic function% to vector of doublestemp = myfcn();% Now right-hand side is a known class and sizebuffer(1:n) = temp;...

Summary: MATLAB fails during export of AUTOSAR runnable from function-call subsystem
Description: When exporting an AUTOSAR runnable from a function-call subsystem, MATLAB will fail if the Function packaging  field in the Block Parameters dialog box is set to Reusable function .
Workaround: When you export an AUTOSAR runnable from a function-call subsystem, ensure that the Function packaging field in the Block Parameters dialog box is not set to Reusable function.

Summary: Bus objects with deeply nested hierarchies may cause block dialogs to become unresponsive
Description: If you have a block that contains a bus object with a deeply nested hierarchy, the block dialog for this block may become unresponsive when you open the dialog or apply changes to a field related to the bus object. You may observe that for a deeply nested hierarchy, the dialog becomes progressively more unresponsive after you apply several changes to a field related to the bus object.
Workaround: Remember: Use a workaround only if Simulink starts becoming progressively more unresponsive after you apply several changes to a field related to the bus object. Note: There is no workaround if the block dialog becomes unresponsive when you open it.If the dialog box becomes unresponsive when you are applying changes to a field in the dialog, you can use one of two possible workarounds:Save and close the model after applying each change. Then, reopen the model to make further changes.After each change, update the model by selecting Edit -> Update Diagram (Ctrl-D) in the Model Editor.

Summary: Unbalanced stator currents in Asynchronous Machine block when the Rotor type is set to Double squirrel-cage and the Reference frame parameter is set to Rotor
Description: If you have an Asynchronous Machine block that is connected to a balanced three-phase circuit, and the Rotor type  parameter of the block is set to Double squirrel-cage  while the Reference frame  parameter is set to Rotor , the Asynchronous Machine block simulates unbalanced stator current and then provides incorrect simulation results of your model. The unbalanced stator currents can be observed by connecting a Scope block to the measurement output of the block and monitoring the stator currents signals with the use of a Bus Selector block.
Workaround: Set the Reference frame parameter to Stationary to get the correct simulation results.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while including custom source files in code generation report
Description: MATLAB might crash when the code generation report attempts to include custom source files, which are not found.

Summary: The "View Log file" process does not end when you close the spooler log file.
Description: On a Windows system, when you use the "View log file" command in the spooler, the software launches the process "follow-progress.exe" to view the log. When you close the log file window,the process is sometimes not killed. The same problem can occur in the PVE when you a remote verification.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Setting the xPC Target parameter Allow tasks to execute concurrently persists even after you set the parameter System target file to another target file (for example, grt.tlc).
Description: Setting the xPC Target parameter Allow tasks to execute concurrently  enables the generated code to handle data transfers as required by a concurrent tasking environment. If you set this parameter and then switch the system target file to one for a target that does not support concurrent execution, the setting remains in effect, although not visible in the Configuration Parameters dialog box. The code generator then produces Rate Transition Block code for a concurrent execution environment, even though the code is incorrect for the current target.
Workaround: Deselect the xPC Target parameter Allow tasks to execute concurrently before switching the parameter System target file from xpctarget.tlc (or xptargetert.tlc) to another target file.

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation fails with a link error for a model containing an S-Function block for which additional S-function modules are specified (LCC compiler only)
Description: Accelerator mode simulation of a model fails with a link error if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains an S-Function block. Additional S-function modules are specified using the S-Function block parameter S-function modules  (SFunctionModules ). You are using the LCC compiler for model builds.  The link error occurs after you initiate the model simulation, during the build of the Accelerator mode simulation target.
Workaround: To work around this bug, download the attached .zip file and extract its contents to your MATLAB installation tree, using the following steps:On your system, log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation root folder by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the .zip file attached to this Bug Report and place the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Examine the contents of the .zip file and, for each file, rename or make a backup copy of the equivalent file in your MATLAB installation tree. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/attachment_679394_10b_win32_2010-11-02.zipExtract the files from the .zip file. Windows systems:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the .zip file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip attachment_679394_10b_win32_2010-11-02.zipDelete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: When downloading results from the PolySpace Metrics web server, the software displays the message 'Failure when writing report data'
Description: When downloading an analysis from the Polyspace Metrics web server, the software displays the following error message: Failure when writing report dataerror: the daemon is not running on the server localhostWebserver: localhostURL : /PolyspaceWebUI?mfunc=WriteReportData&Version=2.0 ....  This happens when the Polyspace Metrics web server was not started with the default port number (8080). ?
Workaround: To work around this issue:Stop the Queue Manager service.Change the web server port number to 8080.Restart the Queue Manager service.

Summary: Incorrect error handling during PIL block simulations that use exported functions
Description: If you run PIL block simulations that use exported functions, then errors that occur during these simulations lead to the following error: ??? Caused by: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "slOneSimStep". Please report this to The MathWorks if you can cause it to recur When this error occurs, you must restart MATLAB. This bug affects export functions configured for the ert.tlc and autosar.tlc system target files. The following are possible reasons for the error: A communications failure during PIL simulation An error in the PIL component under test Bug report ID 676211  ? ?

Summary: Generated software-in-the-loop block may have incorrect code
Description: You might get incorrect code when generating an software-in-the-loop block for a subsystem, if all of the following are true: The subsystem input or output has fixed-point data with a length larger than 64-bit The fixed-point data is a complex scalar  ?

Summary: PerturbationForJacobian parameter values set for input ports of subsytems in block libraries are ignored
Description: When you use a subsystem from a block library, values you set for the PerturbationForJacobian  parameter of any input port in the subsystem are ignored.   Instead, PerturbationForJacobian  parameter values for all subsystem input ports revert to the default value of 1e-5 in the model. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue:Mask the subsystem by right-clicking the subsystem in the library and selecting Mask Subsystem. This action opens the Mask Editor.In the Mask Editor, select the Initialization tab.In the Initialization commands area, enter commands to set the PerturbationForJacobian parameter values for the desired input ports. For example, the following commands set PerturbationForJacobian to zero for the third input port of the block called "UnperturbedBlock" in the subsystem: ph = get_param([gcb '/UnperturbedBlock'],'PortHandles');set_param(ph.Inport(3),'PerturbationForJacobian','0');Check the Allow library block to modify its contents check box.To verify that the mask initialization code runs and sets the parameter value, update the model by selecting the menu item "Edit->Update Diagram". Note that Simulink runs mask initialization code automatically at model loading and at the start of a simulation.

Summary: The data type assistant on block dialogs does not work when the block name contains a forward slash
Description: When the name of a block contains a forward slash '/', the data type assistant for the data type parameter(s) (if any exist) on the block dialog does not work. Specifically, in either the "Binary point" or "Slope and bias" scaling mode, when the "Calculate Best-Precision Scaling" button is clicked, an error incorrectly appears even when valid information for the calculation has been provided. In addition, the "Fixed-point details" panel incorrectly shows that the values of relevant fields cannot be evaluated.
Workaround: Rename the block to a different name that does not contain a forward slash.

Summary: Wrong source files added to project when programming flash memory for F2802x Piccolo devices
Description: When using the flash programmer as part of code generation for 2802x Piccolo devices, the wrong source files for the specified device family are added to the projects that perform flash erase, flash program, and flash verify operations.
Workaround: When performing flash operations, use the Flash_loader Execute button. This action ensures that the correct source files are used in the generated project.To locate the Flash_loader Execute button, open the Target Preferences block in your model, select the Peripherals tab, and select the Flash_loader pane.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: possible incorrect results when a signal with an atomic data type propagates through an auto-inserted or virtual-bus driven Rate Transition block
Description: Beginning in Release 2010b, code generation for a Rate Transition block optimizes away unnecessary semaphore protection or double-buffering if the data type of the input signal is atomic. A data type is atomic  if it can be atomically loaded and stored on the target hardware, that is, if the size of the data type is less than or equal to the Largest atomic type  value displayed on the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. However, in two scenarios, this optimization might generate code in which a local variable is used before it is initialized. Executing the code might produce incorrect numerical results. The following two scenarios might produce incorrect generated code. Scenario 1 - Rate transition with automatic Rate Transition block insertion:  On the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box for your model, the option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is selected, and the parameter Deterministic data transfer  is set to Never  or Whenever possible . In your model, the software automatically inserts a Rate Transition block to transition a signal from a faster rate to slower rate (for periodic rate transition), or from a higher-priority rate to a lower-priority rate (for asynchronous rate transition). The data type of the input signal is atomic.  Scenario 2 - Rate transition with a virtual bus input:  In your model, a Rate Transition block has a virtual bus input. In the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the option Ensure data integrity during data transfer  is selected, and the option Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  is cleared. The Rate Transition block transitions a signal in the virtual bus from a faster rate to a slower rate (for periodic rate transition), or from a higher-priority rate to a lower-priority rate (for asynchronous rate transition). The data type of the signal is atomic.
Workaround: To work around this bug in the automatic block insertion scenario, you must avoid using automatic block insertion when the other criteria of the scenario are met.To work around this bug in the virtual bus input scenario, you can replace the virtual bus input with individual signals, inserting a Rate Transition block for each individual signal that requires it.

Summary: SWT Denoising 2-D GUI errors when saving a denoised color indexed image
Description: Saving a MAT file of a color indexed image denoised with the SWT Denoising 2-D GUI results in an error. The user encounters this error when attempting to execute the Save De-Noised Image as  operation.

Summary: Custom colors set on a Simulink object may be lost on save.
Description: If you apply a custom color to a Simulink model background, block or annotation foreground, or background that has both red and green components (set to 1), the color will appear correctly on screen, but will report to the command line and save into the model as "white." The custom setting will therefore be lost on save and load. For example: >> set_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor', '[1 1 0]') >> get_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor') ans = white
Workaround: Setting a color that is close to your desired color, but with either the red or green component not quite 1 will succeed.For example: >> set_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor', '[0.95 1 0]') >> get_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor') ans = [0.950000, 1.000000, 0.000000]

Summary: Japanese characters in Stateflow or MATLAB Function block path names might cause assertion errors
Description: If a Stateflow or MATLAB Function block path name contains Japanese characters, the block might trigger assertion errors while being loaded, edited or simulated on a Japanese Windows machine.
Workaround: Use only ASCII characters in Stateflow or MATLAB Function block names and in the names of their parent subsystems.

Summary: Segmentation fault when running Simulink Design Verifier
Description: If a model contains a Mux or Demux block, a segmentation fault might occur when running the Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: Simulink may crash while performing an update diagram on old models that cause errors due to undefined variables
Description: While initializing a model (such as during an update diagram, simulation, or code generation), Simulink will error out if any workspace variables referenced in the model are not defined. If all of the following conditions are true, then Simulink will crash. the model being initialized was saved in R2010a or a prior release the model references workspace variables to specify mask parameters of blocks the model is initialized without defining the referenced workspace variable
Workaround: Defining the referenced workspace variables before initializing the model will eliminate this problem.

Summary: Data Cursor text for bar charts with unsorted X data can be incorrect
Description: When the bar  function is supplied unsorted X data, any Data Cursors created for that bar chart can display incorrect data tips.
Workaround: Sort the X data before passing it to the bar function. A simple way to do this is:[sorted_x, idx] = sort(unsorted_x);sorted_y = unsorted_y(idx);?

Summary: Some GigE Vision cameras may fail to be discovered
Description: Some GigE Vision cameras may fail to be discovered using IMAQHWINFO, and therefore be unavailable to be used from within the Image Acquisition Toolbox. Conditions that may cause this to occur are: a camera has an XML file that has a size in bytes that is a multiple of four a camera has only one PixelFormat available
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent numerical results between ERT and GRT generated code when an IC (initial condition) block is present in a repeatedly triggered function-call subsystem
Description: The behavior of generated code (and simulation) for the IC block differs in subtle ways depending on the target selected for the containing model: If an ERT-based target is selected:  When first run, the IC block outputs the initial condition value. When run a second time, the IC block passes through the input value regardless of the current time. If the GRT target is selected:  When first run, the IC block outputs the initial condition value. Subsequently, the IC block outputs the initial condition value only if the current time is equal to the time when the block was first run.  The above behavior might produce numerical results that are inconsistent between ERT and GRT generated code if either of the following conditions is true: The IC block is present in a function-call subsystem that iterates multiple times or is part of an iterator system. The IC block is continuous, and resides in a model that has states and is configured with a variable-step solver.

Summary: sftp can generate a misleading error message: "Received message is too long"
Description: Shell init files such as .profile , .bashrc , and .cshrc  can emit characters to the terminal that do not interact well with the sshd  sftp  subsystem on the server. The server reports the problem as Received message is too long: 1718968924 . (The final number varies based on what exactly is being emitted, but it will be large.) Recent updates to third-party generic scheduler examples rely on sftp  to move files if your desktop does not share a file system with the cluster. The problem does not lie in the generic scheduler examples or in sftp .
Workaround: First, verify that command-line sftp shows a similar error:> sftp your-submit-node Connecting to your-submit-node... Received message too long 1718968929 If this use of sftp works, you might have a different problem. If this code exhibits the problem, the number might different, but should be similarly large. To fix the problem, you need to find the place in your shell's initialization that is emitting extra characters. Start with your shell's man pages to get a list of files to start checking. Those files might call other files. It might be something as obvious as a call to echo or it might be something subtle and buried deep in some code. Also, examine commands that change the colors and fonts of your shell.Once you have located the code causing the problem, either remove the offending lines or bracket them with some checks to ensure that they do not emit the characters when sftp starts the shell. Most sftp server implementations either do not set the TERM variable, or set it to dumb. You can wrap your calls that emit characters with something like this, appropriate for your shell:if ($?TERM != 0)    then if ("$TERM" != "dumb")         then echo "sftp problem fixed by using TERM variable!"     endif endif Use command line sftp again to verify that this works properly before attempting to submit to your cluster from MATLAB.

Summary: Update Diagram (Ctrl+D) failure for hdlcoder_audiofiltering.mdl demo model
Description: hdlcoder_audiofiltering.mdl is a demo model shipped with Simulink HDL Coder in Release 2010b. When you initiate an Update Diagram (Ctrl+D ) for this model, Simulink displays the following error message: The Spectrum Scope block cannot inherit sample time when the 'Spectrum units' parameter is set to dBW/Hertz and the 'Buffer overlap' parameter is set to a non-zero value. To specify the sample time, clear the 'Inherit sample time from input' check box on the Axis Properties tab and specify a value for the 'Sample time of original time series' parameter.
Workaround: See Fix.

Summary: fec.ldpcdec does not decode a row-vector parity-check matrix correctly.
Description: The decode method output for an fec.ldpcdec object is not correct for a row-vector parity-check matrix. Although not a frequent use case (especially for LDPC codes), the decoding algorithm can be used to decode single parity-check codes. In such a case, the method produces an incorrect answer.

Summary: Indices may not be negative error during ASAP2 file generation for Prelookup block with dynamic breakpoint data
Description: When specifying Breakpoint data  to a Prelookup block, you can specify the Source  value as Dialog  or Input port . However, in R2010b, ASAP2 file generation does not support specifying breakpoint data using Input port . If you specify Input port , the ASAP2 file generation process displays a Target Language Compiler (TLC) message similar to the following: Error: File: D:\MATLAB\MATLAB2010b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 211 Column: 73 Indices may not be negative Error: File: D:\MATLAB\MATLAB2010b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 215 Column: 73 Indices may not be negative

Summary: solve errors when called for some systems that contains trigonometric functions and real variables.
Description: The solve  command errors when called with some systems having trigonometric functions and real variables: >> syms p1 p2 p3 y1 y2 y3 real>> S=solve('p1-y1','p2-y2','p3-sin(y3)','y1','y2','y3')??? Error using ==> mupadmex Error in MuPAD command: Index exceeds matrix dimensions.
Workaround: Call the MuPAD symbolic engine directly. For example,>> S = evalin(symengine,'solve([p1-y1,p2-y2,p3-sin(y3)],[y1,y2,y3])')

Summary: Running an ASP.NET application using a MATLAB Builder NE assembly crashes when deployed to IIS 6 or later
Description: A MATLAB function is compiled into a .NET Assembly and is used in an ASP.NET Web application. The web application works without any issues on the ASP.NET Development Server (shipped with Microsoft Visual Studio). However, when deployed to an IIS 6 or later version server it causes the Application Pool to crash. In the system log the following message is seen: A process serving application pool 'DefaultAppPool' suffered a fatal communication error with the Windows Process Activation Service. The process id was 'XXXX'. The data field contains the error number. In the %SystemRoot%\Temp  directory there is a Java crash log (hs_errXXXX.pid ) containing the following: Stack: [0x061b0000,0x069b0000],  sp=0x069a84d0,  free space=8161kNative frames: (J=compiled Java code, j=interpreted, Vv=VM code, C=native code)C  [kernel32.dll+0x3fbae]C  [MSVCR80.dll+0x28e89]C  [libmx.dll+0x16cdf]C  [libmx.dll+0x17928]C  [libmwservices.dll+0x9d83e]C  [libmwservices.dll+0xa66d]C  0x7b65167dj  com.mathworks.services.Prefs.nativeGetPreferencesDirectory()Ljava/lang/String;+0j  com.mathworks.services.Prefs.getPropertyDirectory()Ljava/lang/String;+0j  com.mathworks.services.Prefs.load()V+15j  com.mathworks.services.Prefs.<clinit()>V+64v  ~StubRoutines::call_stub By default, an Application Pool runs under the "NetworkService" ID which does not have write access to the %UserProfile%  folder. This bug is due to the MCR trying to create a default MATLAB preference directory under %UserProfile%  if the preference directory is missing, causing a segmentation violation.
Workaround: To work around the issue, do either of the following:Create an environment variable named MATLAB_PREFDIR. Set the variable to a folder with write access to NETWORK SERVICE.Create a new Application Pool that runs under the "LocalSystem" Identity (found in the Advanced Settings dialog of the Application Pool). Configure your application to use this Application Pool (found in the Basic Settings dialog of your Application).

Summary: Simulink manifest comparison can give the wrong answer if "Include subfolders" is not checked.
Description: Manifest comparisons can incorrectly report that a folder does not have changes if the option to "Include subfolders" is not selected and the manifests refer to files within subfolders of the project root.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, always select the "Include subfolders" check box when comparing Simulink manifests.

Summary: Incorrect mask prompts and values for some masks containing Japanese characters
Description: When loading some masked blocks, the mask prompt strings and values may display incorrectly. The problem occurs only when the last character in a prompt or value is a Japanese character. Not all Japanese characters cause this problem. Instead of displaying one prompt string for each dialog entry, the mask displays two or more strings for the same entry, separated by pipe characters ("|"), and later entries are misaligned. Entries at the end of the mask dialog may display a placeholder string, e.g. "Mask Parameter 5". Similar problems can occur with the values in the mask dialog if they contain Japanese characters.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, make sure you include an ASCII character at the end of each prompt and value string. For example, you can add a space character at the end of each prompt or value.

Summary: Changing the data type of a structure field in the property of a Simulink data object may be ignored.?
Description: If you change the property of a Simulink data object that contains a structure by changing only the data type of a structure field, the change is ignored. To apply a data type change, you must also change the value of the structure field at the same time. Consider the following example. a = Simulink.Parameter; a.Value = struct('field1', 1, 'field2', 2);a.Value.field1 = int8(1); The data type of field1  is still double , despite the explicit change to int8 .
Workaround: Use one of the following approaches:Clear the structure field before setting a field valueReset the entire structure instead of setting an individual field value

Summary: Incorrect scheduling of exported functions during PIL block simulations
Description: Incorrect scheduling of exported functions during PIL block simulations can lead to incorrect simulation results or the following error: ??? Caused by:  Unexpected or internal error encountered in "slOneSimStep". Please report this to The  MathWorks if you can cause it to recur When this error occurs, you must restart MATLAB. This bug affects exported functions that are configured for the ert.tlc and autosar.tlc system target files.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:October 25, 2010: attachment_676211_10b_2010-10-25.zip: initial version

Summary: National Instruments PCI-6221 37-pin driver maps counter and encoder I/O to incorrect pins
Description: The National Instruments PCI-6221 37-pin driver maps counter and encoder inputs and outputs to the same pins as the PCI-6221 68-pin version. However, the 37-pin version does not have all these pins available, causing the National Instruments PCI-6221 37-pin driver counter and encoder to be partially unusable.
Workaround: As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files. These files are named according to the release they support.Select and download the appropriate workaround file for your release.Locate the downloaded file - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Type matlabroot to locate your MATLAB installation - this directory is referred to as <MATLABROOT> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Navigate to <DOWNLOAD> and open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to <MATLABROOT>.Restart MATLAB.October 21, 2010: attachment_676200_9a_win32_2010-10-21.zip: initial version, works on R2009a onlyOctober 21, 2010: attachment_676200_9b_win32_2010-10-21.zip: initial version, works on R2009b onlyOctober 21, 2010: attachment_676200_10a_win32_2010-10-21.zip: initial version, works on R2010a onlyOctober 21, 2010: attachment_676200_10b_win32_2010-10-21.zip: initial version, works on R2010b only?

Summary: C/C++ builds using emlc command might fail on Linux systems if "." is not on the system path
Description: Depending on the shell used on Linux systems, the emlc  command might fail when generating C/C++ code with a message indicating that the file mexopts.sh  cannot be found.
Workaround: First workaround:Ensure that "." is in the path before invoking MATLAB and using the emlc command.Second workaround:Backup toolbox/emlcoder/emlcoder/private/EmitMakefile.pReplace this file with the EmitMakefile.p file contained in the attachment.Restart MATLAB.Depending on your preference settings, you might also need to run the rehash command to refresh the toolbox cache.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect reuse of function-call initiator blocks
Description: Code generation might incorrectly reuse two or more function-call initiator blocks under all of the following conditions: The inputs or outports of the function-call subsystem must connect to the function-call initiator block The function-call subsystem blocks, called by the function-call initiator blocks, must be identical The function-call initiator block must also be identical The input or output connectivity of those function-call initiator and function-call subsystem pairs do not match  The function-call initiator can be a S-Function block, Subsystem block, MATLAB block, or Stateflow chart. The attached model minvalid_fcn_call_reuse.mdl  is an example of this modeling pattern. In this example, the function-call initiator block is a Stateflow chart.
Workaround: Download the attached zip file and extract the P-code MATLAB script findInvalidCodeReuse.p. To determine if the model might produce this failure, execute this p-script with the model name as an argument. For example,>> invalidReuse = findInvalidCodeReuse('minvalid_fcn_call_reuse')returns a structured cell array of size one, that contains two block handles indicating invalid code reuse. You can locate these subsystems using the hilite_system command. For example,>> hilite_system(invalidReuse{1}.blkHdls)highlights blocks that might be incorrectly reused in the generated code.To work around this problem, for each of the highlighted blocks, open the Subsystem Block Parameter dialog box. On the Code Generation pane, set the Function packaging parameter to either Inline or Function.

Summary: Model update may fail if a bus object with complex bus elements is specified for an Outport block
Description: You may receive an error similar to the following if all the conditions listed below exist: Complex signal mismatch. Input port 1 of 'modelname/Subsystem/Out1' expects a signal of numeric type real. However, it is driven by a signal of numeric type complex. At model update time, you may receive a message similar to this if all  of these conditions are true:  The Outport block specifies a bus object for its data type. The bus object has at least one complex bus element. The incoming bus is virtual.
Workaround: You can use either of these two workarounds: Remove the bus object specification from the Outport block dialog box.Use a non-virtual bus.

Summary: Cannot close the Coverage/Informer Help window
Description: After selecting the Coverage for this model  check box in the Coverage Settings dialog and running a simulation, a Coverage Report window and a Coverage window opens. The Coverage window does not have minimize, maximize and close buttons.
Workaround: Select the Coverage Display Window,and then use the keyboard shortcut ALT-F4 to close the window.

Summary: Neural network not compiling
Description: Compiled code is not able to load a saved neural network object, but generates an error like this: Warning: An error occurred when running a class's loadobj method. The object that was loaded from the MAT-file was a copy of the object before the loadobj method was run. The rest of the variables were also loaded from the MAT-file.

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation might use the wrong MEX-file if a custom Simulation cache folder is configured
Description: When the Simulink preference Simulation cache folder  (CacheFolder ) is set to a folder other than the current working folder (pwd ), Accelerator mode simulation might use a potentially stale model_acc.mexext   from pwd , if one exists, rather than a current version created by target code generation in the cache folder. This could lead to silent wrong answers, crashes, or internal errors.
Workaround: To work around this bug, cd to a folder that does not contain any MEX-files before simulating in Accelerator mode.

Summary: When importing large results files, an "out of memory" problem occurs
Description: When importing large results files, a scaling issue can occur, leading to an "out of memory" problem. The "import review" process may lead to scaling problems when large files are part of the verification.
Workaround: The attached file contains a patch. This patch includes fixes for the following issues:Error '@FFT: color of comment is different' when importing" comments Error '@FFT: Unable to find the source file <source.h>' when importing commentsError '@FFT: Try to access line <line>' when importing comments Review information not displayed after import until package is parsed Automatic comments import does not justify the same line of codeWhen importing results, a scaling issue can occur for big files, leading to an "out of memory" problem ??

Summary: SIL simulation with BullseyeCoverage does not produce link to HTML code generation report if Windows is configured for certain Regional and Language Options, for example, Korean.
Description: To find out whether you have this problem: Open a model, for example,  rtwdemo_sil_topmodel . Ensure the model is configured to run in the software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation mode. Through the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > SIL and PIL Verification  pane, configure the model to use the BullseyeCoverage code coverage tool. Run a simulation.  At the end of the simulation, you should see a hyperlink in the MATLAB command window that allows you to open the HTML code generation report (which includes code coverage annotations). However, for certain regional settings and tool versions of BullseyeCoverage, the software may generate an error. For example: [Fatal Error] tp3554ab8a_616e_4f03_bef6_2a78f21f984b:3:102: Invalid byte 1 of 1-byte UTF-8 sequence. ?
Workaround: To work around the issue, update your version of BullseyeCoverage to 7.14.23 or later.?

Summary: STL files generated with SimMechanics Link from Autodesk Inventor 2011 with incorrect units
Description: When you generate a SimMechanics XML file from Autodesk Inventor 2011 with SimMechanics Link, the associated exported STL files save their data in centimeters (cm), irrespective of the assembly unit. The wrong unit in the STL files results in wrong SimMechanics visualization from the model generated from the XML file.
Workaround: Please contact MathWorks technical support for a patch for this problem.https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html

Summary: Precision provided by function worldfilewrite is fixed at 8 decimal digits
Description: The precision provided by function worldfilewrite  is fixed at 8 decimal digits to the right of the decimal place. This amounts to specifying the center of the first pixel or grid cell to within 1/100 of a micron when working in meters or slightly over 1 millimeter when working in degrees (on planet Earth). But for the position of the pixel or grid cell diagonally opposite the first, in an M-by-N image or grid, the precision is decreased by a multiple of M or N. M and N could easily be on the order of 10^4, resulting in a loss of up to 10 cm precision when working in degrees, for example. Just how much loss actually occurs in a given situation depends also on whether or not the specific pixel/cell sizes are "round" numbers that can be represented exactly in the floating point set. This loss of precision does not always occur, and is not critical when it does, but it is unnecessary and it can affect computed latitude-longitude (or map x-y) limits that would otherwise come out to be round numbers. You may also find that if you export a referencing matrix to a world file and then import it back into MATLAB via worldfileread , the result returned by worldfileread  may differ from your original referencing matrix.
Workaround: If you encounter this problem in any release through R2010b, you can modify your installed version of worldfilewrite.m as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/worldfilewrite.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: worldfilewrite.m.oldEdit the format string in the following line, replacing "8" with a larger value:fprintf(fid, '%20.8f\n', W);Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?Alternatively, you can incorporate the more adaptive approach described in the article How Many Digits to Write? in the blog Loren on the Art of MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: parameter represented in generated code in extern declaration instead of with #define macro
Description: If all of the following conditions exist, the code generator declares a parameter as an extern  instead of with a #define  macro: Parameter is defined with a custom storage class (type Define (Custom) ) In the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, Code Generation > Code Placement  > Data declaration  is set to Data declared in source file  In the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, Code Generation > SIL and PIL Verification > Create block  is set to SIL   When you compile the generated code, the invalid declaration statement causes a compilation error.
Workaround: Define the parameter as an MPT parameter object.Configure the model such that the code generator places the #define statement in the header file that you specify with Code Generation > Code Placement > Data declaration filename.

Summary: Models with non-inlined S-functions fail the Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check.
Description: When you run a Simulink Design Verifier analysis on a model that contains S-functions and you enable automatic stubbing, instances of S-functions should be stubbed so that partial results can be generated. If the model includes a non-inlined S-function, stubbing cannot be performed. The software indicates that model is incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier, and no results are generated. When you encounter this problem, the following error message displays: Block $BLOCKPATH$ is a non-inlined s-function, which is not supported with the current configuration. Consider selecting the support 'non-inlined s-functions' option on the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface pane ? ? ?

Summary: MATLAB Function blocks (formerly Embedded MATLAB Function blocks) cause simulation failures if they contain bus objects using enumerations defined in external header files
Description: Simulink models containing MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts fail to simulate if all of the following conditions exist: Stateflow or MATLAB Function block has bus data or signals Associated bus object contains enumeration fields Bus object and enumeration specify custom header files, i.e., are defined in custom header files  The error message is: enumType ~= enumType ?

Summary: daysadd does not return the correct answer when the input basis value is 13 (BUS/252).
Description: Calculating daysadd  with a basis value of 13 gives an answer of 0.

Summary: Output/Update methods for Simulink subsystems are not merged as expected in generated Structured Text code
Description: In the generated Structured Text code from Simulink subsystems, the coder generates code for Output and Update methods in separate case statements instead of one case statement. This is a minor readability issue and has negligible affect on efficiency or code-correctness.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator can produce incorrect code or a segmentation violation for data defined as an alias type for Simulink.StructType
Description: The code generator can produce incorrect code (more or less padding than expected) or a segmentation viloation when using data defined as an alias type for Simulink.StructType . This bug also affects accelerated simulation targets. This bug does not  affect Simulink.Bus  types, because Simulink does not support alias types for Simulink.Bus .
Workaround: Define data to use a Simulink.StructType directly, instead of using an alias for Simulink.StructType.

Summary: MATLAB crashes at model closing time
Description: If you have a Simulink model containing a SimPowerSystems subsystem that is linked to a library block, closing the model might trigger a seg-v, with the Stack Trace referring to a failed assertion in .\utils\object.cpp and in stateflow.dl.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder optimization does not use specified minimum and maximum values for Data Store Memory and some other Simulink blocks with state identifiers.
Description: The code generation optimization based on specified minimum and maximum values does not take into account the minimum and maximum values specified for Data Store Memory blocks. The optimization should use these values whenever corresponding Data Store Read blocks exist in the model. This issue also affects other blocks, such as Unit Delay and Discrete-Time Integrator, that have state identifiers. For these blocks, if the state identifier is specified as a variable that resolves to a Simulink.Signal object, the optimization does not use the minimum and maximum values specified for the Simulink.Signal object.
Workaround: The workaround for the Data Store Memory block issue is to add a Signal Specification block with the desired minimum and maximum values downstream from each Data Store Read block.In the attached model, the first subsystem does not use this workaround, and therefore generates code for the relational operator. The second subsystem does use the workaround so it optimizes the code, simply setting the output to true.void mDSM_workaround_step(void){ Out_Missed_Optimization = (3 * In1 <= (int8_T)(5 * mDSM_workaround_DWork.A)); Out_Workaround = TRUE;}

Summary: When using a path in the addheader option to loadlibrary,   the directory delimiter and case of the file name must match that used by the C compiler.
Description: If a path is specified to a header file in an addheader  statement, then the directory delimiter must match that used in line statements that are placed into the preprocessed header file by the C pre-processor. MATLAB always ignores the file extension. The Microsoft compilers use the C escape for a backslash in the header path. For example: "C:\\dir\\anotherdir\\headerfile"  ?
Workaround: Do not specify a path in addheader arguments to loadlibrary. The only case for using a path is to differentiate between two header files with the same name in different directories.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'Software Safety Analysis Level 1' with the message "@FFT: illegal call to <package.procedure>, #actuals=<nb_actuals>, #formals=<nb_formals>."
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on Ada code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: * Software Safety Analysis Level 1 ...  ----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: illegal call to <package.procedure>, #actuals=<nb_actuals>, #formals=<nb_formals>|An internal consistency problem has been detected.|In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,|the computation has been halted.|...----------------------------------------------------------------------FATAL ERROR while processing <package>.dfaexception Failure(Failure) raised.  Note  that error has nothing to do with <package.proecdure> . The reason for the error is code-specific, so there is no easy workaround. If you experience this error, send the affected code to customer support. We will investigate the problem and provide a workaround, if possible.

Summary: emlc and emlmex commands might fail to compile multiple entry points when attempting to produce a compilation report.
Description: Depending on the order in which multiple entry-points appear on the emlc  or emlmex  command line, the commands might fail when attempting to produce a compilation report. The error message reads "Index exceeds matrix dimensions ".
Workaround: Replace the file toolbox/emlcoder/emlcoder/private/irGenReport.p with the attached file, after backing up the original.

Summary: Setting the ReturnedColorSpace property to 'bayer' results in corrupt images.
Description: When setting the ReturnedColorSpace  property of a videoinput  object to 'bayer ', the resulting image is not properly demosaiced. The raw data is acquired correctly and is not corrupted, but the demosaiced version returned by getsnapshot  or getdata  does not look right.
Workaround: There is no direct workaround in Image Acquisition Toolbox? at this time. The demosaic function in the Image Processing Toolbox? can be used to convert the Bayer pattern into an RGB image after the data has been returned by getdata or getsnapshot.

Summary: TLC symbol qualified with (::) might be incorrectly resolved in local scope
Description: A TLC symbol reference qualified with the scope resolution operator (:: ) is incorrectly resolved in the local scope instead of in the global scope, if all of the following conditions are met: The TLC symbol is defined in the local record scope The %with  scope is added to the current list of scopes
Workaround: Rename the global symbol so that it is no longer shadowed by the definition in the local scope.

Summary: Simulink may produce incorrect answer if Inport block of For Each Subsystem connects directly to a Merge or Concatenate block input
Description: Simulink may produce an incorrect answer under the following conditions: An Inport block residing in a For Each Subsystem is connected directly to the input of a Concatenate block or a Merge block. The corresponding input signal is not set to be partitioned in the Block Parameters dialog of the For Each block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Inport block and the input to the Concatenate or Merge block. Or, if possible, move the Concatenate or the Merge block outside of the For Each Subsystem.

Summary: Double-clicking folders in Report Generator causes components to disappear or be moved to the bottom of pane
Description: When you expand or collapse (by double-clicking) components and data items that contain folders (for example, "Formatting"), items may be moved to the bottom of the middle window pane.
Workaround: After expanding the folder, force the middle pane to refresh:Select the top element of the tree in the left pane ("Report Generator").Click back on any other element in the tree.

Summary: Simulink.saveVars does not save Solver parameter to file
Description: When saving a Simulink.ConfigSet  to a MATLAB file, the Simulink.saveVars  function does not save the Solver  parameter. As a result, if you load a configuration set from a file that was created using Simulink.saveVars , the Solver  value is the default value.
Workaround: You can use Simulink.saveVars to save the configuration set into a MATLAB function, then manually change the Solver value.

Summary: Simulink Library Browser can open empty.
Description: The Simulink Library Browser can open indicating inaccurately that it contains no blocks.
Workaround: Exit MATLAB.The best solution is to change the permissions of the directory /tmp/LibraryBrowser so that it is writable by any user. Then the problem will cease permanently.If it is impossible to change the permissions of /tmp/LibraryBrowser, The second-best solution is to delete the directory /tmp/LibraryBrowser. The problem will be solved for the user who runs MATLAB next, but may exist for other users on the same machine.

Summary: When using the Polyspace Queue Manager (Spooler), multiple processes continue to run after you close the Progress Monitor window
Description: When using the Polyspace Queue Manager (Spooler) to follow the progress of a verification, 3 processes continue to run after you close the Progress Monitor. The following processes continue running: polyspace.exe javaw.exe psqueue-progress.exe  ? ?
Workaround: These processes can be killed manually using the Windows Task Manager.

Summary: On all Windows platforms (32bit & 64bit) using drag-n-drop to copy diagram elements between Simulink Editor windows may paste the diagram elements with an incorrect offset from the mouse location.
Description: When the horizontal or vertical scroll offsets are greater than zero on the target window for a drag-n-drop copy operation between Simulink subsystem windows, the copied elements' locations will be offset incorrectly. This offset is directly proportional to the scrollbar offset values. ? ?
Workaround: There are ways to work around this issue:Use Fit-to-view (spacebar) on the target window to remove scrollbar offsets before dropping, orUse the Simulink clipboard operations copy/paste to copy and paste between the subsystem windows??

Summary: The Simulink software saves the Boolean state as an 8-bit unsigned integer in the SimState
Description: When the block state is a Boolean value, the Simulink software saves the state as an 8-bit unsigned integer in the SimState. Instead, the state should be stored as a logical value.
Workaround: As a workaround, convert the 8-bit unsigned integer to logical values when accessing it using the loggedStates property.

Description: Double-clicking on a SimRF Equivalent Baseband block brings up a wrong dialog in many situations. Meanwhile, the following message shows up in the MATLAB command window. No appropriate method, property, or field Block for class Simulink.SLDialogSource.

Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: graycomatrix errors when input image is a row vector
Description: When you call graycomatrix(I,...)  and I  is a row vector, the function errors, as in this example: I = [ 1 1 5 6 8 8 2 3 5 7 0 2 0 2 3 5 6 7];[glcms, SI] = graycomatrix(I); ??? Error using ==> or Matrix dimensions must agree. Error in ==> graycomatrix>computeGLCM at 233bad = isnan(v1) | isnan(v2);Error in ==> graycomatrix at 196GLCMS(:,:,k) = computeGLCM(r,c,Offset(k,:),SI,NL);

Summary: [U, S, V] = svd(A) may very rarely produce inaccurate results for low-rank matrix A.
Description: In very rare cases, [U, S, V] = svd(A)  may not return the correct result for m -by-n  matrix A  with m > n  and rank(A)<< m  .

Summary: Code generation fails for Embedded MATLAB function block using variable-size matrices
Description: If a model contains an Embedded MATLAB function block, which uses variable-size matrices, a segmentation fault might occur while generating code.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: subsref called within a method of a subclass derived from a built-in class gives wrong result.
Description: In MATLAB, a class can inherit from a MATLAB numeric or logical built-in class. However, because of a bug in MATLAB, calling the parenthesis ()  style subsref  within a method of such a class reults in a wrong value. In the following example, class B10 is a subclass of built-in class int32 . It defines a method called test which calls subsref  in the form of obj(idx) : classdef B10 < int32    methods        function obj = B10(arg)            obj@int32(arg);        end        function back = test(obj, idx)            back = obj(idx);        end    endend The following transcript illustrates the error >> obj = B10([1,2]);obj = B10([1,2]);>> obj.test(1)obj.test(1)ans =   B10  int32 data:           1           2 Noticed that the obj(idx)  inside method test gives back an object of B10 whose underlying value is [1 2]  instead of [1] . ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, call subsref directly. For example, change the above class to:classdef B10 < int32    methods        function obj = B10(arg)            obj@int32(arg);        end        function back = test(obj, idx)            back = subsref(obj, substruct('()', {idx}));        end    endend

Summary: Model simulation build failure or missing code coverage data when using Microsoft Visual Studio Version 9.0 Express, 10.0 Express, or 10.0 Professional
Description: When generating code for a model simulation using Microsoft Visual Studio Version 9.0 Express or 10.0 Express, build errors including the phrase 'not recognized ' might occur. For example: ### Building the Accelerator target for model: mymodel'"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 10.0\Common7\Tools\..\..\VC\bin\vcvars64"' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.'nmake' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.The make command returned an error of 9009'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'mymodel' aborted due to an error.  Also, when running a Software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation with code coverage enabled using Visual Studio Version 9.0 Express, 10.0 Express, or 10.0 Professional, coverage data might be missing, which generates a warning that includes the following text: The model is configured to collect code coverage measurements. However, the coverage file has not been created.
Workaround: To work around these bugs, download the attached .zip file and extract its contents to your MATLAB installation tree, using the following steps:On your system, log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation root folder by entering the matlabroot command in the MATLAB Command Window. This folder is referred to as matlabroot in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the .zip file attached to this Bug Report, attachment_673259_10b_2010-11-09.zip, and place the file in the matlabroot folder.Examine the contents of the .zip file and, for each file, make a backup copy of the equivalent file in your MATLAB installation tree.Extract the files from the .zip file into your MATLAB installation tree.

Summary: Calling the constructor of a package scoped class can crash MATLAB.
Description: This problem happens only when you add the parent directory containing the package using a relative directory name. For example, if /somewhere/dir/+pkg/cls.m  is a class file, executing the following statements when the current directory is /somewhere  can trigger the crash: >> addpath ./dir>> pkg.cls
Workaround: To workaround this issue, always use absolute names when adding new directories to MATLAB path. For example:>> addpath /somewhere/dir?

Summary: Code generation for a Constant block with a structure parameter and a constant sample time might not build
Description: Models with a Constant block that has all  of the following attributes might fail to build: Constant block specifies a structure parameter for its value. Constant block has a constant sample time.  The output signal from the Constant block has a test point.

Summary: In the Simulink Compensator Design Task, clicking "Update Simulink Block Parameters" may return an error?
Description: When designing a controller block using the Simulink Compensator Design Task in the Control and Estimation Tools Manager, you can update the block parmaters by clicking "Update Simulink Block Parameters." In some instances, an error occurs when your controller block is a Transfer Fcn, Zero-Pole or State-Space block. The error message might read "The parameters to the block [block name] could not be written for the following reason: Error due to multiple causes." ? ? ?
Workaround: Download the attachment and unzip its content to a temporary directory. Replace MATLABROOT\toolbox\slcontrol\slctrlguis\+slctrlguis\updateBlockParameter.m.

Summary: (Lookup Table Performance) Interpolation Using Prelookup block causes simulation slowdown
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block causes a simulation slowdown when the data type of the selection port ('sel') is double .
Workaround: To prevent the slowdown, set the data types of the index and selection ports to be uint32.

Summary: The Overwrite Values block may fail to propagate complex input data
Description: The Overwrite Values block may fail to propagate complex input data when the following conditions are met: The Source of overwriting value(s)  parameter is set to Second input port . The input to the second input port, V, is complex.  If you are using the block in this configuration, it may throw an unexpected error.

Summary: HDL Block parameters settings for blocks inside masked subsystems are not applied.
Description: If you set any HDL block parameters for a block inside a masked subsystem, the coder does not apply your settings during code generation. This problem occurs whether you use the HDL Block Parameters  dialog box or the hdlset_param  command to set the parameters.
Workaround: The recommended way to avoid this problem is to install the attached p-file, as follows:Locate the following directory within your MATLAB installation: <matlabroot>matlab\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcoder\@slhdlcoder\@ConfigurationManager\In that directory, remove or rename the file parseUserBlockSettings.pDownload the attached file, parseUserBlockSettings.p into the same directory, replacing the previous version of the file.Restart MATLAB.Alternatively, you can work around this issue by removing the mask on the parent subsystem.

Summary: A model containing a virtual bus issues an incorrect dimensions mismatch error under certain cases
Description: When you update a model, Simulink issues an incorrecr error message if both  of the following conditions are true:  The model contains a virtual bus that is connected to the input of a Bus Assignment block. The original bus element and the assigned signal vary in dimensionality in the following way: one of the signals has dimensions [1x1] , while the other has dimensions 1 .   The error message is similar to the following:  Incoming buses to block 'Model_Name/Switch' have a dimension mismatch. The signal at input port 1 of 'Model_Name/Bus Assignment' is of dimension [mxn], while its corresponding signal at input port 1 of 'Model_Name/Bus Creator' is of dimension [mxn].This error message is related to a hidden SubSystem block.
Workaround: Manually specify the dimensions of the original bus element and the assigned signal at the source so that they have the same dimensions.

Summary: Target Language Compiler build error when model has AUTOSAR inter-runnable variables with alias types
Description: Consider an AUTOSAR model with multiple runnables.  If you configure an inter-runnable variable in this model to have an alias type, when you build the model you see the following Target Language Compiler error:  Error: File: C:\MATLAB\R2010b_win64\rtw\c\tlc\lib\dtypelib.tlc Line: 876 Column: 30 The specified index (18) was out of the range 0 - 16 Error: File: C:\MATLAB\R2010b_win64\rtw\c\tlc\lib\dtypelib.tlc Line: 876 Column: 34 Ambiguous reference to DataType - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopes Error: File: C:\MATLAB\R2010b_win64\rtw\c\tlc\lib\dtypelib.tlc Line: 876 Column: 34 The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier Error: File: C:\MATLAB\R2010b_win64\rtw\c\tlc\lib\dtypelib.tlc Line: 876 Column: 30 The specified index (18) was out of the range 0 - 16
Workaround: Set the data type of the inter-runnable variable to the underlying base data type rather than an alias type.

Summary: Adding a flow graph using the Pattern Wizard while editing a state label causes assertion failures
Description: When editing the label of a graphical function or subcharted state, if you select an option in the Patterns  menu before committing the label edits, you get assertion failure error messages in the command window. The assertion failures are harmless and do not cause any data or session loss.
Workaround: Close and reopen the chart window to return to a proper editable state.

Summary: Certain blocks with input signal of at least 2 dimensions might cause segmentation fault during code generation
Description: A model might fail to generate code if it contains one of the following blocks and the input signal has at least 2 dimensions: Complex to Magnitude-Angle Math Function MinMax MinMax Running Resettable Reciprocal Sqrt Sqrt
Workaround: Insert a Reshape block before the block to reshape the signal to one dimensional.
