Summary: Instances of classes derived from built-in classes do not support compound indexing.
Description: If an object's class inherits from a built-in class, you cannot use compound indexing, like the following parentheses/dot reference to a method of the first object in an array: obj_array(1).methodName For example, enumeration class Simple  is a sub-class of int8 : classdef Simple < uint8  enumeration   Val(1); end methods  function data = Test(obj)   data = uint8(obj);  end endend The following MATLAB statement causes an error: >> arr = [Simple.Val Simple.Val]; >> arr(1).Test(); ??? Error using ==> subsref Improper index matrix reference.
Workaround: Avoid the error by using simple indexing using multiple steps. For example:>> a = arr(1); >> a.Test() ans = 1

Summary: Verilog code generation from a Stateflow chart can fail and generate an internal assertion
Description: Under certain circumstances Verilog code generation for a Simulink model that contains a Stateflow chart can fail with an internal assertion. The assertion message will look similar to: ??? Assertion failed:  .\pir_backend\VerilogBackend.cpp:1696:alreadyGeneratedNames[name] == i   In order for this error to occur, the following conditions must be met: Verilog is the code generation target language The Simulink subsystem for which code is generated must contain a Stateflow chart The Stateflow chart must have multiple subcharts Multiple subcharts reuse the same state name.   If all of these conditions are met, Simulink HDL Coder may emit the assertion above.
Workaround: If you encounter this  assertion, you can work around it by renaming the states such that  the same state name is not used in more than one subchart.

Summary: Polyspace verification stops when another prototype of the standard function "memset" is declared
Description: As soon as another prototype of memset() is declared in the list of source/header files, Polyspace verification stops with the following compilation error: C:\PolySpace\Support\CPP\sources\stdlib.h, line 4: error: declaration of function "memset" is incompatible with a declaration in another translation unit (functions have incompatible types or linkage).
Workaround: Use the option -no-extern-C to circumvent this link error. You can also solve the problem by defining a compilation flag named __polyspace_no_memset.?

Summary: Chart fails to compile due to identically named input or output ports of bus type
Description: A Stateflow chart fails to compile due to function redefinition make errors when the following conditions are true: The chart has Simulink functions. A Simulink function has an input or output (IO) port of the same name as a chart IO port, or another Simulink function IO port. The identically named ports are of different bus types.
Workaround: Change Simulink function bus parameter names so that they do not collide with other Simulink function bus parameter names and chart bus IO data names.

Summary: Subscript referencing symbolic objects with empty parentheses errors.
Description: Subscript referencing a symbolic object with parentheses and no indices errors. For example:</> >> syms x>> x()??? Error using ==> mupadmex Error in MuPAD command: Invalid index [list];

Summary: PIL block containing AUTOSAR runnables with feedback signals generates compilation error
Description: If you use a PIL block containing an AUTOSAR runnable that feeds an output signal through a Merge block back to an inport of the runnable, then the software produces a compilation error similar to the following: pil_interface.c(39) : error C2084: function 'real_T Rte_IrvIRead_VirtualSubsys_Runnable_Runnable1_irvY(void)' already has a body c:\work_dirs\R2010b\Subsystem_autosar_rtw\stub\Rte_VirtualSubsys.h(43) : see previous definition of 'Rte_IrvIRead_VirtualSubsys_Runnable_Runnable1_irvY'pil_interface.c(43) : error C2084: function 'void Rte_IrvIWrite_VirtualSubsys_Runnable_Runnable1_irvY(real_T)' already has a body c:\work_dirs\R2010b\Subsystem_autosar_rtw\stub\Rte_VirtualSubsys.h(39) : see previous definition of 'Rte_IrvIWrite_VirtualSubsys_Runnable_Runnable1_irvY'

Summary: Definite integration over [0, 2*pi] can return incorrect results.
Description: Symbolic definite integration of expressions rational in trigonometric functions over the interval [0,2*pi] can return incorrect results: syms xF = int(1/(1 + sin(x)*cos(x)), 0, 2*pi)F =-(2*pi*((-6*i)^(1/2)*(i + 2) - 3 + 3*i))/(3*((-6*i)^(1/2) - 3 + i)) Approximating the result numerically, you get: vpa(F)ans =3.6275987284684357011881565152843 - 2.9387358770557187699218413430556*10^(-39)*i Numeric integration returns the correct result: quad(@(x) 1./(1 + sin(x).*cos(x)), 0, 2*pi)ans =    7.2552 Incorrect results can also appear if the toolbox uses decomposition to compute an integral and the bug affects one of the branches. For example, the toolbox returns incorrect results for the following definite integral: int(x + 1/(1 + sin(x)*cos(x)), 0, 2*pi)

Summary: Parallel build of model reference hierarchy fails when using Microsoft Visual Studio Express compiler
Description: If you initiate a parallel build of a model reference hierarchy, and if the only compiler available is Microsoft Visual Studio Express, the build process displays the following eror message: A common compiler is needed in order to link the final executable together. However, a common compiler does not exist across all workers in the MATLAB Distributed Compute Server pool.
Workaround: To work around this bug, use a supported Professional edition of Microsoft Visual Studio. Supported compiler versions for current and previous MATLAB releases are listed at https://www.mathworks.com/support/compilers/.

Summary: Find Referenced Vars context menu item not disabled for libraries and blocks in libraries
Description: The Simulink Editor context menu item Find Referenced Variables  is not disabled for libraries and blocks in libraries, even though you cannot use this feature with libraries or blocks within libraries. Selecting that menu item causes an error to be issued at MATLAB command line.

Summary: solve can return incorrect additional solutions
Description: In some cases, the solve  command returns additional solutions that are incorrect. Starting from the version 5.1 (R2008b), the following command returns eight alleged solutions. Seven solutions contain parameter values for which the equations are not defined: A1 = solve('Vpn1_i*((1/Z11)+(1/Zs_i))-((Vpn1_o*Z21/Zs_o)+Vpn1_i)*(Z22/(Z12*Z21))',...           'Vpn2_i*((1/((Z11*Zsh)/(Z11+Zsh)))+(1/Zs_i))-((Vpn2_o*Z21/Zs_o)+Vpn2_i)*(Z22/(Z12*Z21))',...           'Vpn3_i*((1/Z11)+(1/Zs_i))-((Vpn3_o*Z21/Zs_o)+Vpn3_i)*(((Z22*Zsh)/(Z22+Zsh))/(Z12*Z21))',...           'Vpn4_i*((1/((Z11*Zsh)/(Z11+Zsh)))+(1/Zs_i))-((Vpn4_o*Z21/Zs_o)+Vpn4_i)*(((Z22*Zsh)/(Z22+Zsh))/(Z12*Z21))',...           'Z11','Z12','Z21','Z22'); Symbolic Math Toolbox version 5.5 (R2010b) does not find any solutions for this system.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Simulation mismatch between generated code and Normal mode for exported runnables with feedback signals
Description: If your model has an exported runnable that feeds an output signal through a Merge block back to an inport of the runnable, then the behavior of the generated code may not match the Normal mode simulation.
Workaround: By inspecting your model, ensure that no exported runnable produces a signal that feeds back to an inport through a Merge block.

Summary: On all Windows platforms (32bit & 64bit) marquee selection in Simulink Editor as well as block resizing are broken when horizontal or vertical scroll offsets are greater than zero.
Description: When working with Simulink, if the editor is panned to a region other than the upper left portion of the diagram, marquee selection and block resizing no longer work correctly. The marquee selection box is drawn in an incorrect location and the wrong diagram elements are selected. When resizing a block the block size may become corrupted.
Workaround: Change Log:- Uploaded separate win32/win64 binary attachments to fix bug in R2010b to avoid conflicts when applying patch. [Sept. 16th, 2010]Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Error call graph or variable access graph is empty
Description: While reviewing verification results, if you open the error call graph for a check, or the access graph for a variable, the graph window is empty. This is caused by a refresh problem. The problem is sporadic, and the reproduction scenario has not been clearly defined.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\PolySpace or in /usr/local/PolySpace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. PolySpace_Common and PolySpaceFor<lang>_R20xx are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Exit any Polyspace Graphical Intferface.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Summary: Fluid volume in the Single-Acting Hydraulic Cylinder computed incorrectly
Description: In the version of the Single-Acting Hydraulic cylinder introduced in R2010a, the volume of fluid associated with the piston initial position is accounted twice. This could lead to incorrect simulation results, especially at fast-changing signals and high fluid compressibility.
Workaround: Create a masked Simulink subsystem with connected Simscape Foundation library components under the mask. You can then use mask initialization and mask variables to configure the component to behave as you wish.

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector might not align and compare two identical runs
Description: On the Compare Runs  tab, the Simulation Data Inspector might not align and compare two identical simulation runs. This might happen if certain run metadata, such as, block path or signal name, are not unique for signals within a run.
Workaround: Change the alignment methods on the Compare Runs tab.Click the Compare Runs tab. Select the Options button, which displays the Align By and Then By parameters. Modify the Align By and Then By parameters from the drop-down lists.?

Summary: Switch block with variable-sized inputs asserts during code generation
Description: Real-Time Workshop code generation, or simulating a model using Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode, causes the following assertion when the model includes a Switch block with at least one variable-size input: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_dataconnector.cpp line 399: MatrixSubExpr::isa(dims_expr->outData())"

Summary: From Multimedia File block cannot play MOV file
Description: From Multimedia File block throws an error or shows a black screen while trying to play a MOV video file on Windows?7 or Windows?XP platforms.
Workaround: To workaround this problem for R2010b version of MATLAB, follow these steps for your specific platform:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Backup the following two files:For 32 bit platforms:$MATLABROOT\bin\win32\frommmfile.dll$MATLABROOT\toolbox\dspblks\dspmasks\private\DSPWinMMFileInfo.mexw32For 64 bit platforms:$MATLABROOT\bin\win64\frommmfile.dll$MATLABROOT\toolbox\dspblks\dspmasks\private\DSPWinMMFileInfo.mexw643. Download the attached frommmfilefix.zip. Unzip it into a temporary directory. Rename and save the following two files:For 32 bit platforms:frommmfile.dll_R2010b_win32 to $MATLABROOT\bin\win32\frommmfile.dllDSPWinMMFileInfo.mexw32_R2010b to $MATLABROOT\toolbox\dspblks\dspmasks\private\ DSPWinMMFileInfo.mexw32For 64 bit platforms:frommmfile.dll_R2010b_win64 to $MATLABROOT\bin\win64\frommmfile.dllDSPWinMMFileInfo.mexw64_R2010b to $MATLABROOT\toolbox\dspblks\dspmasks\private\ DSPWinMMFileInfo.mexw644. Restart MATLAB?.5. Execute the "rehash toolbox" command.

Summary: nlinfit gives "matrix dimensions must agree" error for robust fit when Y is a row vector
Description: If you specify Y as a row vector in a call to nlinfit , and you request a robust fit, you get the error     Matrix dimensions must agree.
Workaround: Supply a column vector for the Y argument.

Summary: Linearizing a Simulink model in the Control and Estimation Tools Manager returns incorrect results when the model has non-zero inport values
Description: In the Control and Estimation Tools Manager, when you linearize at the operating point currently specified in the Simulink model, non-zero values specified for the root level ports are not used. Therefore, if your model has non-zero inport values, the linearization is computed for an incorrect operating point and returns unexpected results. This issue does not appear when using the command LINEARIZE . ?
Workaround: If you are using version R2010b of MATLAB, install the attached patch:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate root folder of your MATLAB installation by entering matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached file, attachment _669690_10b_2010-10-06.zip. The downloaded file is referred to as <.zip file> in subsequent steps.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:- Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.- Linux/Macintosh Systems: Enter the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file.- Windows Systems: (a) Open the .zip file. (b) Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.- Linux/Macintosh Systems: Enter the following commands in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB> unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file.Restart MATLAB.For versions of MATLAB prior to R2010b, there is no workaround.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier property-proving report is missing parameter values for counterexamples.
Description: The Simulink Design Verifier property-proving analysis report is missing parameter values for the counterexamples.

Summary: Cannot specify the output data type of the Discrete Derivative block as a fixed-point data type or an expression
Description: When a user specifies the output data type of a Discrete Derivative block as a fixed-point data type or an expression, this specification does not take effect. Instead, the compiled output data type is always set to int16 . This bug does not affect output data types specified as inheritance rules or built-in data types (for example, int8 ).
Workaround: You can work around this problem by modifying the model as follows:Insert a Signal Specification block immediately after the Discrete Derivative block.Set the output data type of the Signal Specification block to the type you wanted to specify on the Discrete Derivative block.Set the output data type of the Discrete Derivative block to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation.If modifying the model is not an option, you may choose to replace the simulink.mdl file. If you choose this option, follow these installation instructions:Installation InstructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Function geotiffinfo does not error when reading certain non-GeoTIFF files
Description: Function geotiffinfo  should error if a file does not contain at least one GeoTIFF tag, but in certain cases it fails to issue such an error. This failure to error happens when an input TIFF file does not contain any GeoTIFF tags but does contain one of the following TIFF tags: RichTIFFIPTC , Photoshop , or ICCProfile . In this case, GEOTIFFINFO returns empty fields for the GeoTIFF information and other fields may be filled with data. This may cause function geotiffread  to return image data and referencing information (if an associated world file file is present) even though the input file is not actually a GeoTIFF file.

Summary: Loss of precision in referencing matrix constructed by function geotiffinfo
Description: There may be unnecessary loss of precision in the referencing matrix constructed by function geotiffinfo . The problem occurs when geotiffinfo  reads a GeoTIFF file in which the offset of the image from the origin, in world coordinates, is much larger than the spatial extent of the image. In cases like this, the first two rows of the 3-by-2 RefMatrix  field of the info structure may fail to match the ModelPixelScaleTag  values specified in the GeoTIFF file.
Workaround: Typically, the relationship between the rows and columns of a GeoTIFF image (or data grid) and its world coordinate system is rectilinear (free from rotation and skew). In such cases, you can work around this problem by recalculating the referencing matrix directly from the PixelScale and TiePoints values, as follows:info = geotiffinfo(filename); x = info.TiePoints(4);y = info.TiePoints(5);dx = info.PixelScale(1);dy = -info.PixelScale(2);% Move x, y from corner to center of pixelx1 = x + dx/2; y1 = y + dy/2; info.RefMatrix = makerefmat(x1,y1,dx,dy);

Summary: Subsystem analysis fails when referencing Simulink.Signal objects resolved to Data Store Memory blocks inside the subsystem.
Description: If the blocks inside a subsystem reference Simulink.Signal  objects that are resolved by Data Store Memory blocks inside the subsystem, Simulink Design Verifier subsystem analysis generates errors. The sldvextract  function also generates an invalid model from these subsystems.

Summary: Japanese appears as squares in Simulink Model Discretizer for some Windows display settings
Description: On Japanese Windows XP machines, the Japanese appears as squares in the Simulink Model Discretizer when the Display Properties dialog box has the following settings on the Appearance tab: Windows and buttons  is set to Windows Classic style . Color scheme  is set to Windows Standard .
Workaround: To work around the display issue, complete these steps before launching MATLAB:Open the Display Properties dialog box and click the Appearance tab.Select a different setting for Windows and buttons, such as Windows XP style.Select a different setting for Color scheme, such as Default.If you complete those steps before launching MATLAB, Japanese should appear correctly in the Simulink Model Discretizer.

Summary: Top model cannot be sample time independent if it has Model block (in SIL or PIL simulation mode) that accesses global data stores
Description: With a top model, you cannot set the Configuration Parameters > Solver > Periodic sample time constraint  field to Ensure sample time independent  when all of the following conditions apply: The top model references a model that is in the SIL or PIL simulation mode The referenced model has Configuration Parameters  > Solver > Periodic sample time constrain t set to Ensure sample time independent  The referenced model accesses global data stores  The software produces the following error: This model will not inherit a sample time because there are multiple sample times in the model.If this model does not need to inherit a sample time, change the "Sample time constraint" to something other than "Ensure sample time independent" on the Solver tab of the Configuration Parameters dialog.
Workaround: Use any of the following workarounds:For the top model, do not set Configuration Parameters > Solver > Periodic sample time constraint set to Ensure sample time independent.For the referenced model, do not set Configuration Parameters > Solver > Periodic sample time constraint set to Ensure sample time independent.Choose a different simulation mode for the Model block.Do not access global data stores within the referenced model.

Summary: Text component does not honor color selection on Japanese platforms
Description: The Text  component does not honor color selection on Japanese platforms. The text output is black and white.
Workaround: Try typing the name of the color in English instead of in Japanese. For example, instead of selecting "red" in Japanese, type the word "red" in English.To prevent the problem from happening again, install two patch files:Stop MATLAB.Back up the matlab\toolbox\shared\rptgen\@rptgen\@cfr_text\getDlgStyle.p and matlab\toolbox\shared\rptgen\@rptgen\@cfr_text\ja\xlate files. Download and copy the attached files to their respective locations (see the previous step).Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Generating code for a model referenced in normal mode that contains a Stateflow chart, Stateflow truth table, or Embedded MATLAB Function block may cause a segmentation violation
Description: Generating code for the top model that references a model causes a segmentation violation if the referenced  model meets all  of these conditions: It is referenced in Normal mode. It contains a Stateflow chart, Stateflow truth table, or Embedded MATLAB Function block. It uses External mode as the Data Exchange Interface for code generation.
Workaround: Reference the model in Accelerator mode instead of Normal mode.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Simulation and code generation results might differ for Saturation block with single-precision input and integer output
Description: Simulation and code generation results might differ for a Saturation block when the following conditions are true: The block uses single-precision input and integer output. The saturation limits are greater than 1/eps('single') = 8388608.

Summary: On Windows 7, compiling MEX-files using OpenWatcom might generate the error "Compiler returned a bad status".
Description: On Windows 7, if the MATLAB installation path contains a space, for example: C:\Program Files\MATLAB  compiling a MEX-file using the OpenWatcom compiler generates the following error: Error! E1073: Invalid option '-I"C:\Program' Error! E1139: Command line contains more than one file to compile Unable to open 'Files\MATLAB\R2010b"\extern\include.c' Error: Compiler returned a bad status compiling "<filename>"

Summary: Saving new empty Model Explorer view resets all views to factory settings
Description: If you add a Model Explorer view without specifying any properties for the view, then if you close and reopen Model Explorer, all views are reset to factory settings.
Workaround: Before closing Model Explorer, add at least one property to a newly created view.

Summary: Multiple MATLAB processes cannot easily access exclusive-mode GPUs simultaneously
Description: When trying to run multiple MATLAB processes (for example, MATLABPOOL workers) on a system with multiple GPUs that are configured to operate in exclusive mode, you might encounter errors when accessing the GPUs. For example, on a system with four GPUs, you might attempt to have each of four workers use a different GPU:  spmd, gpuDevice(labindex); end  This might fail on labs other than lab 1, reporting that all CUDA devices are busy. This problem occurs because each process first tries to access GPU 1, and then the device actually requested by the call to gpuDevice .
Workaround: You can initialize four workers by ensuring that each worker can temporarily access GPU 1 before that device is dedicated to lab 1:spmd   for ii = numlabs:-1:1     if ii == labindex       gpuDevice(labindex);     end     labBarrier   end end

Summary: The installgenicam command fails on Windows Vista? and Windows 7?.
Description: When attempting to execute the Image Acquisition Toolbox's? installgenicam  command to install the libraries necessary for using the gige  adaptor, the installation fails with the message: ? ??? Error using ==> installgenicam at 56  The GenICam installation process did not complete correctly for an unknown reason.
Workaround: September 01, 2010: 2010-09-01-attachment-667887-10.zip initial version?Installation instructions 1. Log onto your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.2. Download the workaround files associated with this Bug Report.3. Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to in subsequent steps.4. Exit MATLAB.5. Extract the contents of the downloaded file into your MATLAB installation directory determined in step 3.  You may need to launch your unzip program with administrator privileges depending on where MATLAB has been installed.

Summary: Model Advisor MAAB check Check transition orientations in flowcharts might fail on comments and % operator
Description: The Model Advisor MAAB check Check transition orientations in flowcharts  might issue the following error, Matrix dimensions must agree , when the model contains a Stateflow chart with any of the following: A comment on an unconditional transition. Multiline comments on a transition. A %  operator used for an operation, instead of a comment. For example, a %% b .  ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file, corresponding to your release, associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: volatile qualifier not generated for constant block I/O of standalone subsystems
Description: In R2010b, Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder added support for generating constants with the storage type qualifier volatile . However, this functionality was not enabled for constant block I/O of standalone subsystems.

Summary: Code-to-model navigation does not work for Stateflow atomic subcharts and Simulink functions in Stateflow charts
Description: The traceability comments generated for Stateflow atomic subcharts and Simulink functions in Stateflow charts contain incorrect hyperlinks. This bug causes code-to-model navigation to not work in the HTML Code Generation Report.

Summary: Workspace browser Value column does not show first ampersand in a char array
Description: If a variable contains a char array containing one or more ampersands (& ), the first ampersand does not appear in the Workspace browser Value  column. For example, if you create the following char array: >> x = 'a&b&c'x =a&b&c The Workspace shows the value as 'ab&c'
Workaround: View the variable in the Command Window by typing the variable name without a trailing semicolon, and press the Enter key.

Summary: Simulink models with TimeScopes can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive shortly into a simulation
Description: Simulating a Simulink model that uses TimeScopes for several seconds can cause MATLAB to stop responding. This issue can also occur when a figure contains a uipanel and the uipanel FontUnits  property is set to 'normalized '. To reproduce the issue, execute the following code in MATLAB 7.11 (R2010b) on any platform. uipanel('Title','MyTitle','FontUnits','normalized')  At this point, MATLAB does not respond. You need to halt the application and restart.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by downloading the attached file, patch_g667610.jar:1. Download the attachment and save the file into folder matlabroot\java, where matlabroot is the result of typing matlabroot at the command prompt.2. In the MATLAB Command Window, type cd(fullfile(matlabroot,'java')); unzip patch_g667610.jar3. Restart MATLAB.When you simulate your model or use normalized FontUnits in uipanels, MATLAB should now remain responsive.If you deploy an application that this issue can affect, also extract the patch to mcrroot/java/.The mcrroot is the location of the MCR (MATLAB Compiler Runtime library) used to run the compiled application.cd(fullfile(mcrroot,'java')); unzip patch_g667610.jar?

Summary: The Measurement Computing PCI-DAS-TC board will not initialize correctly if you download a model that does not contain a Measurement Computing DAS-TC block before you download a model that contains a Measurement Computing DAS-TC block
Description: After a target PC reboot, the Measurement Computing PCI-DAS-TC board will only initialize correctly if you download a model that contains a Measurement Computing DAS-TC block as the first model you download.  The board won't initialize correctly if you download any other model first.
Workaround: As a workaound, when working with this board, reboot the target PC before downloading a model that contains a Measurement Computing DAS-TC block.

Summary: Incorrect text location when masked blocks using the text icon command are rotated
Description: If the text icon command does not specify the horizontal or vertical alignment when used in the MaskDisplay of the block, it draws the text at the incorrect location when you rotate the block.
Workaround: To work around this problem, specify the horizontal or vertical alignment when specifying the text icon command, and then the text draws at the correct location.

Summary: Invalid code coverage settings may cause a build failure
Description: Simulink may fail to reset the code coverage settings if you change the system target file for a model. These invalid code coverage settings may cause a build failure. The following steps illustrate this problem:  Open a model, e.g., rtwdemo_sil_topmodel. From Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > SIL and PIL Verification set the "Code coverage tool" to "BullseyeCoverage" From the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation panel, change the "System target file"  When you change the System Target File, the Code coverage tool appears to reset to "None",  however, the code coverage settings internal to the model are not truly disabled. This may cause a build failure for this model (e.g. during a SIL or PIL simulation). ?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, make sure that you set the "Code coverage tool" to "None" before changing the System Target File.?

Summary: Dead Zone block outputs incorrect results when input data type cannot represent zero
Description: The Dead Zone block outputs incorrect results when the input data type cannot represent zero, which applies only to fixed-point data types with nonzero bias. The following expressions are two examples of fixed-point data types that cannot represent zero: fixdt(0, 8, 1, 1) fixdt(1, 8, 6, 3)
Workaround: Change the input data type so that it can represent zero precisely.

Summary: Look-Up Table component reverses column and row labels
Description: The output for the Look-Up Table component reverses the column and row labels.
Workaround: 1. Stop MATLAB.2. Back up matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_blk_lookup/makeFigureTwoD.p.3. Download and copy the attached file to its respective location (see the previous step).4. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error when building AUTOSAR model with data type alias that references enumeration data type
Description: If your Simulink AUTOSAR model has a port with a data type alias that references an enumeration data type, the software generates the following error when you build your model: The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook, during the exit hook generated the following error: Error using ==> feval Undefined function or method 'enumAlias.getIndexOfDefaultValue' for input arguments of type 'char'. The build process will terminate as a result.
Workaround: Instead of using a data type alias for the port, use the enumeration data type directly.Consider an example where the Data type field of an inport is set to enumAlias, a Simulink.AliasType object with the following properties:>> enumAlias.HeaderFile = 'Rte_Type.h';>> enumAlias.BaseType = 'Enum: ARBasicColors'; The software generates an error when you try to build the model. To work around this problem, set the Data type field of the inport to Enum: ARBasicColors.

Summary: MATLAB Report Generator may fail to generate RTF/Word documents on Win64 platforms
Description: MATLAB Report Generator uses a 32-bit version of OpenJade to generate RTF/Word documents. This version of OpenJade depends on the 32-bit Microsoft Visual C++ 2005 SP1 ATL Security Update redistributable. If this update is not installed on your Win64 platform, MATLAB Report Generator cannot generate RTF/Word documents.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, try installing a 64-bit version of OpenJadeBackup all files in the matlab\sys\jade\bin\win64 folder.Extract all files from attached openjade_win64.zip file to above folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Complex to Real-Imag block outputs incorrect imaginary value of variable-size signal
Description: If you configure a Complex to Real-Imag block to output the imaginary part of a variable-size signal of real values, the output of the block is not zero when simulating the model in Accelerator  mode or executing the generated code for the model. This only occurs for non-complex variable-size signals. For an example, see the attached model and MATLAB file.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, insert a Real-Imag to Complex block before the Complex to Real-Imag block in question. Configure the Real-Imag to Complex block to specify zero as a parameter for the imaginary part.?

Summary: Error when saving a figure containing axes synchronized by calling linkaxes with duplicated input arguments
Description: If you specify the same axis handle twice as input to linkaxes  and then try save the figure, the following error occurs and the figure is not saved: The specified string name does not exist in the appdata Error in ==> hgsave at 263  rmappdata(targets(i),'graphics_linkaxes_targets'); This issue usually arises by calling linkaxes  with an array of axes handles that contains duplicate entries. Note that no warning or error occurs upon calling linkaxes , and the axes synchronize correctly.
Workaround: Do not link an axes to itself with linkaxes. To be sure that an array of axes contains no duplicate entries, call the unique function on it before passing it to linkaxes.

Summary: Incorrect warning for Model Advisor MAAB check Check for mismatches between names of Stateflow ports and associated signals (db_0123) and IEC 61508 check Check usage of Stateflow constructs
Description: The Model Advisor MAAB check Check for mismatches between names of Stateflow ports and associated signals (db_0123)  and IEC 61508 check Check usage of Stateflow constructs  issue an incorrect warning when an input to a Stateflow chart is a bus signal.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file, corresponding to your release, associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Simplified Synchronous Machine SI Units block uses wrong unit for speed input signal
Description: When the Mechanical input  parameter of the Simplified Synchronous Machine SI Units block is set to Speed w , the input signal must be in rad/s. However, it is actually considered as per unit (pu). The simulation results are then incorrect.
Workaround: Use the Simplified Synchronous Machine pu Units block with the Mechanical input parameter set to Speed w. You need to divide the input signal (that is in rad/s), by 2*pi*F/p to convert it in per unit. F is the machine frequency, and p the number of pole pairs.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for an unconditionally zero initialized Merge block
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder eliminates a necessary zero initialization of an output variable of a Merge block in the generated root level model initialize function when all of the following conditions are true: Remove internal data zero initializaiton  is selected on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box In the Merge block parameter dialog, the Initial output  is specified as 0  (zero). The Merge block is not inside a conditionally executed subsystem The Merge block is connected to a Concat block  The attached model mmissing_merge_init.mdl  illustrates the modeling pattern that exposes this problem.
Workaround: The zero initialization elimination function in Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder is multi-staged. Download the attached zip archive and extract the P-code MATLAB script. To disable the part of this optimization causing the incorrect zero initialization elimination, call the MATLAB script disableExtendedZeroInitElimination with a 1 or true as an argument. The return value of disableExtendedZeroInitElimination is the previous setting. Example:>> disableExtendedZeroInitElimination(1)ans = 0Alternatively, you can disable this optimization by clearing Remove internal data zero initialization on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.?

Summary: Segmentation violation caused by self-reference to a model from a configurable subsystem block
Description: If you copy a configurable subsystem block to a model and change its active choice to a self-reference to the same model, this causes an inconsistent state. Any subsequent operations on the copied configurable subsystem block can cause a segmentation violation.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, do not change the active choice of a configurable subsystem to self-reference the model containing the block.

Summary: Passing Data Store Memory data by address in Stateflow chart crashes MATLAB
Description: In a Stateflow chart, passing Data Store Memory data to a custom C code function by address crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: Define a chart-parented local data with the same type, size, and complexity as the Data Store Memory data.Pass the address of this local data to the custom C function after assigning its value from Data Store Memory data.For example:my_local_data = my_dsm_data;myfcn(&my_local_data);my_dsm_data = my_local_data; % if "myfcn" modifies the input data.

Summary: Passing inf into textscan as the number of times to repeat a format does not give the correct results and may crash MATLAB.
Description: Using code such as: textscan('1;2;3','%f',inf,'delimiter',';') may cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Omit the repeat parameter, or read the entire file or string by setting the parameter to -1.

Summary: Code generation error when the build folder path name contains single quotes
Description: The following error occurs when generating code in a build folder for which the path name contains single quotes: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Matrix multiplication with mix of fixed-point vector input and scalar input
Description: It is possible that the generated code gives an incorrect answer for either of the following: A fixed point signal, which is a vector and cannot represent zero, is used with a Gain block specified for matrix multiplication and a scalar Gain parameter (or vice versa, a scalar signal and Gain block with a vector value). A Matrix Multiply block is used with a scalar and a vector input  All of the following conditions must be true in order to get a wrong answer: The block must be configured to perform a matrix multiplication The operands must be a scalar and a vector The type of the output signal must be unable to represent zero. For example: an unsigned 8-bit fixed-point type with a scaling of 1 and a bias of 3 has a real-world value range of 3 to 258 and cannot represent zero.
Workaround: Any of the following workarounds generates code that gives the same answer as Simulink:Use a Gain block, where Multiplication is Element-wise(K.*u).Replace a Matrix Multiply block by a Product block.Change the type of the output signal to one that can represent a real-world value of zero without rounding or overflow.

Summary: PolySpace Queue Manager display the status "running" even after the verification has finished.
Description: The PolySpace Queue Manager Interface continues to display the status "running," even after a verification has finished. This occurs when the Remote launcher has kept an entry for the previous version (R2009b) of the product in its configuration. An R2009b version of Polyspace Client can launch a verification, but this verification will stay in a 'running' mode and the CPU it was running on will not be reassigned after the verification completes. All other verifications are queued and "Follow Progress..." or "View Log File..." show that the verification has ended. Trying to stop the verification causes an error message like 'Error: the verification #id has completed before it could be killed'. In the log file associated to Queue Manager the following messages are displayed: Error: Invalid arguments returned by command CPU.ServerProgress().Error: ReceiveResult exception handler.  When this problem occurs, there is no way to remove the verification from the queue except by stopping the Queue Manager service and removing the verification manually on the server.
Workaround: To workaround the issue on Windows machines:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation.Locate your Polyspace installation by using environment variable %POLYSPACE_COMMON% (default location is c:\PolySpace\PolySpace_Common).Browse to %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLauncher folder.Download the attached *_win_*.zip file associated with this Bug Report and make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. The backup concerns bin\x86-mingw32\polyspaced-RL-R2010b-V21.cx86-mingw32.exe file.Stop the Queue Manager (QM) and each CPUs services using command 'services.msc>Polyspace Server' and "stop" button. Check that it does not remain any polyspaced-RL-* process using task manager (tick 'show process for all users').For each CPU and QM, extract the contents of the file to your %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLancher folder.Then restart on each CPU the service using the command 'services.msc>PolySpace Server' and "start" button. Restart QM in last.To workaround the issue on Linux machines:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation by using environment variable $POLYSPACE_COMMON (default location is /usr/local/PolySpace/PolySpace_Common).Browse to $POLYSPACE_COMMON/RemoteLauncher folder.Download the attached *_linux_*.zip file associated with this Bug Report and make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. The backup concerns bin/x86-linux/polyspaced-RL-R2010b-V21.cx86-linux file.Stop the Queue Manager (QM) and each CPUs daemons using command '/etc/init.d/polyspaced stop'. Check that it does not remain any polyspaced-RL-* process using 'ps -auxwww' command.For each CPU and QM, extract the contents of the file to your $POLYSPACE_COMMON/RemoteLauncher folder.Then restart on each CPU the service using the command '/etc/init.d/polyspaced start'. Restart QM in last.

Summary: The generated code for models with image processing blocks might not compile
Description: The generated C/C++ code might fail to compile for a model containing an image processing block, such as the From Video Device block.
Workaround: Increase the Maximum stack size (bytes) value in the Optimization pane of the models configuration parameters dialog.

Summary: Statistics displayed in the RTE view are incorrect when you apply filters after importing comments.
Description: After you import comments into verification results, activating filters causes problems for check statistics (the statistics given in the RTE view).

Summary: Error from right-clicking SimMechanics Ground block connected to Machine Environment block
Description: If you right-click a SimMechanics Ground block which is connected to a Machine Environment block, you see the following error: ??? Error using ==> sl_customization>getSimMechanicsMenu at 26 Machine Environment block (mask) does not have a parameter named 'PMImportedID'.

Summary: System Design Description report generates duplicate IDs when there are system requirements
Description: When you run the report, you get an error message similar to the following message: Java exception generating document: javax.xml.transform.TransformerException: org.apache.fop.apps.FOPException: The id "282b91ced80ee7b49ba3a7b3130226c6" already exists in this document Duplicate Ids are caused by the System and Requirement sections generating the same anchor ID for the subsystem.
Workaround: Stop MATLAB.Back up matlab\toolbox\shared\rptgen\@rptgen\@rpt_looper\execute.p.Download and copy the attached execute.p file to the location in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Possible segmentation fault when a model uses a variable-step solver and automatic rate transition insertion
Description: The model option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is intended for use in a multitasking model. Updating a model for which Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is selected might generate a segmentation fault if all of the following conditions are true: The model uses a variable-step solver, which implies single-tasking operation. The model has a continuous sample time and a discrete sample time, but no block running at a ZOH (zero-order hold) continuous sample time. Rate transition occurs between a continuous block and a subrate discrete block.  Under these conditions, the software should disallow the use of the model option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer .
Workaround: To work around this issue, clear the model option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer on the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. If appropriate, you can manually insert Rate Transition blocks in your model.

Summary: Unsaved changes not reported for model parameters "ModifiedByFormat" or "ModifiedDateFormat"
Description: You can define the format for the derived parameters "LastModifiedBy" and "LastModifiedDate", by setting "ModifiedByFormat" and "ModifiedDateFormat" in the Model Properties dialog.When you change these format parameters, Simulink does not mark the model as "dirty" (that is, having unsaved changes). As a result, if you save the model without any other changes, Simulink fails to increment the model's version number.
Workaround: To ensure that Simulink increments the model's version number when you save, first set the model's "dirty" flag to "on" as follows:>> set_param(bdroot,'Dirty','on')?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling reusable system which accepts bus, vector, or matrix input
Description: Code generation of a child reusable subsystem might generate incorrect code when its input is a bus defined with an ImportedExternPtr  storage class. In this case, the parent might apply the address-of operator when passing the bus argument to the child subsystem. For example, child(&busptr, ...) when the generated code should be child(busptr, ...) The C compiler produces a warning or error about the signature and call site mismatch. ?
Workaround: Use ImportedExtern storage class for a bus signal that is input to a reusable subsystem.

Summary: Warning when "slupdate" fails to replace the deprecated Simulink Extras PID Controller block
Description: If the Simulink Library (simulink.mdl) is not open or loaded, and you run slupdate on a model that contains the deprecated Simulink Extras PID Controller block, then the block is not updated and you see a warning similar to this:  Warning: Simulink has encountered a problem while updating 'PIDsample/PID Controller'. Verify the settings and functionality of this block.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, ensure that the Simulink Library is open before running slupdate by running the command:open_system('simulink')

Summary: S-Function Builder with buses returns error when running in accelerated mode
Description: S-Function Builder with buses returns the following error when running in accelerated mode: Error: File: CtrlSw_EFI.tlc Line: 95 Column: 48 Undefined identifier from_pmBUS Error: File: C:\Program Files (x86)\MATLAB\R2010a\rtw\c\tlc\lib\blocklib.tlc L

Summary: Global Optimization Toolbox does not appear in the Toolboxes On Path setting in deploytool
Description: When I open the Settings  panel from a deploytool  project, I cannot see the Global Optimization Toolbox listed as a toolbox in the Toolboxes on Path  tab.
Workaround: Select all toolboxes.This guarantees that the compilable functionality of Global Optimization toolbox needed by your application is available in the compilable application.

Summary: Verification halts with an Assert_Failure message when generic package contains absolute addressing.
Description: PolySpace aborts compilation with an assertion failure when a certain combination of absolute addressing inside generic package and procedure is used. Error message:================================================= rte-gnat1 fatal error (Assert_Failure)== Detected at <package>.adb:4:1===============================================  N_Package_Body (Node_Id=1388) (source)Rewritten: original node = N_Package_Body (Node_Id=2441)Parent = N_Compilation_Unit (Node_Id=1371)Sloc = 100 simple_generic.adb:4:1Defining_Unit_Name = N_Defining_Identifier "<package>" (Entity_Id=2365)Declarations = List (List_Id=-99999894)Corresponding_Spec = N_Defining_Identifier "<package>" (Entity_Id=2351)  compilation abandoned  ?
Workaround: The attached files contain a patch for release R2009b, for both Linux and Windows OS. Please read instructions inside zip file.

Summary: Generated code for model with multi-word fixed-point type fails to compile
Description: The generated C/C++ code for a model might not compile under the following conditions: The model contains an Arithmetic block with a multi-word fixed-point type as an input and output signal type One of the inputs of the Arithmetic block is unconnected, or connected to a Ground block  A multi-word fixed-point type is a type that can not be represented on the current host platform (e.g. 64-bit on a 32-bit platform or 128-bit on a 64-bit platform).
Workaround: Avoid unconnected input signals by using a Constant block with zero value instead of a Ground block.

Summary: Failure to generate code for model containing a user-written S-function block with custom data type outport and infinite sample time
Description: Real-Time Workshop might fail to generate code for a model under the following conditions: The model contains an user-written S-function block The outport signal type of the S-function block has a custom data type The S-function block has a constant (infinite) sample time
Workaround: Avoid using custom data type signals specified with a constant (infinite) sample time type. Assign a discrete or continuous sample time to the customer written S-function block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Ground block with enumerated type might generate wrong code.
Description: The values of an enumerated type consist of the following: a position in the type, called an ordinal value; a name; and an underlying integer value. The value of the Ground block is expected to be the underlying integer value of the default enumerated value, which is the first element of the enumeration (unless you redefined the default enumerated value). The value of the Ground block is correct if the ordinal and underlying values match. However, the Ground block value is wrong if the default underlying value is different than the default ordinal value. In the following example, myMode uses the underlying value of OFF , which is 1 , to select the enumerated value with ordinality 1, which is ON . Therefore, myMode  returns the underlying value of ON , which is 2 . classdef(Enumeration) myMode < Simulink.IntEnumType   enumeration      OFF(1) /* default value */      ON(2)   endend In addition, Simulink asserts if the underlying integer value of the default enumerated value is not an ordinal value in the type. In the following example, OFF is the default and its underlying value 10 is not an ordinal value in myMode. classdef(Enumeration) myMode < Simulink.IntEnumType   enumeration      OFF(10) /* default value */      ON(20)   endend ?
Workaround: Use a Constant block instead of a Ground block as a source. The value of the Constant block should be the underlying integer value of the default element. For example,classdef(Enumeration) myMode < Simulink.IntEnumType   enumeration      OFF(1)      ON(2)   endendUse a Constant block with value 1 if OFF is the default and use 2 if ON is the default.

Summary: PurelyIntegerCode property cannot be changed in RTWConfig dialog box.
Description: In a new MATLAB session, if you invoke the emlc  command before creating an emlcoder.RTWConfig('ert')  object, you cannot modify that object's PurelyIntegerCode  property in the RTWConfig dialog box.
Workaround: Replace toolbox/emlcoder/emlcoder/private/emlckernel.p with the attached version after backing up the original file. When creating RTWConfig objects, always specify the ert or grt parameter explicitly as emlcoder.RTWConfig('ert') or emlcoder.RTWConfig('grt').

Summary: Using floating-point override on fixed-point subsystems containing Discrete Filter, Discrete FIR Filter or Discrete Transfer Function blocks may crash MATLAB?
Description: MATLAB? may close unexpectedly when you simulate a model in which all of the following conditions are satisfied: The model contains at least one Discrete Filter, Discrete FIR Filter, or Discrete Transfer Function block. Data type override mode is turned on. The input to one or more of the Discrete Filter, Discrete FIR Filter, or Discrete Transfer Function blocks is different than the specified data type override setting.
Workaround: Place a Data Type Conversion block before each Discrete Filter, Discrete FIR Filter and Discrete Transfer Function block that is inside of a fixed-point subsystem. You can find the Data Type Conversion block in the Simulink/Signal Attributes library.

Summary: dataset/export produces a SAS transport (.xpt) file that SAS cannot read.
Description: Sometimes the export  method of a dataset array creates a SAS transport (.xpt) file that SAS cannot read, though the file can be imported back into MATLAB.
Workaround: It may be possible to export a tab-delimited file from MATLAB, and then read that into SAS. Alternatively, you can install the files attached to this report.Installation instructionsLog into your computer using an account with Administrator privilegesDownload the workaround files associated with this Bug ReportLocate the download - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent stepsLocate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent stepsExit MATLABFollow additional steps, depending on your operating systemOn Windows:Open the downloaded fileExtract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> directoryOn Linux and Macintosh:Type the following in a shell (on Linux) or a Terminal (on Macintosh) window:       cd <MATLAB>       tar -xvf <DOWNLOAD>/<downloaded file>

Summary: Heads of arrow annotations display notches at large point sizes
Description: Arrow, textarrow and doublearrow annotations created using the annotation function, the Plot Edit toolbar, or the Figure Pallete Plot Tool can display notches at their points and tips. This effect is particularly noticeable when the LineWidth  property is large (5 points or more). The appearance is different or absent depending on how you set the HeadStyle , HeadWidth , and HeadLength  arrow properties. ?

Summary: Some GigE Vision cameras may be missing some properties for gain, black level, white clip, or balance ratio.
Description: Some GigE Vision cameras that have properties for gain, black level, white clip, or balance ratio may be missing those device specific properties on the selected source of the videoinput object. For example, on a camera that supports properties such as Gain , GainRaw , or BlackLevelRaw , that does not also have a GainSelector or BlackLevelSelector, the Gain, GainRaw and BlackLevelRaw properties will be missing the properties on the source object. This patch fixes the issue. ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Merge block with unconnected input port might cause segmentation fault during model compilation
Description: When a model contains a Merge block with an unconnected input port, model compilation generates a segmentation fault if one of the following two conditions is met: 1. The Merge block is used to merge the outputs of a set of If Action Subsystems 2. The Merge block is used to merge the outputs of a set of Enabled Subsystems
Workaround: Connect all input ports of the Merge block.

Summary: Version 1.0 of Texas Instruments "2802x C/C++ Header Files and Peripheral Examples" unavailable from TI. Errors building models with more recent versions.
Description: The required version 1.0 of the Texas Instruments "2802x C/C++ Header Files and Peripheral Examples" cannot be found at the Texas Instruments site. If, during a model build, the user attempts to specify the installation folder for "2802x C/C++ Header Files and Peripheral Examples" corresponding to a more recent version, the product displays the following error message: Invalid folder. One of the following files(s) could not be located in the installation directory" DSP2802x.txt
Workaround: The user can use a more recent version which has not been tested with the MathWorks product.To set installation folder correctly:Define the following environment variable in your computer environment: DSP2802x_INSTALLDIRSet the value of the DSP2802x_INSTALLDIR to the full path of "2802x C/C++ Header Files and Peripheral Examples" folder.For example, to use version 1.28 from its default installation location, you would set the value to: C:\TI\controlSUITE\device_support\f2802x\v128Start MATLABNote: At this time, version 1.27 is not supported due to a missing IQmath.lib file.

Summary: Polyspace Metrics does not correctly import MISRA-C justifications and comments from header files
Description: After justifying and commenting MISRA-C violations in header files, and then importing them into Polyspace metrics, no MISRA-C comments or justifications appear.  Comments for MISRA-C violations in the C files are imported correctly.  This problem occurs only for header files.

Summary: Visualization of SimMechanics model not changed after model update and with visualization open, after changes to STL files referenced by Body blocks
Description: This bug arises if you have the visualization for a SimMechanics model open, and one or more of the bodies have their visualization set to reference external (STL) graphics files. If you change the contents of one or more of the graphics files (from a CAD export, for example) and perform an update diagram on the model (Ctrl-D), the visualization is not updated and continues to follow the old graphics file.
Workaround: Close the model and re-open it. This refreshes the visualization to follow the updated STL files.

Summary: Models using Model Reference Multi-Instance Normal Mode may experience SegV from Simulink.BlockDiagram.getChecksum
Description: Simulink.BlockDiagram.getChecksum may throw a segmentation violation if a model uses Model Reference Multi-Instance Normal Mode.
Workaround: Change the model, as follows, so that it does not make use of Model Reference Multi-Instance Normal Mode. Specifically, change the simulation mode of Model blocks in the model hierarchy from Normal mode to Accelerator mode until at most one instance of any given model is referenced in Normal mode.

Summary: Computer reboots unexpectedly when using Real-Time Windows Target on Microsoft Windows 7
Description: When using Real-Time Windows Target on Microsoft Windows 7, some computers might do one of the following: Randomly and unexpectedly reboot Stop responding and display a blue screen
Workaround: As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files. These files are named according to the release they support.Select and download the appropriate workaround file for your release.Locate the downloaded file - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Type matlabroot to locate your MATLAB installation - this directory is referred to as <MATLABROOT> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Navigate to <DOWNLOAD> and open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to <MATLABROOT>.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type rtwintgt -setup to reinstall the Real-Time Windows Target kernel.?September 9, 2010: attachment_663215_10b_win32_2010-09-09.zip: initial version, works on R2010b onlySeptember 9, 2010: attachment_663215_10a_win32_2010-09-09: initial version, works on R2010a onlySeptember 9, 2010: attachment_663215_9b_win32_2010-09-09: initial version, works on R2009b onlySeptember 9, 2010: attachment_663215_9a_win32_2010-09-09: initial version, works on R2009a only

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor  Create Project (step 4.1) gives error message when adding  multiple files using Add button.
Description: HDL Workflow Advisor Create Project  (step 4.1) fails and displays an error message if multiple files are selected through clicking the Add   button, as follows: In the HDL Workflow Advisor, right-click on  Create Project  (step 4.1)  and select Run to Selected Task  from the context menu. After the HDL Workflow Advisor completes all tasks up to 4.1, click the Add  button to the right of the Additional source files  edit field.  In the file browser, select 2 or more HDL files. Then click Open . The  HDL Workflow Advisor displays an error message:   Undefined function or method 'eq' for input arguments of type 'cell'
Workaround: To work around the problem, you can do either of the following:Using the Add button, select and add files one at a time.Enter the desired path and file names directly into the Additional source files edit field.

Summary: Using eml.cstructname results in the error: 'The C struct name cannot be set on function inputs...'
Description: Generating C code from MATLAB code that applies eml.cstructname conditionally using 'if strcmp(eml.target, 'rtw')' on a top-level function input results in the error message "The C struct name cannot be set on function inputs..." This error occurs because Embedded MATLAB must infer the properties of the top-level function inputs, but it cannot do so in this case because it does not recognize 'eml.cstructname' inside a conditional. Although you can use the following idioms to speed up (non-compiled) MATLAB execution, you cannot generate C code from this MATLAB code. function y = myfunction(y) %#emlif strcmp(eml.target, 'rtw')  eml.cstructname(y, 'mystruct');end...  or function y = myfunction(y) %#emlif ~isempty(eml.target)  eml.cstructname(y, 'mystruct');end...

Summary: eml.cstructname has no effect with nested struct constructor
Description: During code generation, eml.cstructname  might be ignored if used to set the C type name of a structure created with a nested struct  constructor. As a result, the C type structure name may differ from what is expected. For example, the second eml.cstructname  statement in the following code has no effect because amnu.launch  was created using a nested struct  constructor: ammu = struct('launch', struct('type',uint32(0),'delay',0));eml.cstructname(ammu,'ammu');eml.cstructname(ammu.launch,'launch_struct'); ?
Workaround: Split nested struct constructors into multiple lines. For example, rewrite the above code as:ammu_launch = struct('type',uint32(0),'delay',0); eml.cstructname(ammu_launch,'launch_struct');ammu = struct('launch', ammu_launch); eml.cstructname(ammu,'ammu');?

Summary: Polyspace installation fails with the message: 'the installation of PolySpace is finished but some errors have been detected. for detail see C:\PolySpace\..\..install_log.xm'
Description: At the end of a Polyspace installation, you could see the following message saying that installation has failed:  'the installation of PolySpace R2010b is finished but some errors have been detected. for detail see C:\PolySpace\..\..install_log.xm '.  When looking in the '..install_log.xml'  file, a message surrounded by two '<warning>' tags reports: 'There was a problem deleting C:\PolySpace\PolySpace_Common\ReportGenerator\MCR ' This happens on Windows systems  when you install Polyspace software on top of an existing installation. For instance, if you have installed a Polyspace pre-release and then you try to install the official release on top of it.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, uninstall the previous release and restart the installation process. Another way to do this is to manually remove %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\ReportGenarator\MCR folder and sub-folders, then reinstall the software on top of the previous install.

Summary: Using Probe block without any output port causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When the Probe block is used in a model without any output port, running the simulation causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Adjust the Probe block to have at least one output port.

Summary: When using the "follow progress" feature, the following error appears: "An error has been detected: Remote verification with ID: <id> is not accessible in the spooler"
Description: When you run a sever verification with the option -add-to-results-repository  selected, the "Follow progress" window displays an error after the results are downloaded. This error is: An error has been detected:Remote verification with ID: <id> is not accessible in the spooler This message is printed at the end of the log, and so shown by the gaphical interface. This message is safe and can be ignored.
Workaround: Attachment to bug report https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/669986 includes work around for this issue.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Nondeterministic simulation results for asynchronous tasks with Rate Transition blocks and signal storage reuse
Description: Simulation of a model that contains multiple asynchronous function-call subsystems and Rate Transition blocks might give incorrect results if all of the following conditions are true: The model option Signal storage reuse  is selected. This allows Rate Transition block output buffers potentially to be reused by another Rate Transition block. The output buffers of a Rate Transition block that is connected to one asynchronous function-call subsystem are reused for a Rate Transition block driven by another asynchronous function-call subsystem.  Under these conditions, an incorrect value might be generated in the Rate Transition block output during simulation. (Note:  Generated code behavior is correct.)
Workaround: To work around this bug, clear the model option Signal storage reuse on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using a builtin name as a variable in a script.
Description: MATLAB may crash when using a builtin name as a variable in a script, e.g.for t=1:2  gamma(t)= 0;endc = gamma(1);
Workaround: Use names for variables that do not conflict with MATLAB's builtin function names, e.g.for t=1:2  mygamma(t)= 0;endc = mygamma(1);

Summary: getinputstruct returns error for a model that has inputs with scalar dimension of 2 or greater
Description: The getinputstruct  command returns an error when used with an operating point of a model that has a scalar multi-dimensional Inport block at the root level. The error occurs if you enter a scalar value of 2 or greater in the Port dimensions field of the Inport block dialog box.
Workaround: When you set the inport dimensions for a multi-dimensional input signal, do not use a scalar value. Instead, use a vector value that describes the number of rows and columns of the signal at the inport. For example, if the input signal is a column vector of two elements, enter the value [2 1] in the Port dimensions field of the Inport block dialog box.

Summary: Requirements report fails to correctly include links to DocBlock contents
Description: Creating links from Simulink model objects to the contents of RTF DocBlock blocks in the same or another Simulink model is a new feature in R2010a. Links are highlighted and navigated correctly, but creating a requirements report for a model that includes such links produces the following warnings: Warning: Unresolved relative path: ModelName:[some number].rtf In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\+rmiut\full_path.p>full_path at 44  In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\+rmisl\locateFile.p>locateFile at 18  In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\@RptgenRMI\doc2path.p>doc2path at 8  In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\@RptgenRMI\@ReqDocTable\execute.p>execute at 33  The Document Table at the top of the report shows the following errors : ERROR: unable to locate ModelName:[same number].rtf  Clicking any such link in a Document Table in the requirements report results in the "File or URL Not Found " message from the browser, or current working folder window may open depending on the browser. Similar links in Requirmements Tables in the body of the Report will also not work. ?
Workaround: Installation instructions (Windows only)Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. Use R2010a or R2010b patch depending on your installed MATLAB version.Make a backup copy of each of the *.m files listed in the .zip file and/or of each corresponding *.p file. Remove the originals.Extract the files from the .zip file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Windows Systems: If you are on Windows 7, run "WinZip" as administrator by right-clicking on the "WinZip" executable and selecting "Run as Administrator", otherwise go on to the next step.Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.NOTE: This patch does not fix this issue for Watcom compiler users.August 25, 2010: attachment_661855_10b_win7_2010-08-25.zip: initial version, works on R2010b and Windows 7 only

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer returned when using a vector of fixed-point or Simulink.IntEnumType initial values
Description: In Stateflow, the initial value for data objects can be set in the Model Explorer using a scalar or vector value. When setting the initial value of a data object using either a vector of type fixed-point or Simulink.IntEnumType, the value is not correctly preserved. For example, if a user sets the initial value of a local data object to be: [fi(1, 1, 16, 0) fi(2, 1, 16, 0)] then the actual value being used will be: [1 1]  instead of [1 2]  In other words, the value of the first element will be duplicated for all the elements of the vector or matrix.
Workaround: The workaround is to cast the initial value vectors of type fixed-point or Simulink.IntEnumType to double.For instance, in the case of a Simulink.IntEnumType class called Colors with 'Red' and 'Blue' enumerations, replace the following:[Colors.Red Colors.Blue]withdouble([Colors.Red Colors.Blue])

Summary: F2802x / F2803x Serial Transmit does not transmit all of the data when the baud rate is higher.
Description: The F2802x / F2803x Serial Transmit block interprets the transmit FIFO level incorrectly. The maximum capacity of the FIFO is 4 words instead of 16. As a result, higher baud rates can cause a FIFO full condition and data loss.
Workaround: The patch file, patch_661302.zip, applies to releases R2009b, R2010a and R2010b.Download the attached files, attachment_661302_20110202.zip and patch_661302.zip.Extract patch_installer.m from attachment_661302_20110202.zip.In MATLAB, set Current Folder to the location of patch_installer.m and patch_661302.zip. (e.g., "C:\Users\username\Downloads")At the MATLAB command prompt, enter: patch_installer('patch_661302.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions displayed on the MATLAB command line.Reboot MATLAB.

Workaround: Download the attached isfi.m file to the following location:$matlabroot/toolbox/commblks/commblks.

Summary: A function-call port in a referenced model that has the States when enabling parameter set to inherit may generate incorrect code
Description: The results from simulation do not match the results from code generation for a function-call subsytem in a referenced model if all  of these conditions exist: There is a Model block in a conditionally executed subsystem (such as an enabled subsystem). The Model block references a model that: Contains a function-call subsytem whose function-call port has the States when enabling  parameter set to inherit  Uses an ERT-based system target file (STF) Has the Configuration Parameters  > Optimization  > Optimize initialization code for model reference  parameter turned on
Workaround: For all function-call ports, set the States when enabling parameter to either held or reset.

Summary: MATLAB crashes while reading some H.264 encoded QuickTime movie (.mov) files using mmreader
Description: MATLAB crashes when you use mmreader  to read QuickTime movies with these characteristics: H.264 encoding   "Current Size" set to a width and height different from the native size of the movie   The following code exhibits the behavior:    obj = mmreader('myfile.mov');    img = read(obj, 1);
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog into your computer using an account with Administrator privilegesDownload the workaround files associated with this Bug ReportLocate the download - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent stepsLocate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent stepsExit MATLABType the following in a Terminal window:cd <MATLAB>tar ?xvf <DOWNLOAD>/<downloaded file>

Summary: Running MCR Installer on 64-bit Linux Centos system with LANG set to ja_JP causes null pointer exception.
Description: Running the MCR Installer on a 64-bit Centos Linux system may cause a null pointer exception and prevent a successful installation when the LANG is set to ja_JP.
Workaround: To work around this issue, change LANG to en_US.UTF-8. This change will enable you to install the MCR in English without error.

Description: If you enable Task Profiling  in the model Configuration Parameters, and then build the model for a processor in the Texas Intruments 2804x family of processors, the code generation software produces the following error message: ### Writing source file mAllC2000Procs_data.c ### Writing source file mAllC2000Procs_main.c ### TLC code generation complete. Error: cslLib_C2000_profileReadTimer: Unknown processor family "2804x".
