Extract patch_installer.m from attachment_660896_20110223.zip.3. In MATLAB, set Current Folder to the location of patch_installer.m and the patch zip file. (e.g., "C:\Users\username\Downloads")4. At the MATLAB command prompt, enter the commands that matches your patch file:patch_installer('patch_660896_9b.zip', 'install')patch_installer('patch_660896_10a.zip', 'install')patch_installer('patch_660896_10b.zip', 'install')5. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code for arrays whose size is determined by one of multiple outputs returned by a function
Description: MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink might generate incorrect code for an array when its size is determined by one of multiple outputs returned by a function. For example,   [sz, ...] = foo(...);  m = zeros(sz);
Workaround: Do not return the size of an array as one of multiple outputs to a function call.

Summary: Using a mask parameter for port data logging fields can cause run-time update diagram to fail
Description: Updating a diagram during simulation can fail if both of these conditions apply: For a signal inside a masked subsystem, you use a mask parameter to set either the Limit data points to last  or Decimation  fields in the Signal Properties dialog box. The Configuration Parameters -> Optimization -> Inline parameters  option is checked.
Workaround: Set the Limit data points to last or Decimation fields to numeric values or expressions that do not refer to mask parameters.

Summary: Selecting certain Help menu entries for Operating Points and Linearization Task produces errors
Description: In the Operating Points and Linearization Task nodes of the Control and Estimation Tools Manager, selecting one of the following entries from the Help menu produces an error dialog box: What is Linearization Linearizing Models Selecting Analysis I/Os Inspecting and Validating Results Creating Custom Views Exporting and Saving Results  The error states that the selected help topic "is not found or help is not installed ." ?

Summary: The MSLOWESS and MALOWESS functions use the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) instead of the median of absolute residuals when using the robust option.
Description: A robust implementation of local regression using weighted linear least squares is available when the 'ROBUSTITERATIONS'  input argument is set to 1 or larger in the function MSLOWESS  or when the 'ROBUST'  input argument is set to TRUE  in the function MALOWESS . Multiple iterations of this algorithm prevent deviant points distorting the smoothing points. To emphasize points that are not outliers, the literature recommends using a bisquare function dependent on the median of the absolute value of the residuals, MEDIAN(ABS(res)) . However, the functions MSLOWESS  and MALOWESS  use median absolute deviation of the residuals MEDIAN(ABS(res-MEDIAN(res))) .
Workaround: Installation instructions1. Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges2. Download the workaround files associated with this Bug Report3. Locate the download - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps4. Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps5. Exit MATLAB6. Follow additional steps, depending on your operating systemOn Windows:Open the downloaded fileExtract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> directoryOn Linux and Macintosh:Type the following in a shell (on Linux) or a Terminal (on Macintosh) window:cd <MATLAB>tar -xvf <DOWNLOAD>/<downloaded file>

Summary: Use of a Simulink.AliasType to a Simulink.AliasType defining an AUTOSAR builtin data type results in a redefinition of typedef compilation error when used with code from an AUTOSAR RTE generator
Description: For code generation, when a Simulink.AliasType  is used to reference a Simulink.AliasType  that defines an AUTOSAR builtin data type, the software places the typedef  statement for this data type in model_types.h . An AUTOSAR RTE generator will place this typedef in Rte_Type.h . When code from the model and the RTE generator are compiled together a compilation error will be produced, for example: error: redefinition of typedef 'UInt8'
Workaround: To work around the issue, remove the typedef statement from model_types.h.

Summary: .NET event handlers are not removed by the delete function.
Description: ? If you add a .NET event handler, delete it, and then add it again, the MATLAB event callback function gets called multiple times. This happens because the MATLAB event handler was not removed from the .NET event delegate's invocation target list by the delete  function. The following example demonstrates this behavior. C# class definition:     namespace MyNamespace    {        public delegate void MyDelegate(object src, EventArgs args);        public class MyClass        {            public event MyDelegate MyEvent;            public void DoSomething()            {                if (MyEvent != null)                {                    MyEvent(this, new EventArgs());                }            }        }    } MATLAB event handler:     function MyEventHandler(evtSrc, evtArgs)        disp('FROM: MyEventHandler');    end In MATLAB do the following: obj = MyNamespace.MyEventSource();% Add event listenerl = addlistener(obj, 'MyEvent', @MyEventHandler);% Fire .NET eventobj.DoSomething();    FROM: MyEventHandler% Delete event listenerdelete(l);% Add event listener second timel = addlistener(obj, 'MyEvent', @MyEventHandler);% Fire .NET event. MATLAB event handler is called twice.obj.DoSomething();    FROM: MyEventHandler    FROM: MyEventHandler

Summary: Simulink might not save and restore complete simulation state (SimState) for data with the nonvirtual bus data type
Description: If a Simulink model contains simulation state for data with the nonvirtual bus data type, Simulink might not correctly save and restore the complete SimState.
Workaround: As a workaround, do not use nonvirtual bus data types in the model.

Summary: GigE Vision camera(s) may not be detected on Linux
Description: Due to a bug, GigE Vision cameras can only be detected on one network interface (i.e., an Ethernet card). Users of only one network interface will not notice any issues (if the camera is on the network that the PC is on, or the PC is not connected to a network and instead is only connected to a camera). However, users of more than one network interface may not see their single camera. Alternatively, if you have more than one camera on more than one interface, you may be able to detect at most one camera. For bandwidth reasons, it is recommended that each GigE Vision camera be on its own interface if they are to be used concurrently. ? ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: You receive an error message when you change a sample time parameter that you defined as a workspace variable in the powergui block.
Description: You may receive an error message about Illegal rate transition or inconsistent sample time if you change a sample time parameter that you defined as a workspace variable in the powergui black.For example, in the SimPowerSystem example ac6_example_simplified, the sample time is set to the workspace variable Ts in the powergui. Ts is defined in the workspace as Ts = 75e-6. You simulate the model and it runs without generating any error messages. You change the sample time to Ts=1e-6 at thecommand line, then simulate the model. You receive errors similar to: Illegal rate transition found. Sample time [1e-006 0] of 'ac6_example_simplified/PM Synchronous Motor Drive/Braking chopper/Proportional controller/Logical Operator' at input port 2 is different from sample time [7.499999999999999e-005 0] of its source 'ac6_example_simplified/PM Synchronous Motor Drive/Braking chopper/Proportional controller/Data Type Conversion5' at output port 1. A Rate Transition must be inserted between them.   Warning: Inconsistent sample times. Sample time ([7.5e-005, 0]) of 'ac6_example_simplified/PM Synchronous Motor Drive/Three-phase Inverter/Product1' is slower than sample time ([1e-006, 0]) at input port 2.
Workaround: Run the simulation a second time for the model to apply the sample time change.

Summary: Simulink warning after copying and pasting Joint Spring and Damper block within the same or to a different model
Description: A Joint Spring and Damper block is connected to a joint. When this block is copied and pasted within the same model or to a different model, Simulink issues the following warning at the command line: Warning: Error evaluating 'CopyFcn' callback of Joint Spring & Damper block (mask) 'myModel/Joint Spring & Damper'. Attempt to modify block in a linked subsystem. This can only be done by the block or its parent through their mask initialization code. Warning: Attempt to modify block in a linked subsystem. This can only be done by the block or its parent through their mask initialization code.
Workaround: This warning does not affect simulation results and can be safely ignored.

Summary: The Battery block produces an error when the simulation type of Powergui is changed from Continuous to Discrete, or from Discrete to Continuous
Description: If you have a model with a Battery block and simulate it, and then open the Powergui block and change Simulation type  from Continuous  to Discrete  (or from Discrete  to Continuous ), then when you try to simulate the model again you get an error similar to the following: *Error evaluating 'InitFcn' callback of Battery block (mask) 'power_battery/200 volts, 6.5 Ah Ni-MH battery '. Error in 'power_battery/200 volts, 6.5 Ah Ni-MH battery ': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated.*Error in 'power_battery/200 volts, 6.5 Ah Ni-MH battery ': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated. *SubSystem block does not have a parameter named 'Units'.
Workaround: After getting the error, close the error window and restart the simulation. The model will run correctly.

Summary: permute has no effect on symbolic arrays.
Description: The permute  function has no effect on symbolic arrays. For example: syms a b c A = [a b c]; permute(A,[2 1]) ans = [ a, b, c]

Summary: Unable to see test results after running Test Connectivity in Admin Center.
Description: When running Test Connectivity in Admin Center, the test results disappears after the run is complete. This problem occurs only when the host names do not match exactly with respect to case sensitivity.
Workaround: As a workaround, be sure the host names match exactly, including letter case.

Summary: Incorrect linearization result when using Sum or Matrix Concatenation blocks with variable-size input signals
Description: Simulink produces an incorrect linearization result if all  of the following conditions are true: A Sum block or Matrix Concatenation block is on the linearization path. This Sum or Matrix Concatenation block has multiple input signals and at least one input is a variable-size signal. At linearization time, the current size of this variable-size input signal is less than the maximum possible size for this signal.  ?

Summary: matlabFunction returns incorrect results if you use theta or other MuPAD names and the parameter 'vars'.
Description: If you call matlabFunction  for an expression that contains the variable theta  (or other MuPAD names) and specify the parameter 'vars' , the call returns incorrect results. For example, the following call returns incorrect code for theta : syms theta xmatlabFunction(theta + x,'vars',{[theta x]})ans =     @(in1)theta+in1(:,2)
Workaround: Use another variable name, for example, the name theta_:syms theta_ xmatlabFunction(theta_ + x,'vars',{[theta_ x]})ans =     @(in1)in1(:,1)+in1(:,2)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Optimization combines two consecutive shift operations into one
Description: The code generator incorrectly optimizes the following expression: (x << 16U) << 16U The optimization converts it to: x << 32U In C, the semantics of shifting a 32-bit integer by 32 bits or more is undefined. This optimization might cause the generated code to compute incorrect answers. To determine whether your generated code is affected by this bug, use the following command to find all shifts by a constant: grep -E "(>>|<<)(=)?[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" *.c If none of the shift distances exceeds the bit size of the operand, then your code is not affected by the bug.

Summary: Embedded IDE Link build process errors out with Property 'configurePIL' does not exist.
Description: If your model satisfies both of the following conditions: the model is configured to generate Generic Real-Time code (i.e., System target file  parameter is set to idelink_grt.tlc ) the model uses model reference (i.e., contains Model blocks referencing other models)  then the software produces the following error message: Property 'configurePIL' does not exist.
Workaround: The attached patch is for the R2010b release.To install the patch:Download the attached files, attachment_659334_20110202.zip and patch_659334.zip.Extract patch_installer.m from attachment_659334_20110202.zip.In MATLAB, set Current Folder to the location of patch_installer.m and patch_659334.zip. Summary: Target Preferences block ignores changes to processor setting in "Initialize Configuration Parameters" dialog
Description: When you add a Target Preferences block to a model, the software displays an Initialize Configuration Parameters  dialog. If you change the Processor  setting in the Initialize Configuration Parameters  dialog, the Target Preference block ignores the new setting and uses the default processor value instead.

Summary: Global Optimization Toolbox solvers do not always return improved solutions found by their hybrid function.
Description: A hybrid function optionally runs after the ga , gamultiobj  and simulannealbnd  solvers finish. If the hybrid function returns an improved solution, then the solvers should return the improved solution instead of the original solution. However, the hybrid function feature has a bug whereby solvers might not return an improved solution. In particular, if the hybrid function returns with exit flag 0  or smaller, then the solvers do not return the hybrid solution, regardless of its quality.
Workaround: There is no definitive workaround for this bug. However, you can investigate whether a hybrid function can improve on the solution returned from ga, gamultiobj or simulannealbnd as shown below.A common instance of this bug is when the hybrid function stops by exceeding the maximum number of function evaluations or iterations. If you suspect this is the case, do not set a hybrid function in your call to ga, gamultiobj or simulannealbnd. Instead, use the returned solution as the initial point in a separate call to the hybrid function. This is illustrated in the following example:% Call GA without a hybrid function [gaSoln, gaFval, gaExitflag] = ga(@myObjectiveFcn, 2); % Call the hybrid function from the GA solution [hybridSoln, hybridFval, hybridExitflag] = fminunc(@myObjectiveFcn, gaSoln); You can compare the two solutions using the outputs from the respective calls to the solvers. Furthermore, you can check whether the number of function evaluations or iterations has been exceeded in the hybrid function by inspecting hybridExitFlag and any exit message returned.

Summary: Output constraints in referenced models are not available when computing operating points for parent model
Description: When you place an output constraint on a signal of a referenced model, the constraint is not available for computing an operating point for the parent model. If you are using the Control and Estimation Tools Manager GUI, the constrained output does not appear in the Outputs tab of the Compute Operating Points tool. If you are using the operspec  command, operspec  disregards the output constraint when creating the operating point specification.
Workaround: To compute an operating point with a constraint on block output for a signal inside of a model reference:Bring the signal in the referenced model up to the top parent model.Add the output constraint to the signal in the top parent model.Compute the operating point using one of the following:The Compute Operating Points tab of the Operating Points task of the Contol and Estimation Tools ManagerThe findop command

Summary: Icon drawing code cannot use Simulink parameter objects passed through the mask
Description: You cannot use a Simulink parameter object in icon drawing commands, even if both  of these conditions are satisfied: The parameter object is passed through the mask as the value of one of the mask parameters. The parameter object is not modified by the mask initialization commands.  For example, if the Value  property of a Simulink parameter object P  is 5, the following command does not display the value 11 on the mask:  disp(P+6) A warning is issued and question marks are displayed on the mask.
Workaround: In Versions R2010a and R2010b, if you want to use a Simulink parameter object P in the icon drawing commands, instead of "P", use "P.Value".

Summary: Code generated for top models might not call the initialize, enable, or disable methods of referenced models
Description: Generated code for the top model in a model reference hierarchy does not call the initialize, enable, or disable methods for referenced models that meet all of the following conditions: The only block inside of a referenced model that contains one of these methods is an S-Function block. The S-Function is inlined and uses LibIsModelReferenceRTWTarget  (or similar method) in its TLC to cause code to be generated for this method only for the model reference code generation target. The referenced model is set up to run in Accelerator mode during simulation.
Workaround: Ensure that the SIM and code generate targets either both have a body or neither has a body for the initialize, enable, and disable methods.For example, instead of:%if LibIsModelReferenceRTWTarget()    [your code here] %endifuse the following to add a comment to the model reference SIM target:%if LibIsModelReferenceRTWTarget()    [your code here] %else    /* SIM target comment */ %endif

Summary: Potential regular expression matches may be missed under certain conditions.
Description: If a regular expression begins with a choice of literal strings (e.g., str1|str2), and one of those strings (str1) is contained later in another of those strings (str2), regexp may miss potential matches that begin with the longer string (str2). For example, the following command returns 'string', but not 'longstring', even though 'longstring' is the leftmost match:   regexp('longstring', 'string|longstring', 'match')   ans =        'string' This problem is order dependent.  If the longer string is tried first, the problem does not happen. Patterns beginning with a choice of literal strings aren't always obvious. For example, the following command also exhibits the problem:   regexp('fox', 'x?fox')
Workaround: In order to avoid the problem, ensure that the longer string is tried before the shorter string in the evaluation of the pattern. Replace statements like   regexp('longstring', 'string|longstring')with   regexp('longstring', 'longstring|string')And replace statements like   regexp('fox', 'x?fox')with   regexp('fox', 'x??fox')

Summary: Simulink enters an infinite loop during block diagram update in simplified initialization mode when the model contains a Merge block in a self-loop at the root level
Description: Updating a block diagram causes an infinite loop (instead of producing an error) if all  of the following conditions apply: The Configuration Parameters  -> Diagnostics -> Data Validity -> Underspecified initialization detection  parameter is set to Simplified . The model contains a Merge block in a self-loop or in a loop involving multiple, but only Merge blocks at the root level.

Summary: Problem with tuning parameters of Battery block or Fuel Cell block using Simulink Design Optimization software
Description: You cannot tune some of the parameters of a Battery block or a Fuel Cell block using Simulink Design Optimization software. If your model contains a Battery block, then in order to estimate parameters, you unselect the Use parameters based on Battery type and nominal values  option on the block dialog, so that you can define battery parameters as variables in the MATLAB workspace and assign these variables to the corresponding dialog parameters in the Battery block. However, when you then apply the changes and double-click the dialog box of the Battery block again, the variable names are replaced with corresponding values from the MATLAB workspace. As a result, you are unable to estimate the values of these parameters through the parameter estimation process. This happens only for the parameters that are listed after the Use parameters based on Battery type and nominal values  option in the dialog box. You can estimate the first three parameters, that is, Nominal Voltage , Rated Capacity , and Initial State of Charge . A similar issue exists when tuning parameters of a Fuel Cell block.
Workaround: To fix the issue for both blocks, download and install the attached files, overwriting existing files in your MATLAB installation:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/drives/drives/FuelCellCback.p $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/drives/drives/BatteryCback.pwhere $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window.?

Summary: det does not error for non-square symbolic matrices.
Description: Calling the det  function with a non-square matrix does not error. Instead, the function operates on a square portion of the matrix. For example, syms a bdet([a b]) returns a .
Workaround: Ensure that the matrix is square before calling det.

Summary: Running the program 'PSTRLManager.exe' on a Windows 2008, Windows 7, or Windows Vista system generates the message: "CreateProcess error=740, The requested operation requires elevation"
Description: When using the 'PSTRLManager.exe' user interface to set-up the configuration file for the Queue Manager service on a Windows 7, Windows 2008, or Windows Vista system, a pop-up message appears with the following message: "CreateProcess error=740, The requested operation requires elevation".  This happens even if the login account used to start the Queue Manager service has local administrator rights on the machine.
Workaround: To workaround the issue on Windows 7, Windows 2008 server, or on Windows Vista system:Using a file browser, right-click on the file %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLauncher\PSTRLManager.exe.Select the option Run as Administrator.The program then works as expected.

Summary: MATLAB crash when Weighted Sample Time Math block uses 'Inherit via internal rule' in model with continuous sample time
Description: When the following conditions are all true, MATLAB might crash without any helpful output: The sample time of a Simulink model is continuous. The model has a Weighted Sample Time Math block where the output data type is set to 'Inherit via internal rule'.

Summary: If a referenced model contains a block with one zero-crossing signal and this referenced model is in an enabled subsystem, code generation will cause compilation failure
Description: If a referenced model contains a block with one zero-crossing signal, and this referenced model resides in an enabled subsystem, code generation will cause a compilation failure with following errors: Level2.c(125) : error C2143: syntax error : missing ')' before ';' Level2.c(125) : error C2102: '&' requires l-value.
Workaround: In the file matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/syslib.tlc, you can change the following line:real_T *zcsv = (real_T *) &(%%%);toreal_T *zcsv = (real_T *) &(%%); .

Summary: rectpuls function returns a signal of type logical
Description: When using rectpuls  to generate a rectangular pulse, >> x = rectpuls(0:0.01:1); the returned signal, x , is of type logical.
Workaround: To work around this issue, cast the output to type double. >> x = double(rectpuls(0:0.01:1));

Summary: Model Advisor check Check configuration parameters for MISRA-C:2004 compliance recommendation might increase MISRA-C:2004 violations
Description: The Model Advisor check Check configuration parameters for MISRA-C:2004 compliance  recommends clearing the parameter Shift right on a signed integer as arithmetic shift  on the Hardware Implementation  > Embedded hardware  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Depending on your model and your embedded hardware implementation settings, following the recommendation might increase the number of MISRA-C:2004 violations. Clearing this parameter might reduce violations of rule 12.7, but might cause additional violations of MISRA-C:2004 rules 10.1 and 10.3.
Workaround: After running the Model Advisor check Check configuration parameters for MISRA-C:2004 compliance, you can choose to not clear the configuration parameter Shift right on a signed integer as arithmetic shift. In the Action box, do not click Modify All to automatically modify the configuration parameters.

Summary: Using the command-line API to compile a model containing variable-sized inputs to a Logical Operator, Product or Add block terminates MATLAB execution
Description: MATLAB will terminate compiling and execution under the following circumstances:  if a model containing a Logical Operator, Product or Sum block that has a variable-sized signal as input is compiled using the command model([],[],[],'compile'), where 'model' is the name of the model.

Summary: Freescale MPC5xx Asynchronous Rate Transition block does not disable interrupts
Description: The Freescale MPC5xx Asynchronous Rate Transition block does not disable interrupts during data transfer, leading to the possibility that data integrity is violated.
Workaround: 1. Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.2. Download the .zip file located under the "Attachments" for this Bug Report.3. Locate your MATLAB installation directory by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.4. Exit MATLAB.5. Extract the contents of the .zip file to the MATLAB installation directory.6. Start MATLAB.7. Enter pcode -inplace mpc555_disable_interrupts at the MATLAB command prompt.Change log:September 27, 2010: 2010-07-19-attachment-653556-7a-through-10b.zip: initial version

Summary: Executable running on heavily-loaded VxWorks target might crash when disconnecting from external mode simulation
Description: An executable running on a heavily-loaded VxWorks target system might crash when disconnecting from external mode simulation. Code generated for the Tornado (VxWorks) Real-Time Target (tornado.tlc ) with external mode selected spawns multiple threads. When the code runs on a heavily-loaded VxWorks target system, disconnecting can lead to conflicts between the external mode threads and cause an exception.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2009b, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/src/ext_mode/common/ext_work.cmatlabroot/rtw/c/src/ext_mode/common/ext_svr.cDownload the attached files and place the files at the same location as the files you renamed.Regenerate the target executable.

Summary: The Plot Selector remains in a busy state when it is used to create a plot while in a debug workspace
Description: If you use the Plot Selector to create a plot while at a breakpoint, the Plot Selector will be in a busy state after plotting until the stack has returned to the base workspace. Since the Plot Selector is unresponsive in a busy state, this effectively limits the Plot Selector to creating a single plot when debugging.

That is, the number of columns in the image is not equal to the product of the number of rows in a block and the the number of blocks in each row. Despite the crash, these files are perfectly valid.

Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for Abs block with fixed-point data type conversion
Description: When the input and output data types of an Abs block are different fixed-point types, Simulink generates code to compute the output value using the ratio between input & output fixed-point slopes. However, if the input is positive, the generated code uses a less precise data type for the ratio of the slopes. Hence, the generated code may return imprecise results.

Summary: Replacement types are ignored if SimulationMode parameter is not set to a supported value
Description: When generating code for a model, replacement types are ignored if the SimulationMode  parameter is not set to Normal , Software-in-the-Loop , or Processor-in-the-Loop  and the mode is configured as follows: Uses the ERT target or a target derived from the ERT target On the Data Type Replacement  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Replace data type names in the generated code  parameter is selected. Replacement  types are specified  Real Time Workshop Embedded Coder provides no indication that the replacement types will be ignored.
Workaround: Prior to generating code, set the SimulationMode parameter to Normal temporarily.

Summary: HDL code generated from a Stateflow chart may be incorrect when a function call is in a transition condition
Description: HDL code generated from a Stateflow chart may be incorrect if: The chart contains a transition condition in the form of an expression that contains a function call and a boolean expression The function call and the boolean expression are connected by logical AND("&&") or logical OR ("||") operators
Workaround: You can usually avoid the issue by breaking up the expression into multiple subexpressions. To ensure the same functionality, the subexpressions should be connected by junction nodes. None of the subexpressions should have a function call and a boolean expression connected by logical AND("&&") or logical OR ("||") operators.??

Summary: Opening large call-graphs in the Viewer causes an out of memory problem: The 'Call-Tree View' is not updated any more
Description: After loading verification results in the Viewer and reviewing a few checks (adding comments and selecting the 'Reviewed' check-box), the 'Call-Tree View' no longer updates. This happens on large projects.
Workaround: The following workaround is available for release R2010a and R2010b. To workaround this issue on R2010a:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\binCreate backup copies of the following 5 files: rte-displayer.jarrte-ihme-if.jarrte-ihme-verifier.jarrte-reader.jarrte-writer.jarDownload the attached file: 2010-09-03-attachment-650460-r10a.zip.Extract the .jar files from the zip archive and copy them to the folder:%POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\binTo workaround this issue on R2010b:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_C%\PVE\binCreate backup copies of the following 2 files: util.jarpve.jarDownload the attached file: 2010-09-13-attachment-650460-r10b.zip.Extract the .jar files from the zip archive and copy them to the folder:%POLYSPACE_C%\PVE\bin

Summary: Simulink block data tips options can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you enable block data tips options (from the Simulink View  menu or from the MATLAB command line) for a model that does linearlization or PID controller tuning, then hovering over a subsystem crashes MATLAB after model compilation.
Workaround: When you use linearization or PID controller tuning in a model, disable each of the block data tips options, by using the Simulink model window View -> Block Data Tips Options menu options or command line parameters (shown in parentheses):Block Name (BlockNameDataTip)Parameter Names And Values (BlockParameterDataTip)User Description String (BlockDescriptionStringDataTip)

Summary: The getDiscountFactors method returns incorrect results when the curve Type is forward
Description: Calling the getDiscountFactors  method of IRDataCurve  when the curve Type  is forward  returns unexpected results.

Summary: Code generation  may fail after verifying a model using Simulink Design Verifier.
Description: A normal code generation operation may fail after using Simulink Design Verifier to analyze a given model. This failure is caused by improper data cleanup at the end of Simulink Design Verifier processing. Note that a second attempt to generate code with the same model passes, because the first failed code generation build properly clears all data.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Simulink may produce an incorrect linearization result for a For Each Subsystem if it contains an S-function or a Model block
Description: If a For Each subsystem is on the linearization path, Simulink returns empty arrays for matrices A, B and C under either of the following conditions: An S-Function block, which is inside the For Each Subsystem block, has discrete states, or A Model block, which is inside the For Each Subsystem, references a model containing a discrete state.  ?

Summary: pdetool does not show the full y-axis range at startup.
Description: When the pdetool  GUI is started, the y-axis seems to be clipped from the top. Furthermore, both automatic and manual settings of the y-axis range yield a clipped or improperly scaled y-axis.

Summary: On UNIX, if you type ahead while executing a script using the system command, the type-ahead characters are prepended to the result output argument.
Description: If you call the MATLAB system  command on a UNIX platform using the following syntax: [status, result] = system('script') and type ahead while script  executes, MATLAB prepends the type-ahead characters to the result  output argument. For example, script a.sh  contains the following statements: #!/bin/sh sleep 5echo "123" If you call this script using the system  command: >> [a,b] = system('./a.sh') and wait for the script to execute, MATLAB displays: a = 0 b = 123>>  But if you type ahead characters abc  while the script is running, MATLAB displays: a = 0 b = abc123 >> abc
Workaround: There is no workarond until R2011a. In R2011a the user can avoid the problem by redirecting standard input in the command system:>> [a,b] = system('./a.sh < /dev/null')?

Summary: Simulink HDL Coder crashes (segV) when generating code for some  Embedded MATLAB bit operations on constants
Description: Simulink HDL Coder crashes with a segV error  when generating code for an Embedded MATLAB bitget function when both the following conditions are met: The bitget function is extracting a single bit from a constant. The constant is outside the range of a signed integer. The range of supported values is (-2^31...2^31 - 1), or from -2147483648 to 2147483647.

Summary: Undocumented function uirestore overwrites figure WindowScrollWheelFcn callback
Description: The uirestore  function is undocumented and will change in a future release. However, if you are using it, you can encounter the following issue: uirestore  incorrectly assigns the previous value of the WindowKeyPressFcn  to the WindowScrollWheelFcn  callback. As a result, neither of these callback functions operates as expected.
Workaround: Edit the uirestore function:edit uirestore Find the following line, which resets the WindowScrollWheelFcn callback: set(fig, 'WindowScrollWheelFcn', uistate.WindowKeyPressFcn)and replace it with this code:set(fig, 'WindowScrollWheelFcn', uistate.WindowScrollWheelFcn)

Summary: Progress bar dialog does not close after calling plotbrowser('off').
Description: After starting a fresh session of MATLAB, enter the following on the command line: plotbrowser('off')  A dialog box with a progress bar displays as plotting tools load. Instead of vanishing after the tools are available, the dialog box remains on-screen. Subsequently when you open or close any of the plotting tools from the command line, the progress dialog does not display again because the tools are now loaded into memory.
Workaround: Close the progress dialog manually by click ing its close box.

Summary: Incorrect Saturation block coverage when using fixed-point or mixed (different input/output) signal types and one of the limits is eliminated by optimizations.
Description: The Saturation block coverage might show incorrect results after one of the limits has been eliminated. For example, if the lower limit is set to 0 and the signal is an unsigned fixed-point data type (ufix), the Saturation block does not execute the lower limit check, but the coverage report might show that the branch was always false.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this problem. You can reduce its occurrence by disabling the Simulink Inline parameters optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation: size of variable sized signal is not updated correctly by Embedded MATLAB function block
Description: This bug can occur when an Embedded MATLAB function block updates the size of a variable sized signal. It can only occur when the x=foo(x) idiom is used inside the Embedded MATLAB function block. For example: function y1 = libs_scalar_in5(u,y) %#eml  y1 = myfcn(u,y);function y = myfcn(u,y)  eml.inline('never');  if u == 1    y = zeros(3);  end The generated code for this example does not change the size of the output to 3x3 when u==1.
Workaround: Do not use the x=foo(x) idiom on variable sized signals.

Summary: Peak-to-peak jitter measurement of the eye diagram returns wrong answer if the horizontal histogram is not symmetric
Description: Communications Toolbox provides eye diagram analysis through an object called commscope.eyediagram. This object captures eye diagram information in the form of horizontal and vertical historgrams and uses this information to provide measurements such as peak-to-peak jitter. When the horizontal histogram is not symmetric, the peak-to-peak jitter measurement returns the wrong answer because the algorithm only considers one-half of the histogram.
Workaround: 1) Go to the directory MATLABROOT/toolbox/comm/comm/@commscope/@eyemeasurements/private/2) Create a copy of the file calcJitterRMSP2P.m and name calcJitterRMSP2P_bk.m3) Open the file calcJitterRMSP2P.m4) Replace line 45 with:peak2PeakR = max(idx(idx >= histLen2)) - min(idx(idx >= histLen2));

Summary: Publishing: Sometimes MATLAB? becomes unresponsive
Description: Sometimes, when attempting to publish code, MATLAB becomes unresponsive.

Summary: blp.getdata fails with "dimension mismatch" or "conversion to cell from double not possible" error
Description: The blp.getdata  method fails with the error: ??? Subscripted assignment dimension mismatch.  Error in ==> blp.getdata at 146  d.(flds{j})(i,1) = tmp{i}.(flds{j});  Or: ??? Conversion to cell from double is not possible.  Error in ==> blp.getdata at 133  [tmp{k},newflds,tmpsec] = processMessage(msg);  ?Requested fields may also be missing from the returned data structure.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@blp/getdata.m to matlabroot/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@blp/getdata.m_old.Download the attached file getdata.m.Save this file to matlabroot/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@blp.Restart MATLAB.From the MATLAB command line, run 'rehash toolboxcache'.?

Summary: pmtm errors when the input signal length is greater than 2^20.
Description: When the input signal length exceeds 2^20, pmtm  errors. For example, execute the command: pmtm(rand(1,2^20+1),[],256);  The following error occurs: ??? Error using ==> fftfilt at 78 Filters of length greater than 2^20 are not supported. Use dfilt.fftfir instead.  ? ?
Workaround: Replace the version of fftfilt.m in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment as follows:Determine your MATLAB installation directory by entering matlabroot at the command prompt.Make a backup copy of the following file: matlabroot/toolbox/signal/signal/fftfilt.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory returned in step 1. For example, you can rename fftfilt.m by adding "old" to the file extension: fftfilt.m.old.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached file, fftfilt.m, and save it in the matlabroot/toolbox/signal/signal directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Some computational geometry and interpolation functions and methods may abort in a deployed application when they are invoked through multiple shared libraries or components created using MATLAB? Compiler or MATLAB? Builder products.
Description: The MATLAB? Compiler and Builder products create multiple shared libraries and invoke them from a single deployed application. This will result in multiple MCR instances in the same client application. If each of these shared libraries or components calls certain computational geometry and interpolation functions or methods from the same client application, then the application may abort. The affected functions and classes are: delaunay  convhull  griddata  voronoi  TriScatteredInterp  DelaunayTri   ?
Workaround: Restrict your use of these functions to a single shared library. Applications linked against multiple shared libraries will function properly if only one of the shared libraries calls these computational geometry and interpolation functions.

Summary: Code generation errors when an Iterator Subsystem contains a Model block configured for C++ encapsulation
Description: If you generate code for a model in which an Iterator Subsystem directly or indirectly contains a Model block configured for C++ encapsulation, Target Language Compiler (TLC) errors or compile-time errors might be generated, depending on the modeling pattern and the state of the model option Use operator new for referenced model object registration : If there is no reusable subsystem between the Model block and the Iterator Subsystem block, TLC errors might be issued. The errors are similar to the following, where matlabroot   represents your MATLAB installation root directory: Error: File: matlabroot /rtw/c/tlc/mw/modelrefutil.tlc Line: 881 Column: 34 Unable to find DWork within the Block scopeError: File: matlabroot /rtw/c/tlc/mw/modelrefutil.tlc Line: 881 Column: 43 The argument for an [] operation must be a repeated scope symbol, a vector, or a matrixError: File: matlabroot /rtw/c/tlc/mw/modelrefutil.tlc Line: 881 Column: 68 The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifierError: File: matlabroot /rtw/c/tlc/mw/modelrefutil.tlc Line: 881 Column: 34 Unable to find DWork within the Block scope  If no TLC errors are issued, and if Use operator new for referenced model object registration  is selected, compile-time errors might be issued. The errors vary depending on the compiler you are using, but might be similar to the following: Parent.cpp(184) : error C2228: left of '.Subsystem' must have class/struct/union        type is 'rtDW_CoreSubsys_Parent[1]'Parent.cpp(184) : error C2228: left of '.Subsystem_MDLOBJ0' must have class/struct/unionParent.cpp(192) : error C2228: left of '.Subsystem' must have class/struct/union        type is 'rtDW_CoreSubsys_Parent [1]'Parent.cpp(192) : error C2228: left of '.Subsystem_MDLOBJ0' must have class/struct/union
Workaround: To work around this bug:If you are experiencing the TLC errors, perform the following steps:Create a new subsystem that directly contains the Model block.Open the block parameters dialog box for the newly-created Subsystem block, select the option Treat as atomic unit, and set the parameter Real-Time Workshop system code to Reusable function.Open the Configuration Parameters dialog box, go to the Real-Time Workshop > Interface pane, and make sure that the option Use operator new for referenced model object registration is cleared.If you are experiencing only the compile-time errors, open the Configuration Parameters dialog box, go to the Real-Time Workshop > Interface pane, and clear the option Use operator new for referenced model object registration.

Summary: Error taking address of field of structured parameter with custom storage class
Description: If both of the following conditions exist, calls to the TLC parameter function LibBlockParameterAddr  fail: Block accesses an element of a tunable parameter structure, such as P.a  Storage class of the parameter is Custom   When the error occurs, the following message appears: Error: Real-Time Workshop Error: Parameter 'P' is not addressable because of its custom storage class
Workaround: If you encounter this bug due to expression folding, locate the relevant section of the block diagram and insert a Signal Conversion block.Otherwise, change the parameter expression or the storage class.

Summary: You cannot add current channels after you add voltage channels in the session-based interface
Description: When you use a CompactDAQ device in the session-based interface, you cannot add a current channel after you add a voltage channel from another device. For example, if you add a voltage channel from an NI 9205 with device ID cDAQ2Mod2 and then add a current channel from an NI 9227 with device ID cDAQ2Mod3, you will see the following error: s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ2Mod2',0, 'voltage'); % NI 9205   s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ2Mod3',0, 'current') % NI 9227 ??? NI Error -200077: Requested value is not a supported value for this property. Property: DAQmx_SampTimingType You Have Requested: DAQmx_Val_OnDemand You Can Select: DAQmx_Val_SampClk Device: cDAQ2 Task Name: _unnamedTask<1F> Status Code: -200077
Workaround: Add current channels before you add voltage channels.s = daq.createSession('ni'); s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ2Mod3',0, 'current'); Warning: The Rate property was increased to 1612.9032 due to changes in the session configuration. s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ2Mod2',0, 'voltage');

Summary: mcc crashes when two MATLAB files with the same name, having different paths, are used during compilation
Description: If two MATLAB functions with the same name but different paths are used with the mcc  command line, mcc  either crashes (on UNIX) or hangs (on Windows). For exampe, if you compile dir1 /myFunction.m  and dir2 /myFunction.m , where dir1   and dir2   are sub-directories of the current working directory on UNIX, you get a segmentation violation similar to the message below; On Windows, this compilation results in a hung mcc  process. ************************************************************ ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Segmentation violation detected at Thu Apr 29 14:54:04 2010 ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Configuration: MATLAB Version: 7.7.0.471 (R2008b) MATLAB License: unknown Operating System: Linux 2.6.18-128.1.14.el5 #1 SMP Wed Jun 17 06:40:54 EDT 2009 i686 GNU C Library: 2.5 stable Processor ID: x86 Family 15 Model 4 Stepping 1, GenuineIntel Virtual Machine: Java 1.6.0_04 with Sun Microsystems Inc. Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM mixed mode Default Encoding: UTF-8 Fault Count: 1 Register State: eax = 09ae8a48 ebx = 0038bff4 ecx = 00000001 edx = b7338534 esi = fffffff4 edi = b7340004 ebp = b7336238 esp = b7336210 eip = 00343c3f flg = 00210293 Stack Trace: [0] libstdc++.so.6:std::basic_string, std::allocator >::basic_string(std::string const&)~(0xb7338534, 0x09ae8a48, 0x0ba8c09d "myFunction.m", 0x0ba8c0d9 "/mathworks/home/maben/cases/1-CN..") + 31 bytes Please follow these steps to report this problem to The MathWorks so we have the best chance of correcting it: The next time MATLAB is launched under typical usage, a dialog box will open to help you send the error log to The MathWorks. Alternatively, you can send an e-mail to segv@mathworks.com with the following file attached: /home/maben/matlab_crash_dump.16910 If the problem is reproducible, please submit a Service Request via: https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html/ts/help_request_1.html A technical support engineer might contact you with further information. Thank you for your help. ************************************************************   .
Workaround: Avoid using duplicate file names.

Summary: Creating a class named string can crash MATLAB
Description: If you create a class named string , MATLAB creates the class, but does not overwrite the builtin string  type until the class is cleared. At this point the builtin string  type has been removed, and any MATLAB functionality that uses string  can cause a crash.
Workaround: Do not define a class with a name that is also the name of a MATLAB builtin type, such as 'string'.

Summary: The GENEONT constructor function builds a gene ontology object that indicates two or more terms have a 'part_of' relationship, when in reality they have another type of relationship.
Description: The GENEONT  constructor function builds a gene ontology object that indicates two or more terms have a 'part_of'  relationship, when in reality they have another type of relationship. This occurs with OBO-formatted files that contain relationship types that are not supported by the GENEONT  object, for example: 'has_part' , 'regulates' , 'regulates_positively' , and 'regulates_negativily' . This also happens when the 'part_of'  relationship is first used in the OBO-formatted file after any of the unsupported types of relationships, but not after an 'is_a'  relationship. This BUG does not affect the 'is_a'  relationships in an OBO formatted file.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\@geneont\geneont.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\@geneont\geneont.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\@geneont\Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: The emlc and emlmex commands produce errors or hang when msys is installed and on the path.
Description: The msys  toolset is incompatible with emlc  and emlmex . If the msys  toolset is installed on a machine, it must be removed from the path before starting MATLAB and using emlc  or emlmex .
Workaround: Uninstall msys or remove msys from the path before starting MATLAB.

Summary: The output from qr may contain non-finite values when the input is exceptionally poorly scaled.
Description: Passing an exceptionally poorly scaled matrix to qr  may result in output matrices with non-finite values: [Q R] = qr([1 1; 1e-154 1])Q =   1    -Inf   Inf   InfR =   1     Inf   0     Inf

Summary: Matrix displayed with misaligned columns
Description: When a matrix contains NaN or Inf entries and is diplayed using SHORT or LONG ENG format, the columns do not align properly.

Summary: Assignments with left-hand scalar expression and logical indexing produce wrong answers in Embedded MATLAB code
Description: The following type of assignment in Embedded MATLAB code might produce wrong answers: A(E) = V; where A  is a scalar and E  is a logical index expression. No matter what the value of E , this assignment always changes the value of A  to V  (as opposed to correct MATLAB behavior, where the assignment happens only if E  is true). For example, the following code assigns A  the value of 0, even though the correct answer is 1: A = 1; A(A<0) = 0; This issue also affects assignments with more than one index expression, as long as the left-hand expression is a scalar.
Workaround: Modify your code to explicitly test whether your left-hand expression is scalar. For example, replace the above code with:A = 1;if isscalar(A)   if (A<0)       A = 0;   endelse   A(A<0) = 0;end?

Summary: Users of GigE Vision cameras from vendors without Linux configuration software may be unable to see their cameras from within the Image Acquisition Toolbox
Description: Users of GigE Vision cameras from vendors without Linux configuration software may be unable to see their cameras from within the Image Acquisition Toolbox. This happens because cameras sometimes retain previous IP configuration and may not be assigned a new IP address by the Linux system.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: 'canny' method of edge function uses effective Gaussian smoothing factor sqrt(2) larger than advertised
Description: In previous versions of the edge  function, the implementation of the Canny filter smoothed the image twice during the construction of the gradient image. The edge  function smoothed the image using a Gaussian filter and then used a first derivative of a Gaussian filter to extract a smoothed version of the image gradient. Smoothing an image and then differentiating it is the same as convolving the image with a derivative of the smoothing kernel, so this implementation had the effect of smoothing the image twice. In addition, the original implementation of the Canny filter included an extra morphological thinning step that is not in the published algorithm.
Workaround: If you have R2010a or R2010b, you can work around this problem as follows. Replace the version of edge.m in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/images/images/edge.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: edge.m.oldDownload the attached file, extract edge.m, storing it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator fails to initialize state variables that have an imported custom storage class
Description: When the following conditions exist, the code generator does not initialize a state variable (for example, data store memory or output of an enabled subsystem): You specified an initial value for the variable Variable uses an imported custom storage class, such as GetSet  or ImportFromFile .  A mismatch occurs between the simulation output and output of the generated code.

Summary: Using linear algebra operations such as mtimes on a machine with 32 or more cores may crash MATLAB?
Description: All usage of linear algebra operations, such as mtimes , mldivide , etc., on a machine with 32 or more cores running Windows? Server 2008 may crash MATLAB?.

Summary: Publishing to PDF: incorrect page numbers in table of contents
Description: When using MATLAB? 7.10 (Release 2010a) and  publishing a file to PDF, sometimes the page numbers in the table of contents are incorrect.

Summary: The TLC debugger might crash with SEGV when attempting to exit or continue from an irrecoverable error
Description: The TLC debugger might crash with SEGV when all of the following conditions are met: The debugging session reaches an irrecoverable error through a 'break error' or 'stop error' command An attempt is made to exit or continue the execution from the irrecoverable error

Summary: Incorrect analysis results for models that contain Discrete-Time Integrator blocks with external initial conditions connected to Constant blocks.
Description: The Constant block connected to the Discrete-Time Integrator block's external initial condition input is not initialized. This causes incorrect analysis results.

Summary: Copying items in Microsoft Word causes bookmarks to appear when some Simulink windows are open
Description: When some Simulink windows are open, such as Library Browser, Model Explorer, or property dialogs, the act of copying items in Microsoft Word causes a bookmark to appear in Word.

Summary: Direct Lookup Table (n-D)  block displays  assertion error when generating HDL code
Description: Under certain conditions, when generating HDL code for a model that includes a Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block, Simulink HDL Coder displays  an internal assertion error message and terminates  HDL code generation. The error occurs if the data type of the table  data  (specified in the Table data  block parameter)  is different from the data type of the block's output port.
Workaround: To avoid this problem:Always provide an explicit data type specification for the table data  in the Table data block parameter.Always make sure that this data type specification is the same as the block's output data type.For example, if the Table data is [0 0.5 1 2 4 8] and the output data type is int8, change the Table data specification to int8([0 0.5 1 2 4 8]). If the output data type is a fixed point type such as sfix8_En2,  change the Table data specification to fi([0 0.5 1 2 4 8], 1, 8, 2).

Summary: Slow performance or hang when dragging mouse to select several blocks/states in Simulink or Stateflow
Description: Trying to select several blocks or states in Simulink and Stateflow may cause very slow performance or even software hangs. This is usually accompanied by flickering in the window. This has only been observed on Windows Vista or Windows 7.
Workaround: Turn off the Aero theme in Windows Vista or Windows 7.Users have reported that this helps address the problem.

Summary: MATLAB throws Segmentation Violation errors if the XServer connection is broken while MATLAB is running.
Description: When using MATLAB on a Linux or Mac operating system, a Segmentation Violation might occur if the connection to the XServer is broken.

Summary: Data Access Mode of outports cannot be set programmatically after AUTOSAR import
Description: If you create a Simulink model with arxml.importer  commands and then try to change the Data Access Mode of an outport using the AUTOSAR configuration object, the software produces an error: % Import AUTOSAR arxml fileimporter = arxml.importer('mySWC.arxml');  % Create Simulink modelimporter.createComponentAsModel('/ComponentType/SWC');  % Get the AUTOSAR configuration object confObj = RTW.getFunctionSpecification('SWC'); % Set the Data Access Mode of outport 'Out1' confObj.setIODataAccessMode('Out1', 'ExplicitSend'); ??? Error using ==> setIODataAccessMode at 16 Allowed data access modes are 'ImplicitReceive', 'ExplicitReceive', 'ImplicitSend', 'ExplicitSend', 'ErrorStatus' and 'BasicServicePort'. The given input was 'ExplicitSend'.
Workaround: Use the RTW.AutosarInterface method syncWithModel to synchronise the configuration object with the Simulink model:% Import AUTOSAR arxml fileimporter = arxml.importer('mySWC.arxml');  % Create Simulink modelimporter.createComponentAsModel('/ComponentType/SWC');  % Get the AUTOSAR configuration object confObj = RTW.getFunctionSpecification('SWC'); % Sync with model confObj.syncWithModel(); % <---- Inserted line% Set the Data Access Mode of outport 'Out1' confObj.setIODataAccessMode('Out1', 'ExplicitSend');

Summary: Code generation fails for model with virtual buses entering or leaving variant Model blocks
Description: Code generation can fail with a segmentation fault for a model with virtual buses that enter or leave a variant Model block.
Workaround: Place bus conversion blocks at any ports of a variant Model block that reads from, or writes to, a virtual bus:At a Model block inport that reads from a virtual bus, place a Signal Conversion block and set its Output parameter to Nonvirtual bus. At a Model block outport that writes to a virtual bus, place a Signal Conversion block and set its Output parameter to Virtual bus.

Summary: MATLAB displays the prompt >> before processing the command line switch -r.
Description: In MATLAB version 7.10 (R2010a), the command: matlab -r "disp('hello');"  displays this output: >>hello>>  instead of: hello>>

Summary: On dual-monitor display systems, the figure's default location is inconsistent.
Description: When determining the figure's default position in a dual-monitor setup, the entire display area (both monitors) was taken into consideration for the calculation. The entire display area was reported differently for different platforms with different setups.

Summary: In the Viewer, large call-graphs may lead to an out of memory problem : the Viewer is stuck in the "Parsing call graph" phase.
Description: After loading results in the Viewer, the message "Parsing call graph" appears and the mouse icon changes to an hourglass, and nothing happens. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, increase the amount of RAM available to the Viewer.To do this, you will need to modify the following file:"PolySpace_Common/Viewer/PSTViewer.lax"In this file, you have to look for the line that starts withlax.nl.java.option.additional=In this line, look for "-ms128m -mx512m".The option "ms" indicates the minimum amount of ram, and "mx" the maximum amount.Depending on how much memory is available on your PC, you can try the setting -mx1024m.Then restart PolySpace and try to load the results, you will need to allow for some time as your Call-Graph file is quite large.Note: If this workaround does not work, please refer to work around  in bug report https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/650460.

Summary: Cannot start license manager using lmstart utility on systems running Mac OS 10.6
Description: If you try to start the license manager on a Mac OS X 10.6 system using the lmstart  utility, it returns the following error:Waiting 300 secs for MATLAB vendor daemon to come up...
Workaround: A bug in the FLEXnet license manager prevents the license manager scripts from starting the license manager on systems runningMac OS X 10.6. To workaround this issue, start the license manager manually by executing the lmgrd binary, as follows:Open Terminal from Applications/UtilitiesRun the following command for a 64 bit Mac:$MATLAB/etc/maci64/lmgrd -c $MATLAB/etc/license.dat -l /var/tmp/lm_TMW.logwhere $MATLAB is the MATLAB installation location. For example:/Applications/MATLAB_R2009b.app/etc/maci64/lmgrd -c /Applications/MATLAB_R2009b.app/etc/license.dat -l /var/tmp/lm_TMW.logRun the following command for a 32 bit Mac:$MATLAB/etc/maci/lmgrd -c $MATLAB/etc/license.dat -l /var/tmp/lm_TMW.logwhere $MATLAB is the MATLAB installation location. For example:/Applications/MATLAB_R2009b.app/etc/maci/lmgrd -c /Applications/MATLAB_R2009b.app/etc/license.dat -l /var/tmp/lm_TMW.logOnce started, the license manager will continue to run until the computer is restarted.  The license manager boot scripts are also affected by this bug so you will not be able to start the license manager automatically.  When you restart your computer, you will have to restart the license manager, as described above.

Summary: Changing the processor affinity of the MATLAB? process on Windows may disable multithreading in linear algebra computations.
Description: On Microsoft? Windows?, setting the processor affinity of the MATLAB process to use fewer than all of the available CPUs may cause successive linear algebra computations to run in serial on only one CPU.
Workaround: To work around this issue, set the MKL_DYNAMIC environment variable to false. Note that setting the MKL_DYNAMIC environment variable to false may have the side effect of causing MATLAB to fail to achieve optimal performance in linear algebra computations.

Summary: On UNIX? platforms, setting the BufferLength or NumberOfBuffers property of an audiorecorder object returns an error
Description: Setting either the BufferLength  or NumberOfBuffers  property returns an error on UNIX? platforms.For example, the following code: obj = audiorecorder();set(obj, 'BufferLength', 5) Results in the error: ??? Error using ==> audiorecorder.set at 103Changing the 'BufferLength' property of an audiorecorder object is not allowed.

Summary: Setting a breakpoint in an object's GET method can make MATLAB enter an infinite loop or generate a segmentation violation
Description: Setting a breakpoint in an object's get  method can cause MATLAB to enter an infinite loop, or generate a segmentation violation. This is more likely if the Workspace Browser is open or if an object with such a breakpoint set is open in the Variable Editor.
Workaround: Workarounds include:Close the Workspace BrowserDo not set breakpoints in get methods.If MATLAB enters such an infinite loop, use the Editor to unset the breakpoint

Summary: On Macintosh OS X systems running 64-bit MATLAB, the sound and soundsc functions add distortions
Description: The sound  and soundsc  functions introduce audible pops or clicks on Macintosh OS X systems when running 64-bit MATLAB. For example, the following code:sound(0.5*sin(2*pi*440*linspace(0,1,44101)),44100) produces an audible pop during playback.

Summary: Large data arrays do not display or display very slowly in the Variable Editor
Description: Large data arrays (with approximately 500,000 or more elements) do not display properly, or display very slowly, in the Variable Editor. Upon opening, the Variable Editor has only a blank white background. The data might or might not eventually populate the Variable Editor.
Workaround: You can access sections of a large array at the command line by indexing them. Copy a section of the array to a new variable and view that variable in the variable editor. For example:smallerData = data(1:10000,:);openvar(smallerData)Even if you attempt to partition the entire array in this manner, you will still not be able to load all the variables into the Variable Editor at the same time.

Summary: Setting a breakpoint in an overloaded SIZE method may cause MATLAB to enter an infinite loop or generate a segmentation violation
Description: Setting a breakpoint in an object's overloaded size  method can cause MATLAB to enter an infinite loop or generate a segmentation violation. This is more likely if the Workspace is open or if the Variable Editor is being used to view an object with such a breakpoint set.
Workaround: Workarounds include:Close the Workspace.Do not set breakpoints in size methods.If MATLAB enters such an infinite loop, use the Editor to unset the breakpoint.

Summary: Error occurs when attempting to change the LineStyleOrder of an axes using the Property Inspector.
Description: When attempting to select the Marker Style  or Line Style  drop down menu in the LineStyleOrder  property of the Property Inspector in MATLAB , options to choose other line and marker styles are not displayed. Additionally, following messages are displayed on the MATLAB Command Window: The window containing this component must havean MWBorderLayout for this component to function.It is recommended that you use MWFrame or MWDialog.
Workaround: There is currently no fix for this issue. The only workaround is to set the LineStyleOrder property on the command line. For example,set(gca,'LineStyleOrder',{'-*',':','o'}).

Summary: Data for signals logged in Simulink.Timeseries format may have incorrect attributes or values, or be missing
Description: In releases prior to R2011a, all signal logging data used Simulink.ModelDataLogs  objects, which store data using Simulink.Timeseries  objects. In releases from R2011a through R2015b, you could set the Signal logging format  option in the Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export  tab to either ModelDataLogs  or Dataset  (which does not use Simulink.Timeseries  objects). Starting in R2016a, the ModelDataLogs  format is ignored and data is automatically logged in Dataset  format.  In releases prior to R2016a, the following problems occur when you use ModelDataLogs  format, when Simulink logs: Outputs of a Signal Specification block, the signal attributes set by the block, such as data type or dimension, may be incorrect. An input signal within a conditionally executed subsystem: Incorrect time values appear in the log for a periodic input signal of a function-call subsystem. No time data values appear in the log for an input signal of a triggered subsystem if both of the following conditions exist: Signal logging was enabled using the input's Signal Properties dialog box. The triggered subsystem contains only virtual blocks.     A virtual bus signal, if multiple signals in a virtual bus have the same source block, only one signal is included in the logged object. A virtual bus output from an enabled subsystem, the times when the subsystem was not enabled may be omitted from the log. A signal that feeds a root outport of a referenced model and has a non-Auto storage class, the signal values are not logged. A mux or bus signals using the ModelDataLogs  signal logging format, Simulink creates a Simulink.TsArray  object. If you use MAT-file version 7.3 to save Simulink.TsArray  data in a MATLAB object, then when you load the saved object from the file, the data in the MATLAB object is empty.
Workaround: In R2010b and prior releases, you can avoid the problem described in items 1-5 by inserting a Signal Conversion block and logging the output of the Signal Conversion block. For bus signals, in the Signal Conversion block set the Output parameter to Bus copy. Otherwise, set the parameter to Contiguous copy.You can avoid the file saving problem described in item 6 by using one of these two approaches.Save the MATLAB object in MAT-file version 7 instead of 7.3. You can change the MAT-file save format with this command:        save -v7Before you save the Simulink.TsArray data, use the Simulink.SimulationData.createStructOfTimeseries method to convert the signal format to a structure of MATLAB timeseries objects.In R2011a and later releases, use Dataset format for logging.

Summary: dbstop if caught error can cause infinite loop error for certain Workspace browser configurations
Description: If the Workspace browser displays?certain statistical columns, such as Min  or Max , running MATLAB with the dbstop if caught error  option might cause an infinite loop error.?This also applies when you select Debug > Stop if Errors/Warnings > Errors  and then select the Always stop if error  option.MATLAB becomes unreliable and must be restarted.
Workaround: There are several workarounds you can use:Close the Workspace browser when using?dbstop if caught errorClose the Workspace browser's statistical columns (Min, Max, Mean, and others) when using dbstop if caught errorDo not use dbstop if caught error

Summary: The sound, soundsc, and audioplayer functions might play back at incorrect sample rates on Linux and Macintosh systems
Description: Due to a limitation in the Java Linux and Java Macintosh implementations, the following MATLAB functions have an upper limit of 65536 for their sample rate parameter: soundsoundscaudioplayer This might cause audio to play back at unexpected rates on Linux and Macintosh platforms.? Note the comments in the following example: % Load the sample sound data (y) and sample rate (Fs).  load handel;% Play back the sound data (y) at a sample rate of 8192 (Fs).  soundsc(y, Fs)% Attempt to play back the sound data at a sample rate 8.5 times faster than normal (Fs * 8.5).  soundsc(y, Fs * 8.5) The last line in the example plays back audio at approximately 0.5 times normal speed instead of correctly playing back 8.5 times faster than normal.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, do not use sample rates higher than 65536 when using the sound, soundsc, or audioplayer function on Linux or Macintosh systems.

Summary: Macintosh: MATLAB menus not located with other screen menus
Description: On the Macintosh, MATLAB menus are not located at the top of the screen. Instead, MATLAB menus appear within the MATLAB desktop and MATLAB tools: For MATLAB versions 7.0 to 7.2, which run with Mac OS versions prior to 10.4 and Java versions prior to 1.5, locating the MATLAB menus at the top of the screen causes crashes and memory problems. For MATLAB version 7.3, which runs with Mac OS 10.4 and Java 1.5, the menu at the top of the screen flickers noticeably. Therefore, the MATLAB menus are not located with other screen menus.

Summary: Tabs in the FDATool's Set quantization parameters panel disappear when you maximize the FDATool GUI
Description: The Coefficients , Input/Output , and Filter Internals  tabs disappear from the FDATool's Set quantization parameters  panel when you perform the following steps: Open FDATool by typing fdatool  at the MATLAB command line Maximize the FDATool GUI window Click the Set quantization parameters  button Select Fixed-point  for the Filter arithmeti c parameter  After you maximize the FDATool GUI and navigate to the Set quantization parameters  panel in this way, you will not be able to access the tabs on the Set quantization parameters  panel. You must close the FDATool and follow the workaround described below.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you must navigate to the Set quantization parameters panel before you maximize the FDATool GUI. After you navigate to the Set quantization parameters panel, you can maximize the GUI and access the Coefficients, Input/Output, and Filter Internals tabs.
